the investments shall be credited to such fund .
If there shall be an undisbursed balance in said fund after
payment of all approved claims received by the Administrator
within the time limits established in Section 7 of this Act, the
same shall be transferred to the General Fund of the Common-
wealth .
Section 26. Administrator authorized to make regulations .
This Act shall be given a liberal construction in the inter-
pretation thereof . The Administrator shall have power to make
rules and regulations for implementing this Act ; and when
promulgated according to KRS Chapter 12 , the same shall have
the force and effect of law.
Section 27. Appropriations . There is hereby established a
veterans bonus administration fund in the Department of Finance ,
and to this fund there is hereby appropriated from the General
Expenditure Fund one million dollars ($ 1,000,000.00 ) for fiscal
year 1959-60 . The Commissioner of Finance shall have the
responsibility for allotting the funds in this account to agencies
responsible for the execution of this Act as the need for such
funds is evidenced to him . Any unencumbered balance at the
end of the fiscal year 1959-60 shall be carried forward into the
fiscal year 1960-61 , and any unencumbered balance at the end of
the fiscal year 1960-61 shall be carried forward into the fiscal
year 1961-62 . Any unencumbered balance existing when the
Board of Review is dissolved by the Governor shall revert to the
General Expenditure Fund .
Section 28. Emergency . It is found and declared that the
public interest requires prompt payment of the veterans bonus ;
and to that end it is essential that the Administrator forthwith
commence preparations for receiving and processing claims so
that personnel may be trained , premises made ready , forms pre-
pared and regulations promulgated . Accordingly an emergency
is declared to exist and this Act shall be effective upon approval
by the Governor .
Approved February 20 , 1960

CHAPTER 17 89
CHAPTER 16
(S. B. 15 )
AN ACT relating to the return of fugitives .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 440.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
When the Governor of this state makes requisition upon the
governor of another state for a fugitive from justice , the person
named in the requisition as the agent of this state shall , unless a
different condition is contained in his commission, be allowed as
compensation from the State Treasury for his services ten cents
per mile for the distance he travels to and from the jail of the
county designated in the proclamation to the place where the
fugitive is arrested, and any other necessary expenses he pays in
reclaiming and returning the fugitive. If the agent is not given
possession of the fugitive for reasons beyond the agent's control ,
he shall nevertheless be allowed the compensation prescribed
by this section . Upon the return of the fugitive to the county in
which the jail named in the proclamation is located , the person
having him in charge shall go before a magistrate , who shall
issue a mittimus to the jailer to receive the prisoner and keep him
until he is discharged .
Approved February 25 , 1960
CHAPTER 17
(S. B. 50)
AN ACT relating to county law libraries ; permitting an alternate method
of financing a county law library in a county containing a city of the
second class as well as counties containing a city of the third or
fourth class and relating to Chapter 172 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 172.180 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Any county containing a city of the second, third or fourth
class may adopt the following method of financing the cost of

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
operation and maintenance of the county law library, in lieu of
the method set out in KRS 172.130 or KRS 172.170 :
(1) Upon petition of three -fourths of the duly licensed and
practicing attorneys resident in the county addressed to the circuit
judge of the county , to the effect that they , as officers of the
various courts of the county , recognize the need of a more ade-
quate county law library , there being attached to said petition an
attested copy of a resolution of the fiscal court of the county
endorsing the adoption of this optional method of financing the
cost of operation and maintenance of the county law library , the
circuit judge shall enter an order upon the official order book of
the circuit court of the county noting that said optional plan for
the financing of the cost of operation and maintenance of the
county law library has been adopted .
(2) The order shall set forth the names of each duly licensed
and practicing attorney signing said petition , and the county
court order book and page number containing the resolution of
the fiscal court .
(3) The order shall direct the following :
(a) That the clerk of the circuit court shall forthwith cause
to be delivered to the clerk of every court in the county , includ-
ing quarterly , county , justice's , and city or police courts , a certi-
fied copy of the order ;
(b) That upon receipt of the order by the clerks of said
courts there shall be taxed as costs in all actions, except examin-
ing trials and felony trials , thereafter instituted in said courts the
following fee , which shall be designated as county law library
fee : in quarterly , county , justice's , and city or police courts , a
sum not to exceed fifty cents ; in circuit courts , on all civil actions
a sum not to exceed one dollar ; and on all misdemeanor trials , a
sum not to exceed fifty cents ; and
(c) That the clerks of the various courts shall at the end
of each month , pay all sums collected as county law library fee
in their respective courts , during the preceding month , to the
trustees of the county law library , and the clerks shall make a
full report with said payment , and receive a receipt for
allpay
.
ments
.
Approved February 25
, 1960

CHAPTER 18 91
CHAPTER 18
(S. B. 81 )
AN ACT relating to voting machines .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 125.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) The voter shall be instructed by the officers of elec-
tion , with the aid of the instruction cards and the mechanical
model, in the use of the machine , if the voter so requests .
(2 ) No voter shall be permitted to receive any assistance
in voting unless he makes and signs an oath that , by reason of
inability to read English, or by reason of blindness or other
physical disability he is unable to vote without assistance . Upon
making and filing with the judges such oath , the voter shall
retire to the voting machine , with the judges , and one of the
judges shall then , in the presence of the other and the voter ,
operate the machine as the voter directs . In case the person
applying to vote is blind, he may , if he prefers , be assisted by
a person of his own choice . The clerk shall swear the person
accompanying the blind voter to operate the voting machine in
accordance with the directions of the blind voter , and the blind
voter and the person sworn shall then enter the voting booth and
the one so sworn shall operate the machine for the blind voter
as he directs . No voter shall be assisted under this subsection
unless the judges and the sheriff of election are satisfied of the
truth of the facts stated in the oath . The voter shall state in
his oath the specific physical disability that requires him to
receive assistance , and the judges of election shall enter in writ-
ing, on a record of assisted voters , the voter's name and the
specific physical disability which required him to receive as-
sistance .
( 3 ) No voter shall be permitted to occupy the voting
machine more than two minutes if other voters are waiting to
use it.
(4) In primary elections , before a voter is permitted to
use the voting machine , a judge of the election shall adjust the
machine so that the voter will only be able to vote for the per

92 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sons who are seeking nomination as candidates of the party with
which the voter is affiliated .
(5) If the machine is so constructed as to require adjust-
ment after one person has voted before another person may vote ,
the judges of election shall so adjust it after each person has
voted.
(6 ) The election officers shall constantly maintain a watch
in order to prevent any person from voting more than once .
Approved February 25 , 1960

CHAPTER 20
(Н. В. 173 )
AN ACT relating to temporary courthouse facilities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 67.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The county judge may rent , for the period that a court-
house is being built or repaired , or cannot be used, a house not
more than a mile and one quarter from the public square or
courthouse site , as a temporary courthouse . He may also rent ,
for the period that the clerk's office or jail is being built or

98 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
repaired , a house within the same distance of the public square
or courthouse site, as a temporary clerk's office or jail . The
rent for such temporary courthouse , clerk's office or jail shall
be paid out of the county treasury .
Approved February 25 , 1960
CHAPTER 21
(Н. В. 122 )
AN ACT relating to expenses of members of the General Assembly .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 6.211 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
In addition to any and all sums , compensation , or expenses
now paid the members of the General Assembly and the
President of the Senate , they , and each of them shall receive
an expense allowance of twenty -five dollars for each legislative
day . This sum shall be paid whether the General Assembly is in
regular or extraordinary session and shall be in full recompense
of any additional expenses incurred in excess of that now received
by them .
Section 2. Whereas , present inflationary trends have
raised the actual expenses of the members of the General
Assembly and the President of the Senate , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
, and shall
beretro-
active
toinclude any and
alldays
ofthe 1960 Regular Session
ofthe General Assembly which may have elapsed up
tothe

ofthis Act

CHAPTER 22
(Н. В. 38)
AN ACT relating to the establishment of state tuberculosis hospital
districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 215.061 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The commission shall divide the state into tuberculosis
hospital districts . The commission shall have authority , with the
approval of the Governor , to transfer counties from one district
to another when necessary in order to utilize the hospital facilities
efficiently .
Approved February 26 , 1960
CHAPTER 23
(Н. В. 39)
AN ACT relating to the training and educating of personnel employed
by the State Tuberculosis Hospital Commission .

100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created
to read as follows :
The State Tuberculosis Hospital Commission is authorized
to establish training schools at or within any of the hospitals
operated by the commission , or at any hospital or hospitals co-
operating in a joint program with the commission , for the training
of necessary personnel for the hospitals , or may arrange for the
training of employes or prospective employes in any public or
private school or institution having available facilities for that
purpose . Funds of the commission may be used to pay salaries to
employees , or to pay tuition and subsistence for employes or pros-
pective employes , while receiving such training . Any employe or
prospective employe who is paid a salary , or for whom tuition and
subsistence are furnished , while receiving such training , shall be
required to enter into a contract , prior to receiving such training ,
that unless he accepts and continues in any employment offered
by the commission for at least a period equivalent to the training
period , immediately following the completion of such training ,
he will reimburse to the commission the sum paid to or for him
by the commission during or for the period of training .
Approved February 26 , 1960
CHAPTER 24
(Н. В. 54)
AN ACT relating to the treatment of patients having histoplasmosis or
other fungus diseases in the state tuberculosis hospitals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is created
to read as follows :
The State Tuberculosis Hospital Commission is authorized
to accept and treat patients having histoplasmosis or other pul-
monary fungus diseases in the state tuberculosis hospitals insofar
as permitted by available facilities not fully utilized in the treat-
ment of tuberculosis patients .
Approved February 26 , 1960

CHAPTER 25
(S. B. 32)
AN ACT relating to the Board of Claims , and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , the present statutes requiring the appointment of separate
and independent officers and employes of the Board of Claims have
created a situation which is wasteful and burdensome to the Com-
monwealth ; and
WHEREAS , the same functions can be performed by the members ,
secretaries , and employes of the Workmen's Compensation Board ,
which is established to handle cases procedurally similar to those
processed by the Board of Claims ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

is repealed and re-enacted as follows :
(1) A Board of Claims , composed of the members of the
Workmen's Compensation Board as hereinafter provided , is cre-
ated and vested with full power and authority to investigate , hear
proof , and to compensate persons for damages sustained to either
person or property as a proximate result of negligence on the part

the chairmanship shall be filled by the Governor . No member
shall , at the same time , serve as chairman of the Workmen's
Compensation Board and as chairman of the Board of Claims .
(4) The executive secretary , referees and employes of the
Workmen's Compensation Board , without additional compensa-
tion , shall be ex -officio the executive secretary, referees and em-
ployes respectively of the Board of Claims .
(5) Regardless of any provision of law to the contrary , the
jurisdiction of the board is exclusive , and a single claim for the
recovery of money or a single award of money shall not exceed
ten thousand dollars , exclusive of interest and costs .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Hearings involving claimants who are residents of the Com-
monwealth shall be conducted in the county wherein the claim
accrues , provided , however , that the parties may , with the ap-

for the conduct of hearings . Hearings involving claimants who
are non - residents shall be conducted in the county wherein the
claim accrues , provided , however , that a hearing , with the ap-
proval of the board , may be conducted in Franklin County .

CHAPTER 28
(S. B. 54)
AN ACT relating to parking meters in cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 94.740 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Cities of second class may provide and maintain parking
meters .
(1) The governing body of any city of the second class
may enact ordinances providing for the acquisition , establish-
ment , erection , maintenance and operation of a system of park-
ing meters or other similar mechanical devices to regulate and
control vehicular traffic in the city, said meters or devices re-
quiring a deposit of money , the amount thereof to be deter-
mined by the legislative body of the city , from those who park
vehicles for stipulated periods of time in certain areas in the
city in which the congestion of vehicular traffic is such that
public convenience and safety require such regulation .
(2) The proceeds derived from the use of such parking
meters or other similar mechanical devices shall be used : First ,
for the purpose of the acquisition , establishment, erection , main-
tenance and operation of the system ; second, for the purpose of
making the system effective ; and third , for the expenses incurred
by and throughout the city in the regulation and limitation of
vehicular parking , traffic relating to parking , traffic safety ,
devices , signs , signals , markings , policing , lights , traffic surveys
and safety programs .
(3) Nothing contained in this section shall prohibit the
governing body of any city of the second class from employing

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Any utility providing electric service in any area annexed ,
subsequent to the effective date of this Act , by any municipality
shall have the dominant right to continue to provide electric
service in said area to consumers then being served and to new
consumers located nearer to its facilities than to the facilities of
any other utility as all those facilities were located immediately
prior to annexation .
Approved March 2, 1960

The respective terms of the first members appointed shall be

Two members of the commission shall be appointed from a list
of six names submitted by the Kentucky Society for Crippled
Children , one member from a list of three names submitted by the
Kentucky State Medical Association , and the other four members
shall be intelligent and discreet citizens appointed from the state

manner as original appointments , maintaining original representa-
tions .
Section 3. ( 1 ) Members of the commission shall be quali-
fied voters of the Commonwealth , shall be at least thirty years of
age and have been residents of the Commonwealth for at least
two years immediately preceding appointment .
( 2 ) Commissioners shall take the constitutional oath of
office before entering upon the discharge of their duties , and a
record of such taking shall be entered upon the executive journal .
(3 ) Members of the commission shall serve without com-
pensation but shall be entitled to reimbursement for actual and
necessary expenses incurred in the performance of their duties .
Section 4. ( 1 ) The commission shall be a corporation
with the usual corporate powers , and may adopt rules and regula-
tions necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act .
( 2 ) The commission shall elect a president , vice - president ,
secretary and treasurer . The office of secretary and treasurer may
be held by the same person .
( 3 ) The commission shall employ a medical director and
such professional and other personnel as may be required to
administer the program of the commission , in accordance with
state and federal laws and regulations and under such administra-
tive policies as are established by the commission . The medical
director and

CHAPTER 34
(S. B. 10
)
AN ACT relating
tothe conduct of regular elections
.

112 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 118.400 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The county board of election commissioners shall issue
certificates of election where the successful candidate was voted
for by a county or by a district less than one county . The board
shall forward the certificate to the elected candidate , unless he
has failed to comply with KRS 123.060 , 123.070 or 123.080 . If
the board finds that two or more candidates have received the
highest and equal number of votes for the same office , the board
shall determine by lot which of the candidates is elected .
(2) In the case of offices voted for by the state at large
or by a district greater than one county , and in the case of public
questions submitted to the vote of the people of the state at large
or of a district greater than one county , the county board of
election commissioners shall make out duplicate certificates of
the total number of votes received by each of the candidates
for such offices , and the total number of votes for and against
each of such questions . The county clerk shall keep one of the
certificates in his office . He shall not later than three days after
receiving such certificates from the board , forward the other
certificate by mail to the Secretary of State , who shall deliver it
to the State Board of Election Commissioners .
Section 2. Any county clerk who fails to comply with sub-
section (2) of KRS 118.400 shall be fined not less than twenty-
five dollars nor more than one hundred dollars . Each day such
violation occurs shall constitute a separate offense .

(4) All licenses shall expire on April 1 of each year . Han-

for each place . Each application shall bear the names of the
persons responsible for candling and grading eggs .
Section 2. Section 260.610 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) All eggs bought or sold by or to retailers , consumers ,
and institutional users by licensees shall be identified according
to grade and size , using USDA standards and weight classes for
consumer grades .
(2) Eggs to be offered for sale for human consumption
shall be handled in such manner as to maintain and preserve
the quality and grade in which they are to be offered for sale .
(3) No person shall sell, offer or expose for sale , or have
in his possession for sale, for human consumption , eggs that are
inedible , including eggs that are unfit for human food in whole
or in part , addled or moldy , containing black rot , white rot, or
blood ring , adherent yolks , or bloody whites , incubated beyond
the blood ring stage , or consisting to any extent of filthy de-
composed substance .
Section 3. Section 260.620 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Eggs offered for sale as graded eggs shall conform to the
standards prescribed in administrative regulations adopted by

standards for grading eggs for sale .
Section 4. Section 260.630 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) All wholesale egg packs consisting of cases or portions
of cases must bear a label designating contents , quality , date of
packing , candler's name or number , dealer's name and address ,
size and grade of eggs .
(2) Graded eggs offered for sale in cartons or other packs
containing one dozen or less shall be plainly marked as to grade ,
quality , and size with letters not less than one- fourth inch in
height .
CHAPTER 37

Approved March 5, 1960
AN ACT relating to the licensing , recording of liens and registration of
motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 186.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Whenever a motor vehicle that has been previously
registered changes ownership , the registration plate shall remain
upon such motor vehicle as a part of it until the expiration of the
registration year .
(2 ) No person shall sell or trade any motor vehicle without
delivering to the county clerk of the county in which the sale or
trade is made the registration receipt issued on the motor vehicle
for the current year and the bill of sale .
(3 ) Upon delivery of the registration receipt and the bill
of sale to the county clerk of the county in which the sale or trade
was made , the seller shall pay to the county clerk a transfer fee
of one dollar , which shall be remitted to the department . The
county clerk shall thereupon issue to the purchaser a transfer
of registration bearing the same data and information as con-
tained on the original registration receipt , except the change in
name and address . The seller shall pay to the county clerk a fee
ofone dollar for his services .

116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(4) Whenever a county clerk issues to a purchaser a trans-
fer of registration in a county other than the one in which the
motor vehicle was originally registered, the clerk shall immedi-
ately mail one copy of the transfer of registration to the clerk of
the county of original registration .
(5) If the owner junks or otherwise renders a motor vehi-
cle unfit for future use , he shall deliver the registration plate and
registration receipt to the county clerk of the county in which the
motor vehicle is junked . The county clerk shall immediately
return the plate and reciept to the department . The owner shall
pay to the county clerk fifty cents for his services .
(6) A licensed motor vehicle dealer shall not be required to
pay the transfer fee provided by this section , but shall be required
to pay the county clerk's fee provided by this section .
Section 2. Section 186.195 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Whenever the owner of a motor vehicle , properly
registered in this state , executes a lien thereon , he shall deliver
the registration receipt to the secured party. The secured party
shall , within ten days thereafter , present such receipt , together
with the lien instrument and proper fees , to the county clerk of
the county in which the motor vehicle is registered for recording
in accordance with KRS 382.670 and KRS 186.040 .
(2 ) The county clerk shall , within five days , mail the
registration receipt with the lien recorded thereon to the owner ,
and shall also record the lien on the copy of the registration
receipt on file in the county clerk's office .
(3 ) Whenever a lien instrument affecting a motor vehicle
is presented to a county clerk for recording in any county other
than the one in which the motor vehicle is registered , the county
clerk shall , in addition to the other requirements of this section
and the requirements of KRS 382.670 , mail a notice of the re-
corded lien to the clerk of the county in which the motor vehicle
is registered on a form which the Department of Revenue shall
supply . Upon receipt of said notice , the county clerk shall record
the lien on the copy of the registration receipt on file in the county
clerk'soffice .
(4) ( a) When a chattel mortgage , or other instrument
construed as such in this state , is assigned or discharged pursuant

CHAPTER 37 117
to the law , the owner shall present the registration receipt to the
secured party who shall record thereon in language prescribed
by the department that the lien has been assigned or discharged .
The recording shall show the date thereof and shall be signed by
the secured party or his authorized agent and acknowledged
before an officer authorized to administer an oath.
(b ) When a discharge or assignment has been recorded
on the owner's registration receipt as provided in ( 4 ) ( a ) , the
owner may present the registration receipt to the county clerk
who shall record the information on the copy of the receipt which
contains the lien information on file in the county clerk's office ,
for which services the owner shall pay the clerk a fee of twenty-
five cents .
(5) For failure to present the registration receipt and
record the lien instrument within the time prescribed , the secured
party shall pay a penalty of two dollars to the county clerk as a
prerequisite for recording the lien information on the registration
receipt . The penalty , less the county clerk's commission of five
percent , shall be remitted to the department .
(6) No lien executed prior to January 1, 1960 , shall be
recorded on the registration receipt issued by the county clerk.

CHAPTER 40
(Н. В. 119 )
AN ACT relating to pension fund's board of trustees in cities of the
third class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 95.530 , paragraph 2, of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(2) In cities of the third class the board of trustees of the
policemen's and firemen's pension fund shall be composed of
the mayor , city treasurer , chief of the police department , chief of
the fire department and one member each from the police and
fire departments . The members from the police and fire de-
partments shall be elected by the respective members of those
departments annually by ballot, one from each department , and
shall serve for one year and until their respective successors are
elected and qualified . The board shall select from their number
a president and a secretary .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 7, 1960
CHAPTER 41
(Н. В. 174)
AN ACT relating to officers and employes of cities of the third class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 85.330 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) All officers , agents or employes appointed or employed
by the common council may be removed by the common council
at pleasure , except as otherwise provided by law.
(2) Except where otherwise provided by law, any city of-
ficer may , for incompetency , inefficiency , neglect of duty or mis-
conduct in office , be impeached and removed from office by a
three - fourths vote of the members of the common council , the
yeas and nays being called and entered upon the journal . The
common council shall , by ordinance , prescribe the method of
procedure .

CHAPTER 43 125
Section 2. Members of police and fire departments in cities
of the third class may be removed only in accordance with the
provisions of KRS 95.450 .
Became law without Governer's signature , March 7, 1960
CHAPTER 42
(S. B. 14)
AN ACT relating to destruction of property by use of explosives .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
Kentucky :
Section 433.460 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Any person who willfully explodes or who aids, counsels or
procures the explosion of any explosive substance on or near
another's real or personal property under circumstances that show
an intent to cause destruction thereto and thereby destroys or
damages another's real or personal property shall be punished
by confinement in the penitentiary for not less than two nor more
than twenty -one years .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 7, 1960

Section 10. KRS 161.540 is amended to read as follows :
Each member shall contribute to the Retirement System
five per cent of his annual compensation . The contribution of a
member shall not exceed this percentage on his current annual
compensation .
Section 11. KRS 161.550 is amended to read as follows :
Beginning with July 1, 1960 , the state shall contribute an-
nually to the Retirement System a permanent amount equal to
that contributed by all members. The state shall contribute an-
nually an amount sufficient to discharge the prior service obliga-

than thirty years .
Section 12. KRS 161.600 is amended to read as follows :
Any member who has attained the age of sixty years and

The State Board of Education shall not approve any work-
ing budget or salary schedule for local boards of education for
the school years 1960-61 and 1961-62 in which the total amount
budgeted for teachers' salaries is not equal to the amount
budgeted for teachers' salaries in 1959-60 plus the additional
amount for teachers ' salaries and guarantee received by the
district under this appropriation . The amount which any district
receives under any guarantee provision shall be presumed to be
for teachers ' salaries and shall be placed in the teachers' salaries
budget and allotted to the various teachers ' salary ranks in a
proportionate manner in keeping with KRS 157.390 . No work-
ing budget or salary schedule shall be approved in which the
average salary paid any rank of teacher is not at least equivalent
to the allotment for that rank and in which any teacher is paid
less than ninety per cent of the foundation program allotment
for that rank .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot to
any school district more basic classroom units than the number
of teachers employed by that school district for basic classroom
units . Entitlement for basic classroom units obtained in schools
or buildings of one classification shall not be allocated to schools
or buildings of another classification , for which purposes the
classifications shall be as follows :
(1) One -teacher schools ;
(2) Schools , other than one-teacher schools , with less than
one hundred pupils in average daily attendance; and ,
(3) Schools , other than one-teacher schools, with more
than one hundred pupils in average daily attendance .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall allot class-
room units for superintendents , principals , their certified assist-
ants and special instructional personnel as provided in KRS
157.360 , subsection 6, only for personnel who are qualified for

CHAPTER 45 157
their respective positions under provisions of law and regulations
of the State Board of Education and who maintain programs
approved under regulations of the State Board of Education .
The number of units allotted by the Superintendent of Pub-
lic Instruction for vocational education and the education of
exceptional children shall not exceed the following:
1960-61 1961-62
Vocational Agriculture 260 265
Vocational Home Economics 365 370
Trade and Industrial 100 100
Distributive Occupations 25 30
Exceptional Children 215 250
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot a
full classroom unit for any non -isolated one-teacher school with
fewer than 27 pupils in average daily attendance and no full
classroom units shall be allotted for any one -teacher school which
has fewer than 12 pupils in average daily attendance .
Facilities that are used to house school children shall be
approved by the Superintendent of Public Instruction under
KRS 162.060 and 162.160 . No alterations or additions of old
buildings shall be made without prior approval of the Super-
intendent of Public Instruction . No space shall be rented or
purchased and converted to classroom use without prior ap-
proval of the Superintendent of Public Instruction . The State
Board of Education may adopt regulations for the approval on
an annual basis of temporary facilities for the housing of school
children but these regulations shall not permit the division of
any present room by partitions of nonstructural material . No
classroom units shall be allotted for a second teacher in the
same classroom . No facilities shall be approved for classroom
use which are detrimental to the health of or dangerous to the
safety of school children .
If a school district operates any school or grade of a school
for a term of less than nine scholastic months or for a school
day of less than the minimum required by KRS 158.060 , the
average daily attendance of such school shall be reduced pro-
portionately by the Superintendent of Public Instruction in de-
termining the allotment of classroom units provided for by
regulations of the State Board of Education .

III . ADMINISTRATION AND EFFECT
The Department of Highways shall submit an annual budget
for each fiscal year to the Department of Finance for approval
by the Commissioner of Finance immediately before the fiscal
year begins . The budget shall be based upon the most current
state road fund revenue estimates and contractual and non -con-
tractual federal aid in which the department will be eligible to
receive during that fiscal year .
The appropriations made by this Act are subject to the pro-
visions of Chapters 12, 42, and 45 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes except where additional requirements are imposed by this
Act. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any ap-
propriation for the fiscal year to the State Road Fund ending
June 30, 1960 .

PART FOUR
GENERAL PROVISION
1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1960 , and
nothing in this Act is to be construed to amend or alter the
provisions of Chapter 42 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the duties and powers of the Commissioner of Finance .
All questions that arise in interpreting any appropriation in this
Act as to the purpose for which the same may be expended
shall be decided by the Commissioner of Finance in accordance
with the detailed estimates embodied in the budget report , and
supporting work papers , as considered by the General Assembly ,
and the decision of the Commissioner of Finance shall be final
and conclusive .
2. Whereas , the finances of the Commonwealth can not be
ascertained at this time , and since the actual needs of each
officer , department , board , commission , institution , or subdivision
of the State Government can only be anticipated and not finally
determined by the General Assembly , and since the total amount
of revenue accruing to the General Expenditure Fund and to the
General Road Fund from which these appropriations are made

CHAPTER 45 179
can only be anticipated through existing laws and those that
may hereafter be enacted, it is the purpose and intention of this
Act that the expenditures for each fiscal year under Part One of
this Act shall not exceed the total financial resources anticipated
to accrue to the General Expenditure Fund and that the ex-
penditures for each fiscal year under Part Two of this Act shall
not exceed the total financial resources anticipated to accrue
to the General Road Fund for the same fiscal year respectively .
As the conditions can not be fully determined until after the
adjournment of the 1960 General Assembly , and as the needs
of each officer , department , board , agency, commission , institu-
tion , or subdivision of the State Government and the total
financial resources accruing to the General Expenditure Fund
and General Road Fund can not be fully determined at this
time, it is the intention and the purpose of this General Assembly
that the Commissioner of Finance , with the advice of the Com-
missioner of Revenue and the approval of the Governor of the
Commonwealth , is authorized , empowered and directed to ascer-
tain and determine the needs of each officer , department , board ,
commission , institution or subdivision of the State Government ,
and to ascertain the total financial resources anticipated to
accrue to the General Expenditure Fund and General Road
Fund of the Commonwealth , and upon the ascertainment and
determination of said facts , he , the Commissioner of Finance ,
in the manner set forth and as provided by general law, is
authorized , empowered and directed by the General Assembly
to prevent an overdraft or deficit in any fiscal year for which
appropriations are herein made , by equitably reducing , accord-
ing to need, without discrimination , the appropriations herein
made in Part One and Part Two to any officer , department , board ,
commission , institution , or subdivision of the State Government ;
provided that the power vested shall not permit any reduction
of the appropriation of any officer , department , board , commis-
sion , institution , or subdivision of the State Government that
will actually impair the necessary constitutional functions of any
agency whose operations and functions are determined to be
necessary constitutional functions of government .
3. Receipts in excess of $ 142,000,000 in the fiscal year
1959-60 together with any additions resulting from liquidation of

180 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
encumbrances on June 30, 1960 are appropriated to the Depart-
ment of Finance to be used solely for capital construction in
accordance with the provisions of Chapter 56 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes . Receipts in excess of $ 212,000,000 in the
fiscal year 1960-61 , not to exceed $ 500,000 , are hereby appro-
priated to the Governor's General Emergency Fund to be used
by him for the fiscal year 1961-62 for supplementing existing
appropriations for ordinary recurring and extraordinary govern-
mental expenses deemed emergencies by the Governor . Receipts
in excess of $ 212,500,000 in the fiscal year 1960-61 are hereby
appropriated to the Department of Finance to be used solely for
capital construction in accordance with the provisions of Chapter
56 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . Any amount in excess of
$6,000,000 of the lapse and surplus at the end of the 1960-61 fiscal
year is hereby appropriated to the Department of Finance to be
used for capital construction in accordance with the provisions of
Chapter 56 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
4. It is the intent of the General Assembly that funds
appropriated for expanded or new programs shall not be used
for continuation programs . Therefore , the Commissioner of
Finance shall take the proper action to insure that only those
funds necessary to initiate and administer the new and expansion
programs through the remainder of the biennium are allotted to
the various budget units .
5. In enacting this Appropriation Act, it is the deliberate
intention of the General Assembly to enact each section , and
each subsection thereof , as a separate and/or specific appro-
priation and law, and if any section or any subsection thereof
shall be held invalid , or unconstitutional , the decision of the
courts shall not affect or impair any of the remaining sections ,
subsections , or provisions contained herein .
6. All continuing appropriations in existence at the time
this Act takes effect are discontinued and repealed by this Act ,
except as provided by Chapters 12 , 42, 45, 56, 157, 177, and 341
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes. All laws and parts of laws in
conflict with any of the provisions of the above enactment , to the
extent of such conflict, are repealed .
Approved March 10 , 1960

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 48
(Н. В. 47)
AN ACT relating to conveyance of Federal prisoners to courts of this
state for trial of felony prosecutions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. The Commonwealth's attorney may enter into
an agreement with representatives of the Federal government
for conveyance of a Federal prisoner to a circuit court of this
state to stand trial therein on a felony charge , upon condition
that such prisoner shall remain in the custody of an agent of the
United States and be returned to the Federal penitentiary for
completion of the term of confinement there following conclusion
of the trial in the circuit court , and thereafter to be returned to
this state to serve any sentence imposed here . It may also be
agreed that the state will bear the expense of such conveyance .
The Commonwealth's attorney shall then file in the circuit court
his verified petition for writ of habeas corpus ad prosequendum
stating facts as to the situation of the prisoner , the charge
against him and date set for trial, the necessity of securing the
prisoner for trial prior to his release in due course by Federal
authorities and the terms of the agreement which has been
reached with such authorities . If the court deems the petition
sufficient , it shall issue the writ and if the state is required to
pay the expense the writ shall designate the officer to transport
and guard the prisoner and such officer shall be reimbursed
from the State Treasury for expenses incurred in the amount
and manner provided in KRS 64.070 ( 1) . The circuit court shall
enter an order of allowance of expenses for the officer upon
conclusion of the trial .
Section 2. When a material witness for the Commonwealth
in a felony prosecution pending in a court of this state is con

184 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fined under judgment of conviction in any Federal penal institu-
tion , the Commonwealth's attorney may enter into an agreement
with the appropriate Federal authorities for conveyance of the
witness at the expense of this state to the circuit court in which
the trial is pending , and after trial , for return to the Federal
penitentiary . The Commonwealth's attorney shall move the
circuit court for an order for the attendance of the witness and
shall state the situation of the prisoner , the date set for trial of
the case , the importance of his testimony to the Commonwealth ,
and the agreement of the Federal authorities to produce the
prisoner in court . The circuit court shall designate the officer
to transport and guard the prisoner and such person shall be
reimbursed from the State Treasury for his expenses in the
amount and manner provided in KRS 64.070 (1 ). The circuit
court shall enter an order of allowance of expenses for the
officer upon conclusion of the trial .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 11 , 1960
CHAPTER 49
(Н. В. 90 )
AN ACT relating to injury to public property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 433.750 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Any person who picks , pulls , digs , tears up, cuts , breaks ,
burns or otherwise damages any tree, shrub , flower , vine , bush
or turf on the right of way of any state highway or county road
or upon any land set aside , dedicated or maintained by this state
as a public park or as a refuge or sanctuary for wild animals ,
birds or fish , or deposits , throws or leaves any trash, debris or
litter upon any such public highway or public land , without
having first obtained permission in writing of the engineer having
charge of the maintenance of the highway , or the superintendent
or custodian of the park , refuge or sanctuary shall be fined not
less than ten dollars or more than one hundred dollars for each
offense .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 11 , 1960

CHAPTER 51
(S. B. 46)
AN ACT relating to deaths and the disposal of dead bodies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. No person shall cremate or cause to be cre-
mated , or transport or cause to be transported for the purpose
of cremation , the body of any person whose death occurs in
this state , without first obtaining from the coroner of the county
in which the death occurred , a death certificate stating the
cause of death and authorizing the cremation , or transportation
for cremation , of the body. Such certificate shall be filed im-
mediately with the local registrar of vital statistics . No burial
transit permit for the cremation or transportation for the purpose
of cremation of the body of any such person shall be issued pur-
suant to KRS 213.110 by any registrar of vital statistics , except
upon such authorization by the coroner as provided herein .

Section 1. KRS 316.010 is amended to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires other-
( 1 ) "Association " means the Funeral Directors Association
of Kentucky ;
( 2 ) "Board " means the State Board of Embalmers and
Funeral Directors ;
(3) "Branch establishment " means an establishment
owned by a licensed funeral director in a place apart from his
main establishment .
Section 2. KRS 316.020 is amended to read as follows :
(1) No person not registered in this State as an embalmer
or as a funeral director on or prior to May 15, 1960 , shall engage
in, or attempt to engage in, embalming or funeral directing until
he has been licensed to do so under the provisions of this chapter .
(2) One member of every firm, and one officer and one
stockholder of every corporation , that engages in embalming and
funeral directing in Kentucky , shall be a licensed embalmer and
licensed funeral director , having Kentucky license in full force
and effect . Each establishment devoted to the care, preparation or
disposal of dead human bodies must be under the direct and con-
stant supervision of a licensed embalmer and licensed funeral
director , having Kentucky licenses in full force and effect .
Section 3. KRS 316.030 is amended to read as follows :
An applicant for embalmer's license must be 21 years of age
and a graduate of an accredited high school , as evidenced by a
certificate or diploma from such accredited high school . He

art of embalming , disinfection and sanitation in an accredited
school of embalming recognized by the board . He shall have
served two additional years of apprenticeship under a licensed
embalmer and licensed funeral director holding a Kentucky
license . The licensed embalmer and funeral director must have
an established funeral home in Kentucky .

Section 14. KRS 316.140 is amended to read as follows :
Any person holding a valid embalmer's or funeral director's
license currently in force which has been issued by an authority
of another state or federal district having a law recognized by
the board as comparable to the Kentucky law governing em-
balmers and funeral directors , and who wishes to engage in the
practice of embalming or funeral directing in this State , may make
a written application to the secretary of the board , accompanied
by a $ 50.00 application fee and by a certificate from the authority
which issued his license showing that he is the holder of a
currently valid license obtained by examination while a resident
of the state or federal district in which he is licensed . If the
board finds that the applicant has met the qualifications and
complied with the requirements of this chapter , it may grant him
the privilege of taking the desired examination for an embalmer's
or a funeral director's license .
Section 15. KRS 316.150 is hereby amended to read as
follows :
(1) The board may , upon sufficient proof submitted to
them by sworn statements or testimony , refuse to issue , refuse
to renew , or may revoke or suspend, the license of any licensed
Kentucky embalmer or funeral director for any of the following
causes :
( a ) Conviction for violation of the requirements of this
chapter ; conviction of a felony, or any misdeameanor
resulting in imprisonment or a fine of more than five
hundred dollars ;
( b ) The payment or offering to pay , directly or indirectly
of any consideration of value to secure a funeral , prior
to death or after death of any individual , or allowing
any person in his employ or a member of his immediate
family to pay anything of value to secure a funeral ;
( c) The solicitation of business in any way by offering
or giving any service which is not a normal function
of a licensed funeral director or embalmer in a regular
service ;
(d) Permitting any person who is not a licensed embalmer
or funeral director , holding Kentucky license in full
force and effect , to perform any service pertaining to

192 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
embalming or funeral directing required by law of a
licensed embalmer or funeral director , from the time of
death until interment .
( e ) For assisting any applicant for funeral director's or
embalmer's license to obtain the license by misrepre-
sentation or fraud either in making application for
apprenticeship , registration for apprenticeship , or dur-
ing the period of serving apprenticeship , or in taking
or passing the examination for embalmer's or funeral
director's license ;
( f) For the operation of a branch establishment without a
licensed embalmer and funeral director in complete
charge who devotes his or their sole time to the man-
agement and affairs of the branch establishment and
who is subject to call at all times in the section where
the branch is located . The branch manager must be
a licensed embalmer and a licensed funeral director
with his license in full force and effect in Kentucky .
( 2 ) For the purposes of taking testimony under this sec-
tion , and for the purposes of any other section of this chapter ,
each member of the board , and the secretary thereof , is hereby
endowed with the authority to administer oaths in the same
manner as a clerk of a court of record . The board , or any
member thereof , may subpoena licensees or other witnesses to
appear before it .

any decision of the board revoking , suspending , or refusing to
issue or renew, any license . Upon appeal the court shall be
limited to a determination as to whether the board had acted
in excess of
itspowers

CHAPTER 53
(Н. В. 71)
AN ACT relating to commercial transactions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
(1) When a secured transaction evidenced by record in
the county clerk's office has terminated , and there is no out-
standing secured obligation and no commitment to make ad-
vances , incur obligations or otherwise give value , the secured
party shall send to the clerk a statement that he no longer claims
a security interest under the financing statement or other instru-
ment , which shall be identified by file number and date filed.
A termination statement signed by a person other than the se-
cured party of record shall include or be accompanied by the
assignment or a statement by the secured party of record that
he has assigned the security interest to the signer of the termina-
tion statement . The uniform fee for filing and indexing such an
assignment or statement thereof shall be $ .75 . If the actual
secured party willfully fails to send such a termination statement
within thirty days after he no longer claims a security interest , he
shall be liable to the debtor for twenty -five dollars , and in ad-
dition for any loss caused to the debtor by such failure .
(2) On presentation to the clerk of such a termination
statement he shall note it in the index . He shall remove the

196 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
terminated instrument from the files , mark it " terminated " and
send or deliver it to the secured party .
(3) The uniform fee for filing and indexing a termination
statement and for sending or delivering the terminated instru-
ment shall be $ .75.
Became law without Governor's signature , March 16, 1950

failure to pay an employe the wages when due him under KRS
337.020 shall constitute a separate offense .

more than five hundred dollars for each violation .

not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than two hundred

(4) Any person who violates KRS 337.060 shall be fined
not more than three hundred dollars or imprisoned for not more
than sixty days , or both .

keep and present records as provided in KRS 337.320 or who
falsifies such records , or who fails to keep posted a copy of the
minimum fair-wage orders as required in KRS 337.330 , or who
in any way hinders the commissioner or his authorized repre-
sentative in the enforcement of KRS 337.210 to 337.360 shall be
fined not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than one hun-
dred dollars . Each day of violation of any of these provisions
shall constitute a separate offense .
(6) Any person who violates subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
337.350 shall be fined not less than fifty dollars nor more than
two hundred dollars .
(7) Any person who violates subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
337.350 shall be fined not less than fifty dollars nor more than

202 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 84.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) Members of the general council shall be elected for
two years and until their successors have qualified .
(2 ) Each member of the general council shall reside in
the city for two years next preceding his election , and shall
at the time of his election , be a citizen of Kentucky and a
housekeeper or owner of real estate in the city . At the time
of his election , each councilman shall be at least twenty - four
years of age and shall have been, for one year , a bona fide
resident and voter of the ward for which he is chosen , and
each alderman shall be at least thirty years of age .
(3) No person shall be eligible to be a member of the
general council if, at the time of his election , he is interested
in any contract with the city or in any application therefor ,
or is in arrears to the city for money or property collected or
held, or has been convicted of malfeasance in office , bribery
or other corrupt practice of crime , or holds any office or em-

CHAPTER 58
(Н. В. 50 )
AN ACT relating to reading matter for the blind .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
For the benefit of blind readers of Kentucky , the Library
Extension Division may make available books and other reading
matter in Braille , talking books or any other medium of reading
used by the blind . To this end, the division is authorized to pro-
vide library services for the blind of the Commonwealth through
contract , agreement or otherwise with the Library of Congress
or any regional library thereof .

AN ACT relating to voter registration and purgation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 117.810 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) The county boards of registration and purgation may
examine the registration lists and registration books in the
county clerk's office as often as they deem advisable , or the
boards may proceed upon the challenge by any citizen or politi-
cal executive committee having representation upon the State
Board of Election Commissioners , and shall examine the registra-
tion books after the closing date of the books for registration
and before any primary or regular election , for the purpose of
removing from the lists or books the names of persons who have ,
since the date of their registration , died , moved or otherwise
become disqualified to vote . If a county board of registration
and purgation , upon investigation , has reasonable grounds to
believe that there are names upon the registration lists or books
of persons who have died , moved or otherwise become disquali-
fied to participate in any regular , special or primary election , the
board shall prepare a list of such names . When the board chal-
lenges, or upon the challenge of any citizen or political executive

204 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
committee having representation upon the State Board of Elec-
tion Commissioners , the registration of any voter , it shall im-
mediately notify the voter in writing, by first-class mail , at his
place of residence shown upon the registration list or book that
his right to vote in the primary or regular election has been
challenged and that his name will be stricken from the registra-
tion lists or books unless he appears before the board within five
days from the date of the notice and shows cause why his name
should not be stricken . If the voter appears and desires to be
heard upon the challenge , the board shall hear and determine
whether or not the name of the voter should remain upon the
registration lists or books , and no name shall be stricken from
the registration lists or books until the voter has been given an
opportunity to be heard . If the board determines that the voter
is legally registered , his name shall remain upon the registration
lists or books . If after a hearing the board determines that the
name of the voter is illegally or improperly upon the lists or
books , it shall enter an order directing the county clerk to strike
the name of the voter therefrom . If, after the notice , as herein
provided , the voter fails to appear before the board and contest
the challenge , it shall be prima facie evidence that his name
illegally or improperly appears upon the registration lists or
books , and the board , without further hearing , shall strike his

CHAPTER 62
(Н. В. 197 )
AN ACT relating to the control of pests and noxious weeds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act, unless the context otherwise
requires :
( 1 ) “Department " means the Department of Agriculture ,
Labor and Statistics ;
( 2 ) “Commissioner " means the Commissioner of Agricul-
ture , Labor and Statistics ; and
( 3 ) "Division " means the Division of Pest and Noxious
Weed Control .
Section 2. Section 246.030 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The department shall consist of :
( 1 ) The Commissioner ;
( 2 ) The Board of Agriculture ;
( 3 ) The State Fair Board ;
(4 ) The Division of Markets ;
( 5 ) The Division of Weights and Measures ;
( 6 ) The Board of Veterinary Examination ; and
(7) The Division of Pest and Noxious Weed Control .
Section 3. The Division of Pest and Noxious Weed Control
in the Department of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics shall be
under the supervision of the commissioner and shall consist of
personnel determined and appointed by him .
Section 4. The division shall promote and sponsor pro-
grams to control pests and noxious weeds , enforce related regula-
tory and service measures assigned to the department , and con-
duct a Johnson grass control and eradication program.

CHAPTER 63 209
Section 5. In order to perform the duties listed in Section
3 of this Act , the division may :
(1) Utilize the services of a State advisory committee on
Johnson grass control to be composed of persons interested in
Johnson grass control appointed by the commissioner ;
( 2 ) Issue and enforce regulations for the eradication and
control of noxious weeds , including Johnson grass , and pests ;
( 3 ) Cooperate with county agricultural agents , vocational
agricultural teachers , University of Kentucky extension spe-
cialists , civic groups , or any federal , state or county agency in
promoting county programs and organizations to control and
eradicate noxious weeds and pests ;
(4) Conduct or assist any governmental agency in con-
ducting local or county -wide surveys to determine the degree of
Johnson grass infestation ;
(5) Cooperate with Federal or State agencies in research
and educational work in the field of pest and noxious weed
control ;
( 6 ) Assist counties in qualifying for cost sharing programs
to control Johnson grass or other noxious weeds ;
(7) Publicize the threat of noxious weeds and pests and
methods of combating them ;
( 8 ) Cooperate with the Department of Highways , county
fiscal courts , transportation and industrial organizations in con-
trolling Johnson grass and other noxious weeds on their premises
and right of ways .
(9 ) Accept funds or contributions to carry out the pur-
poses of this Act .
Section 6. To carry out the purposes of this Act, there is
appropriated to the department out of the General Expenditure
Fund in the State Treasury the sum of fifty thousand dollars for
the 1960-61 fiscal year and the sum of fifty thousand dollars for
the 1961-62 fiscal year .
Approved March 17, 1960

recognized by the Governor , whose appointment is not to

(10) "Employe " means a person regularly appointed to

( 11 ) "Position " means an office or employment in an agency
(whether part-time , full -time , temporary or permanent , occu-

person .
(12) "Probation " means the period of service following
appointment to any position under this Act which requires
special observation and evaluation of an employe's work and
which must be passed successfully before status may be con-
ferred as provided in Section 9 of this Act. An employe serv-
ing the probationary period is known as a " probationary
employe" .
(13) "Promotion " means a change of rank of an employe
from a position of one class to a position of another class hav-
ing a higher minimum salary and carrying a greater scope of
discretion or responsibility .
(14) "Provisional employe " means any employe appointed
on a temporary or interim basis to a permanent position under
this Act without examination , such appointment being made
necessary by the absence of a register for the position involved
and by the time limitations and prohibition on renewal as de-
fined in Section 9 of this Act .

(16) "Register " means any official list of eligibles for a

renewable ; a "temporary employe " is one appointed to such
temporary position , either from an existing pertinent eligible
list or without reference to an eligible list. A "seasonal posi-
tion " , although temporary in duration in the foregoing sense ,
coincides in duration with a particular season or seasons of
the year and may recur regularly from year to year ; a "seasonal
employe " is one appointed to such a seasonal position from an
existing eligible list or without reference to an eligible list .
(19) "Transfer " means a movement of any employe from
one position to another of the same grade having the same
salary ranges and the same level of responsibility within the
classified service .

have vacated his office and the Governor shall proceed forthwith
to appoint a successor to his unexpired term .
The members of the first board shall be appointed by the
Governor within thirty days of the effective date of this Act. Of
the members first appointed , one shall serve until January 1,
1961 , one until January 1, 1962 , one until January 1, 1963 , and
two until January 1, 1964. Thereafter , each term shall run for
four years from the date of expiration of the term for which his
predecessor was appointed , except that a person appointed to fill
a vacancy prior to the expiration of such a term shall be appointed
for the remainder of the term . Each member of the board shall
hold office until his successor is appointed and qualifies .
Members of the Personnel Board may be removed by the
Governor only for cause after being given a copy of charges
against them and an opportunity to be heard publicly, with the
advice of counsel , on such charges against them before the
Governor and a finding of fact by the Governor . A copy of the
charges and transcript of the record of the hearing and findings
of fact shall be filed with the Secretary of State .

candidates on a continuous basis long enough to assure a suffi-
cient number of eligibles to meet the needs of the service, and
shall add the names of successful candidates to existing eligible
lists in accordance with their respective ratings . The commis-
sioner shall be free to use any investigation of education and
experience and any test of capacity , knowledge , manual skill ,
character , personal traits , or physical fitness which in his judg-
ment serves the need to discover the relative fitness of applicants .
(4) For the granting of appropriate preference in original
entrance examinations to qualified persons who have been mem-
bers of the armed forces of the United States in time of war , and
who were honorably discharged . Such preference shall be in
the form of points added to the final passing grades of such
persons on original entrance examinations , provided that they
otherwise qualify and are entitled to appear on the list of those
eligible for appointment . The preference may be as much as
five points for non -disabled persons, and as much as ten points
for persons currently receiving compensation from the U. S.
Veterans Administration for war -service -incurred disabilities
certified as compensable . The rank of such persons on the
eligible list shall then be determined on the basis of their aug-
mented rating .
(5) For promotions which shall give appropriate con-
sideration to the applicant's qualifications , record of perform-
ance , conduct and seniority . Vacancies shall be filled by
promotion whenever practicable and in the best interest of the
service .
(6) For the establishment of eligible lists for appointment ,
upon which lists shall be placed the names of successful candi-
dates in the order of their relative excellence in the respective
examinations . Eligibility lists shall expire automatically at the
end of two years , unless the life of a list is extended by action
of the Personnel Board for a period not to exceed one additional
year.
(7) For the rejection of candidates or eligibles who fail
to comply with reasonable requirements of the commissioner
in regard to such factors as age , physical condition , training and
experience , or who have been guilty of infamous or disgraceful
conduct ; who are addicted to alcohol to excess or to narcotics ,

CHAPTER 63 223
or who have attempted any deception or fraud in connection
with an examination .
(8) For the appointment of a person whose score is in-
cluded in the three highest scores earned on the examination .
( 9 ) For a period of probation not to exceed one year
before appointment or promotion may be made complete , and
during which period a probationer may be discharged or reduced
in class or rank , or replaced on the eligible list. The appointing
authority shall , within (30) days prior to the expiration on an
employe's probationary period , notify the commissioner in writ-
ing whether the probationary employe is recommended for perm-
anent status . Only if the appointing authority certifies to the
satisfactory nature of the probationary employe's services , shall
permanent status be conferred .
( 10 ) For emergency employment for not more than thirty
days with or without examination , with the consent of the
commissioner .
( 11 ) For provisional employment without competitive
examination when there is no appropriate eligible list available .
No such provisional employment shall continue longer than six
months , nor shall successive provisional appointment be allowed ,
except during the first year after the effective date of this law
in order to avoid stoppage of orderly conduct of the business
of the Commonwealth .
( 12) For transfer from a position in one department to a
similar position in another department involving similar qualifi-
cations , duties , responsibilities , and salary ranges .
( 13 ) For reinstatement within not more than five ( 5)
years , only with the approval of the commissioner , of persons
who resign in good standing or who are laid off from their posi-
tions without fault or delinquency on their part .

(b) Employes holding positions in the classified service

the effective date of this Act, who pass or have passed quali-

and who are recommended by their respective department ,
board, commission or agency head for the position they held,
shall continue in the positions and have status in the classified
service without further examination .
(c) Those employes holding their positions for less than
one year immediately prior to the effective date of this Act
may also be continued in their respective positions provided
within one year after this law takes effect they pass a qualify-
ing examination prescribed by the Commissioner of Personnel .
The qualifying examinations may have been given before the
effective date of this Act provided they are approved by the
Commissioner of Personnel after the effective date of this Act
as representing reasonable qualifying tests of fitness for the
positions in which the employes are serving or are to serve
on or after the effective date of this Act . Such employes shall
be considered provisional employes and will be retained in
the provisional category until they have qualified by examina-
tion no later than one year from the effective date of this Act
and have completed a probationary period of one year for the
positions they hold.
(d) The commissioner shall conduct examinations to pro-
vide eligible lists to fill positions held by unqualified employes ,
as well as examinations to qualify present employes , as ex-
peditiously as is practicable and in any event no later than
one year from the effective date of this Act . Nothing herein
shall preclude the reclassification or reallocation as provided
by law of any position held by any such incumbent , and from
the date of this Act all state employes , whether enjoying status
or not, shall be expected to perform their duties efficiently
and adhere to the highest ethical principles of conduct in such
performance . Any employe who fails to pass the examination

228 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for his position shall be dismissed from his position 30 days
after the establishment of an appropriate eligible list .
(e) As of the effective date of this Act all hiring for the
classified service shall be on the basis of competitive examina-
tions and certification by the Department of Personnel in ac-
cordance with the provisions of this Act .
Section 13. Certification of payrolls . ( a ) No disbursing or
auditing officer of the Commonwealth shall make or approve or
take any part in making or approving any payment for personal
service to any person holding a position in the classified or un-
classified service unless the payroll voucher or account of such
pay bears the certification of the commissioner, or of his auth-
orized agent , that the persons named therein have been appointed
and employed in accordance with the provisions of this law and
the rules , regulations , and orders thereunder . The commissioner
may for proper cause withhold certification from an entire payroll
or from any specific item or items thereon . The commissioner
may , however , provide that certification of payrolls may be
made once every six months , and such certification shall re-
main in effect except in the case of any officer or employe
whose status has changed after the last certification of his
payroll. In the latter case no voucher for payment of salary
to such employe shall be issued or payment of salary made
without the further certification of the commissioner .
(b) Any citizen , including public officers , may maintain a
suit to restrain a disbursing officer from making any payment
in contravention of any provision of this law, rule , regulation
or order thereunder . Any sum paid contrary to any provision
of this law or of any rule , regulation , or order thereunder may
be recovered in an action maintained by any citizen , from any
officer who made , approved , or authorized such payment or
who signed or countersigned a voucher , payroll , check or war-
rant for such payment , or from the sureties on the official bond
of any such officer . All monies recovered in any such action
shall be paid into the Treasury .
(c) Any person appointed or employed in contravention
of any provision of this law or of any rule , regulation , or order
thereunder , who performs service for which he is not paid , may
maintain an action against the officer or officers , employe or

CHAPTER 63 229
employes , who purported so to appoint or employ him , to re-
cover the agreed pay for such services , or the reasonable value
thereof if no pay was agreed upon . No such officer or employe
shall be reimbursed by the Commonwealth at any time for
any sum paid to such person on account of such services .
(d) If the commissioner wrongfully withholds certifica-
tion of the payroll voucher or account of any employe , such
employe may maintain a proceeding in the courts to compel
the commissioner to certify such payroll voucher or account .
Section 14. Appeals by employes to the Personnel Board .
Any classified employe who is dismissed , demoted , sus-
pended , or otherwise penalized after completing his probationary
period of service may , within thirty days after such dismissal , de-
motion , suspension or penalization appeal to the Personnel Board
for review thereof . Upon such review , both the appealing
employe and the appointing authority whose action is reviewed
shall have the right to be heard publicly and to be represented
by counsel to present evidentiary facts . At the hearing of
such appeals , technical rules of evidence shall not apply . If
the Personnel Board finds that the action complained of was
taken by the appointing authority for any political, religious ,
or ethnic reason, the employe shall be reinstated to his former
position or a position of like status and pay , without loss of
pay for the period of his suspension. In all other cases , the
findings and recommendations of the Personnel Board shall be
submitted to the appointing authority for his consideration ,
who may, not later than thirty days after receipt of such find-
ings and recommendations , reinstate the employe with or with-
out pay for the period of the suspension, modify his original
decision of suspension, demotion , or discharge , or continue his
original decision . When any employe is dismissed and not rein-
stated after such appeal , the Personnel Board in
itsdiscretion
may direct that his name be placed
on
anappropriate
re-em-
ployment list
, for employment
inany similar position other
than the one from which he had been removed
. Any action
or
decision taken
ormade hereunder shall
befinal and not subject
tojudicial review
.
