CHAPTER 63 231
Section 19. Political activities prohibited . ( a) No person
shall be appointed or promoted to, or demoted or dismissed from ,
any position in the classified service , or in any way favored or dis-
criminated against with respect to employment in the classified
services because of his political or religious opinions or affiliations
or ethnic origin .
(b) No person shall use or promise to use, directly or
indirectly , any official authority or influence , whether possessed
or anticipated , to secure or attempt to secure for any person an
appointment or advantage in appointment to a position in the
classified service , or an increase in pay or other advantage in em-
ployment in any such position , for the purpose of influencing the
vote or political action of any person .
(c) No employe in the classified service or member of the
Personnel Board or commissioner shall , directly or indirectly ,
pay or promise to pay any assessment for political purposes , or
solicit or take any part in soliciting for any political party , or
solicit or take any part in soliciting any political assessment ,
subscription , contribution , or service . No person shall solicit any
political assessment , subscription , contribution , or service of
any employe in the classified service .
(d) No employe in the classified service shall be a member
of any national , state, or local committee of a political party , or
an officer or member of a committee of a partisan political club ,
or a candidate for nomination or election to any paid public
office , or shall take part in the management or affairs of any
political party or in any political campaign , except to exercise his
right as a citizen privately to express his opinion and to cast his
vote . Officers or employes of the classified service may be candi-
dates for and occupy a town or school district office if the office
is one for which no compensation is provided and the election
is on a nonpartisan basis .
(e) Any officer or employe of the classified service who
wilfully violates any of the foregoing provisions of this section
shall forfeit his office or position , and for one year shall be ineligi-
ble for any office or position in the Commonwealth's service .
Violation of this section shall constitute a misdemeanor subject
to a sentence of from thirty days to a maximum of six months in
jail .

232 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 20. Unlawful acts prohibited . ( a ) No person shall
make any false statement , certificate , mark , rating , or report with
regard to any test , certification , or appointment made under any
provision of this law or in any manner commit or attempt to com-
mit any fraud preventing the impartial execution of this law and
the rules , regulations or orders thereunder .
(b) No person shall , directly or indirectly , give , render ,
pay , offer , solicit , or accept any money , service , or other valuable
consideration for or on account of any appointment , proposed
appointment , promotion , or proposed promotion to, or any ad-
vantage in , a position in the classified service .
(c) No employe of the department , examiner , or other
person shall defeat , deceive , or obstruct any person in his right
to examination , eligibility , certification , or appointment under
this law, or furnish to any person any special or secret informa-
tion for the purpose of affecting the rights or prospects of any
person with respect to employment in the classified service .
Section 21. Appropriations . ( a ) Appropriations shall be
made from the general expenditure fund to the Department of
Personnel to meet the estimated pro rata share of the cost of ad-
ministering the provisions of this Act for departments , commis-
sions , boards or agencies which receive their sole support from
the general expenditure fund .
(b) The commissioner shall maintain accurate records re-
flecting the cost of administering the provisions of this Act. At
the close of each quarter year period he shall summarize the cost
and shall bill each department , commission , board or agency
which receives support from sources other than the general
expenditure fund, except the Department of Fish and Wild-
life resources , for a pro rata share of the administrative cost
based on the relationship between the quarterly average number
of employes in the service of such department , commission , board
or agency and the quarterly average number of employes in the
service of
allthe departments
, commissions
, boards and agencies
for the appropriate calendar quarter
.
(c) All departments
, commissions
, boards
oragencies which
receive support from other than the general expenditure
fund
, except the Department
ofFish and Wildlife Resources
,
shall include
intheir budgets sufficient amounts
tomeet their

CHAPTER 63 233
pro rata shares of the cost of administering this Act and shall
remit such shares quarterly to the Department of Personnel in
the manner provided by law .
(d) The department is authorized and directed to accept
on behalf of the state any grant or contribution , federal or other-
wise , made to assist in meeting the cost of carrying out the pur-
poses of this Act .
Section 22. Federal standards . The Personnel Board and the
commissioner shall see that rules , regulations and practices meet-
ing federal merit system standards shall , where such standards
apply as a prerequisite for federal grants - in - aid, be in effect con-
tinuously , notwithstanding any other provision of this Act .
Section 23. Penalties . ( a) Any person who wilfully violates
any provision of this law or of the rules shall be guilty of a mis-
demeanor , and shall upon conviction be punished therefor with a
sentence of from thirty days to a maximum of six months in jail .
(b) Any person who is convicted of a misdemeanor under
this law shall , for a period of five years , be ineligible for ap-
pointment to or employment in a position by the Commonwealth ,
and if he is an officer or employe of the Commonwealth , shall
forfeit his office or position .
Section 24. Separability . If any provision of this law or of
any rule , regulation , or order thereunder or the application there-
of to any person , department , or circumstances shall be held in-
valid , the remainder of this law and the application of such pro-
vision of this law or of such rule , regulation , or order to persons ,
departments , or circumstances other than those as to which it is
held invalid shall not be affected thereby .

CHAPTER 65
(Н. В. 236 )
Approved March 17, 1960
AN ACT relating to the administration of estates .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Upon the death of a patient , or of a person who has been
a patient , in a hospital or school operated by the Department
of Mental Health , or upon the death of a person who is obli-
gated under KRS 203.080 for the support of such a patient when
such person is possessed of an estate , the executor or adminis-
trator of the deceased's estate shall upon receipt of letters of
appointment , ascertain from the Department whether the sup-
port as provided in KRS 203.070 and 203.090 has been fully paid.
Approved March 17 , 1960

CHAPTER 68
(Н. В. 439 )
Approved March 17 , 1960
AN ACT providing for reorganizing and simplifying certain departments
of State Government for the purpose of better service and economy ;
providing for the departments and the independent agencies therein
specified ; assigning and redistributing among them the powers and
duties of existing offices , departments , boards , commissions , institu-
tions and other agencies ; abolishing certain offices , departments ,
boards , commissions , institutions , and other agencies ; transferring
certain others ; creating others ; defining the organization , powers and
duties of offices , departments , boards , commissions and other agencies
that are hereby created or retained and providing for their coordina-
tion; fixing methods of appointments and election to office , terms of
office and salaries , and defining duties of, qualifications for , and
methods, of removal from , offices and positions ; fixing certain fees ;
repealing all laws and parts of laws in conflict with the provisions
of the Act ; appropriating funds for carrying out the purposes of
this Act ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
ARTICLE I. ADMINISTRATIVE DEPARTMENTS
Section 12.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Except as otherwise provided by law, the functions of the
state are divided among the constitutional administrative de-
partments , statutory administrative departments and independ-
ent agencies as follows :
I. Constitutional Administrative Departments :
1. The Governor .
2. Department of State .
3. Department of Law.
4. Department of Treasury .

262 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
5. Department of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics .
6. Department of Education .
7. Department of Military Affairs .
II . Statutory Administrative Departments :
1. Department of Finance .
2. Department of Personnel .
3. Department of Revenue .
4. Department of Highways .
5. Department of Welfare .
6. Department of Health .
7. Department of Mental Health .
8. Department of Industrial Relations .

10. Department of Aeronautics .

ofBanking

CHAPTER 68 263
ARTICLE II . DEPARTMENT OF LAW
Section 1. The Attorney General may promulgate and file
with the Legislative Research Commission those rules and reg-
ulations which will facilitate performing the duties and exer-
cising the authority vested in the Attorney General and the De-
partment of Law by law .
Section 2. ( 1 ) The Attorney General , with the approval
of the commissioner of the department or the commission in-
volved shall appoint an Assistant Attorney General for the De-
partment of Highways , for the Public Service Commission , for
the Department of Revenue , and for the Railroad Commission .
( 2) The assistant attorneys general and additional attor-
neys provided for in subsection ( 1 ) of this section shall each be
a person admitted to the practice of law by the Court of Appeals
of this Commonwealth and shall qualify by taking the oath of
office. They shall be paid out of the appropriation or other funds
of the respective department or commission to which they are
assigned .
Section 3. The Attorney General , when requested in writ-
ing, under KRS 15.020 , shall furnish such opinions subject to the
following conditions :
(1 ) When questions of law of interest to the Common-
wealth are submitted by a state department , agency , board or
commission ;
( 2 ) When public questions of law are submitted by either
house of the legislature or by any member of the legislature ;
( 3 ) When public questions of law pertaining to local gov-
ernment are submitted in writing by the proper public official
of the county or other political subdivision of the Commonwealth ;
(4) When, in the discretion of the Attorney General , the
question presented is of such public interest that an Attorney
General's opinion on the subject is deemed desirable and when
provided for by regulation pursuant to the provisions of this
section .
Section 4. Section 12.210 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Governor , or any department with the approval
of the Governor , may employ and fix the term of employment

264 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and the compensation to be paid to an attorney or attorneys for
legal services to be performed for the Governor or for such de-
partment . Before approving the employment of an attorney the
Governor shall consult the Attorney General as to whether legal
services requested by departments are available in the Attorney
General's office . The compensation and expenses of any attorney
or attorneys employed under the provisions of this section shall
be paid out of the appropriations made to such department as
other salaries , compensation and expenses are paid , except when
the terms of employment provide that the compensation shall be
on a contingent basis , and in such event the attorneys may be
paid the amount specified out of the moneys recovered by them
or out of the General Expenditure Fund. If the Governor ap-
proves the employment , the terms of employment shall be duly
entered by executive order upon the Executive Journal in the of-
fice of the Secretary of State .
(2 ) An attorney may be employed pursuant to the pro-
visions of subsection ( 1) of this section to render legal services
for one or more departments , boards , agencies or commissions.
Section 5. Section 15.100 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Attorney General shall appoint a Deputy Attorney
General , who shall qualify as required by law, to serve at the
pleasure of the Attorney General , and such assistants and special
attorneys as he deems necessary to transact the business of the
Department of Law, and perform such duties as he may desig-
nate . The deputy attorney general , assistants , and special attor-
neys shall have full power , as authorized and under the direction
of the Attorney General , to perform such duties as may be per-
formed by the Attorney General . The Attorney General shall
be responsible for the official acts of his deputy , assistants , and
special attorneys .
( 2) In addition to the appointment and designation of
assistants and special attorneys pursuant to subsection ( 1 ) of
this section , the Attorney General may enter into such contracts
for legal services as he deems necessary and advisable .
( 3 ) Each assistant or special attorney so appointed or
designated shall be a person admitted to the practice of law by

CHAPTER 68 265
the Court of Appeals of this Commonwealth and shall qualify
by taking the oath of office .
Section 6. Sections 15.110 , 15.115 , 15.120 and 15.140 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes are hereby repealed .
ARTICLE III . DIVISION OF SURPLUS PROPERTY
Section 1. There shall be established a Division of Surplus
Property in the Department of Education .
Section 2. The Division of Surplus Property is authorized
and empowered to receive, warehouse, and distribute surplus
property under the provisions of the Federal Property and Ad-
ministrative Services Act of 1949 , as amended, and other federal
laws relating to surplus property . The division shall comply
with federal laws and regulations in the administration of surplus
property received through federal agencies , and shall establish
with the approval of the State Board of Education such rules and
regulations necessary for compliance with minimum standards
established therein .
Section 3. All functions , personnel , funds , records , equip-
ment, and facilities heretofore in the Division of Property Utiliza-
tion shall be transferred to the Division of Surplus Property and
the Division of Property Utilization is hereby abolished .
ARTICLE IV . KENTUCKY SCHOOL FOR THE DEAF
Section 1. The Kentucky School for the Deaf at Danville ,
Kentucky , shall be managed and controlled by the State Board
of Education . The board shall have possession and the re-
sponsibility and authority for preservation , repair and control
of the buildings and grounds belonging to the state and dedicated
to the school . The board may, except as provided in KRS
42.060 , sell any property held for the use and benefit of the
school, and purchase other property deemed by the board to be
suitably and conveniently located , and erect buildings necessary
for carrying out the purposes of the school . The board may
adopt rules and regulations to carry into effect
itspowers with
respect
tothe school
, and may require from the superintendent
of
the school any reports and information
itdesires
as
tothe con-
dition of the school
.

ARTICLE V. STATE LOCAL FINANCE OFFICER
Section 1. All of the functions of the State Local Finance
Officer provided in Chapters 66, 68, 103 , and 131 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes relating to the issuance of bonds and control
of funds of counties , cities , and other local units and county
financial administration shall be transferred to the Department
ofFinance .
Section 2. The records , property , personnel , and any un-
expended appropriations balances maintained for carrying out
the functions of the State Local Finance Officer shall be trans-
ferred to the Department of Finance .
Section 3. Section 66.280 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Whenever used in KRS 66.290 to 66.390 , unless the
context requires otherwise :
(a) "Commission " means the County Debt Commission
as hereinafter established .
(b) "State Local Finance Officer " means the Commissioner
of Finance or his agent designated in writing with the approval
of the Governor .
( c) "Fiscal court " means the judge of the county court
and the justices of the peace or the judge of the county court
and the commissioners of the county .
(d) "Funding bonds " or "funded indebtedness " means
bonds issued to fund floating indebtedness , or reissues thereof .
( e) "Road and bridge bonds " means county road and

268 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bridge bonds issued under the authorization of Section 157a of
the Constitution of Kentucky , or reissues thereof .
( f ) "Refunding bonds " means bonds issued for the pur-
pose of reissuing or renewing outstanding bonds .
( 2 ) KRS 66.280 to 66.390 may be cited as the " County
Debt Act . "
Section 4. Section 66.380 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) The State Local Finance Officer, with the approval of
the commission, shall have authority to invest any funds accrued
to the County Sinking Fund and the fiscal court shall have
authority to invest any funds accrued to and maintained in a
county sinking fund which are not needed to meet interest and
principal maturities on county debts within the next succeeding
twelve months in obligations secured by the credit of the United
States Government , or in warrants of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or in road and bridge bonds of any county of the
Commonwealth which have been approved by the commission
for such investment , provided that the securities thus purchased
shall become payable before the money with which they were
purchased shall be required for meeting obligations of the sink-
ing fund . Nothing in this section shall prevent a county or the
State Local Finance Officer from purchasing any bonds , at a
price which will result in a saving to the county , prior to their
maturity date with sinking fund monies created for their redemp-
tion; provided that such monies are not required for the redemp-
tion of bonds maturing prior to those purchased , and provided
further that bonds so purchased shall be canceled and interest
thereon shall cease .
(2 ) Any earnings or losses of the County Sinking Fund
shall be charged or credited as of July 1 of each year among the
counties maintaining deposits therein in the ratio which the
largest amount in any sinking fund account continuously on
deposit for a period of six months during the twelve months pre-
ceding allocation bears to the sum of such amounts for

fund accounts

ARTICLE VI . DIVISION OF MENTAL RETARDATION
Section 1. There shall be established in the Department of
Welfare a Division of Mental Retardation , the executive head
of which shall be appointed by the Commissioner of Welfare in
accordance with the provisions of KRS 12.050 .
Section 2. The function of the Division of Mental Retarda-
tion shall be to develop and encourage the development of pro-
grams in the areas of unmet need for the mentally retarded and
to coordinate and assist in developing further the programs as-
signed to or performed by private and governmental agencies .
Section 3. The division may , to the extent that funds are
available , enter into cooperative agreements with public or
private agencies operating facilities for the care, treatment and
training of the mentally retarded .
Section 4. The duties of the Division of Mental Retarda-
tion may include but are not limited to :
( 1 ) Developing community resources .
(2) Assisting in the development of parent counseling
centers , vocational services and diagnostic clinic facilities and
services .
(3 ) Advising local and state agencies as to the needs of
the mentally retarded .

CHAPTER 68 271
(4 ) Fostering interdepartmental cooperation among the
state agencies responsible for providing services to the mentally
retarded .
Section 5. ( 1 ) There shall be established a Mental Re-
tardation Board attached to the Division of Mental Retardation .
( 2 ) The Mental Retardation Board shall be composed of
five members , appointed by the Governor , for terms of four years .
Members shall serve until their successors are appointed . The
board shall elect from
itsmembership
achairman
. Members
of
the board shall serve without compensation but shall be reim-
bursed for necessary expenses incurred
inthe performance

3) The Mental Retardation Board shall act

inregard

(4) "Medical care" as used in this article means essential
medical , surgical , dental , and nursing services, in the home ,

service , including drugs and medical supplies , appliances , labora-

valescent care , hospital care as defined in Section 7 ( 1 ) ( a ) of

( 6 ) "Nursing home " means a facility which provides rou-

which provides nursing services and procedures employed in
caring for the sick which require training , judgment , technical
knowledge , and skills beyond that which the untrained person
possesses , and which maintains complete records on patient care ,
and which is licensed pursuant to the provisions of KRS 216.400 ;
(7 ) “Pharmacist " means a person authorized to practice
pharmacy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 8 ) " Physician " means a person authorized to practice
medicine or osteopathy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 9 ) " Public assistance recipient " means a person who
has been certified by the Division of Public Assistance of the
Department of Economic Security as being eligible for, and a
recipient of, public assistance under the provisions of Chapter
205 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes or a needy person who is
living with and essential to such recipient and is recognized in
the determination of budgetary requirements for such payment ;
( 10 ) "Vendor payment " means a payment for medical care
which is paid by the Department of Economic Security directly
to the authorized person or institution which rendered medical
care to a public assistance recipient .
Section 4. This article shall be administered by the De-
partment of Economic Security which shall contract with the
Department of Health for the medical care aspects of the pro-
gram as provided in Section 8 of this article .
Section 5. ( 1 ) An Advisory Council for Medical Assist-
ance shall be established in the state government . The council

CHAPTER 68 273
shall consist of eleven members . The Commissioner of Health
and the Commissioner of the Department of Economic Security
shall be ex-officio members . The other nine members of the
council shall be appointed by the Governor and shall hold office
for a term of four years and until their successors are appointed
and qualify , except that the members appointed to fill the first
vacancy occurring for a term beginning on July 1, 1960 , shall be
as follows :
Two members shall be appointed for one year , two for two
years , two for three years , and three for four years , and the respeс-
tive terms of the first members shall be designated by the Gover-
nor at the time of their appointments . Upon the expiration of the
respective terms of the members first appointed , the term of each
successor shall be for four years or until his successor is appointed
and qualified . Appointments shall be made one from each list of
three nominees submitted by the following organizations : the
Kentucky State Medical Association ; the Kentucky Dental As-
sociation ; the Kentucky Hospital Association ; the Kentucky Phar-
maceutical Association ; and the Kentucky State Association of
Registered Nurses . The other four appointive members shall be
intelligent and discreet citizens residing within the Common-
wealth , who are not members of professional groups which are
recipients of vendor payments . No nominee or member of the
council shall be a stockholder or have any financial interest in
any hospital , nursing home , or related institution .
(2) Each appointive member of the council shall serve
without compensation but shall be entitled to reimbursement
for his actual and necessary expenses in carrying out his duties .
(3) Vacancies shall be filled for the unexpired term in the
same manner as original appointments , maintaining original
representations .

ARTICLE VIII .
Section 1. ( 1 )
DIVISION OF BOATING
There shall be established in the Depart-
ment of Public Safety a Division of Boating .
( 2) The head of the Division of Boating shall be appointed
by the Commissioner of Public Safety in accordance with the
provisions of KRS 12.050 .
( 3 ) The Division of Boating shall be authorized to employ
necessary personnel to carry out the functions assigned to the
division in this article .
Section 2. This article shall be known as the State Boating
Act and shall be administered by the Division of Boating .
Section 3. Definitions . As used in this article unless the
context clearly requires a different meaning :
( 1 ) "Vessel ” means every description of watercraft , other
than a seaplane on the water , used or capable of being used as a
means of transportation on water .
( 2 ) "Motorboat ” means any vessel propelled by machinery ,
whether or not such machinery is the principal source of pro-
pulsion , but shall not include a vessel which has a valid
marine document issued by the Bureau of Customs of the United
States Government or any federal agency successor thereto .
( 3 ) "Owner ” means a person , other than a lien holder ,
having the property in or title to a motorboat . The term in-
cludes a person entitled to the use or possession of a motorboat
subject to an interest in another person , reserved or created by
agreement and securing payment or performance of an obliga-
tion , but the term excludes a lessee under a lease not intended
as security .
(4) "Waters of this state " means any waters within the
territorial limits of this state .
( 5 ) "Person " means an individual , partnership , firm , cor-
poration , association, or other entity .
(6) " Operate " means to navigate or otherwise use a motor-
boat or a vessel.

CHAPTER 68 279
(7 ) "Department " means the Department of Public
Safety .
(8 ) "Division” means the Division of Boating within the
Department of Public Safety.
(9) " License " and "certificate of number " as used herein
are synonymous .
(10) "Clerk ” means circuit court clerk .
Section 4. Every motorboat on the waters of this state shall
be numbered . No person shall operate or give permission for
the operation of any motorboat on such waters unless the motor-
boat is registered and numbered in accordance with this article ,
or in accordance with the applicable federal law , or in accordance
with a federally approved numbering system of another state,
and unless ( 1 ) the certificate of number awarded to such motor-
boat is in full force and effect , and ( 2 ) the identifying number
set forth in the certificate of number is displayed on each side
of the bow of such motorboat .

(3) Any person who, by negligent operation of a motor-
boat , causes the death of another , under circumstances not
otherwise punishable as a homicide , shall be imprisoned in the
county jail for not more than one year .
Section 27. ( 1 ) It shall be the duty of the operator of
a vessel involved in a collision , accident , or other casualty , so far
as he can do so without serious danger to his own vessel , crew ,
and passengers ( if any ) , to render to other persons affected by
the collision , accident , or other casualty such assistance as may
be practicable and as may be necessary in order to save them
from or minimize any danger caused by the collision , accident
or other casualty , and also to give his name, address , and identi-
fication of his vessel in writing to any person injured and to the
owner of any property damaged in the collision , accident , or
other casualty .
(2) In the case of collision , accident , or other casualty
involving a vessel, the operator thereof , if the collision , accident
or other casualty results in death or injury to a person or damage
to property in excess of one hundred dollars shall file with the
division a full description of the collision , accident , or other
casualty , including such information as said agency may , by
regulation , require .
Section 28. All required accident reports and supplemental
reports shall be without prejudice to the individual making them ,
and shall be for the confidential use of the division , except that the
division may disclose the identity of a person involved in an ac-
cident when his identity is not otherwise known or when he
denies his presence at an accident . No such report shall be used
in evidence in any trial arising out of an accident , but the division
shall furnish upon demand of any person who has, or claims to
have , made such a report or, upon demand of any court , a certi-
ficate showing that a specified accident report has or has not
been made to the division , solely in order to prove compliance
or noncompliance with the requirements of Section 27 of this
article that such a report be made .
Section 29. In accordance with any request duly made by
an authorized official or agency of the United States, any infor-
mation compiled or otherwise available to the division pursuant

CHAPTER 68 287
to Section 27 of this article shall be transmitted to said official
or agency of the United States .
Section 30. The division , with the approval of the Com-
missioner of Public Safety , shall promulgate rules and regulations
to govern the operation of boats on any waters of this state tow-
ing a person or persons on water skis , a surfboard , or similar de-
vice.
Section 31. ( 1 ) The division may authorize the holding
of regattas, motorboat or other boat races , marine parades, tourn-
aments or exhibitions on any waters of this state. It shall adopt
and may , from time to time , amend regulations concerning the
safety of motorboats and other vessels and persons thereon , either
observers or participants . Whenever a regatta , motorboat or
other boat race, marine parade , tournament or exhibition is
proposed to be held, the person in charge thereof shall , at least
fifteen days prior thereto , file an application with the division
for permission to hold such regatta, motorboat or other boat race,
marine parade , tournament or exhibition . The application shall
set forth the date, time and location where it is proposed to hold
such regatta , motorboat or other boat race, marine parade , tourn-
ament or exhibition , and it shall not be conducted without au-
thorization of the division in writing .
( 2 ) The provisions of this section shall not exempt any
person from compliance with applicable federal law or regula-
tion, but nothing contained herein shall be construed to require
the securing of a state permit pursuant to this section if a permit
therefor has been obtained from an authorized agency of the
United States .
Section 32. The operator of a vessel or motorboat shall be
liable for any injury or damage occasioned by the negligent
operation of such vessel or motorboat , whether such negligence
consists of a violation of the provisions of the statutes of this
state or neglecting to observe such ordinary care and operation as
the rules of the common law require . Where the owner is not
the operator of the vessel or motorboat he shall not be liable for
such injury or damage unless such owner is aboard the vessel or
motorboat at the time of such injury or damage or unless the
operator at such time of injury or damage is operating said boat

upon the owner's business or in the course and scope of his em-
ployment with the owner . The "family purpose doctrine " as
it is applied in the use and operation of automobiles shall be
applicable to the use and operation of vessels or motorboats .
Nothing contained herein shall be construed to relieve any other
person from any liability which he would otherwise have, but
nothing contained herein shall be construed to authorize or per-
mit any recovery in excess of injury or damage actually incurred .

Section 39. KRS 17.010 is hereby amended to read as
follows :
(1) There shall be in the state government a Department
of Public Safety , a statutory administrative department within
the meaning of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
the executive head of which shall be a Commissioner of Public
Safety .
( 2 ) The Commissioner of Public Safety shall be appointed
by the Governor as provided in KRS 12.040 for the heads of
statutory administrative departments .
( 3 ) The Department of Public Safety shall be divided for
administrative purposes into a Division of Accident Control , a
Division of Kentucky State Police , a Division of Fire Prevention ,
a Division of Driver Licensing , and a Division of Boating , but
this provision shall not affect the authorization of the Commis-
sioner of Public Safety to establish new divisions and make
changes with respect to existing divisions in accordance with the
terms of KRS 12.030 .
(4) The heads of the division of the Department of Public
Safety shall be appointed by the Commissioner of Public Safety
in accordance with the provisions of KRS 12.050 .
ARTICLE IX . DEPARTMENT OF CHILD WELFARE
Section 1. ( 1 ) There shall be established in the state
government a Department of Child Welfare , a statutory adminis-
trative department within the meaning of Chapter 12 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , the executive head of which shall be
a Commissioner of Child Welfare .

290 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) The Commissioner of Child Welfare shall be appointed
by the Governor as provided in KRS 12.040 for heads of statutory
administrative departments and shall receive an annual salary
of $ 11,000 .
Section 2. There shall be established a Kentucky Children's
Advisory Council which shall be attached to the Department of
Child Welfare . It shall be composed of eleven members who are
appointed by the Governor , to serve part time for staggered,
overlapping four -year terms on the following basis :
( 1) One member of the council shall be a diplomate
psychiatrist , preferably with experience in child psychiatry , ap-
pointed from a list of three names of eligible psychiatrists sub-
mitted by the State Association of Diplomate Psychiatrists ;
( 2) One member of the council shall be appointed from a
list of three names submitted by each of the recognized state-
wide veterans associations ;
( 3 ) Two members of the council shall be appointed from
a list of three names for each position submitted by the chapters
in the state of the National Association for Social Workers ;
(4) Two members of the council shall be appointed from
a list of three names for each position submitted by the Kentucky
Citizens for Child Welfare , Inc .;
(5) One member of the council shall be appointed from a
list of three names submitted by the Kentucky Education Asso-
ciation ;
( 6 ) One member of the council shall be appointed from
a list of three names submitted by the League of Women Voters ;
(7) One member of the council shall be appointed from a
list of three names of eligible psychologists submitted by the
Kentucky Psychological Association ;
(8 ) One member of the council shall be appointed to re-
present the religions of the Commonwealth ;
( 9) One member of the council shall be appointed from
a list of three names submitted by the Kentucky Welfare Associa-
tion.

ARTICLE X. DEPARTMENT OF PARKS
Section 1. ( 1) There shall be established in the state govern-
ment a Department of Parks , a statutory administrative depart-
ment within the meaning of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , the executive head of which shall be a Commissioner of
Parks .
( 2 ) The Commissioner of Parks shall be appointed by the
Governor as provided in KRS 12.040 for the heads of statutory
administrative departments and shall receive an annual salary
of $ 12,000 .
( 3 ) The Department of Parks shall be divided for adminis-
trative purposes into a Division of Operations , a Division of
Planning and Development , and a Division of Administrative
Services, but this provision shall not affect the authorization of
the Commissioner of Parks to establish new divisions and make
changes with respect to existing divisions in accordance with the
terms of KRS 12.030 .
(4) The heads of the divisions in the Department of Parks
shall be appointed by the Commissioner of Parks in accordance
with the provisions of KRS 12.050 .
Section 2. ( 1 ) There shall be established a Parks Board
attached to the Department of Parks . The Parks Board shall be
a corporate entity. It may receive , acquire , preserve, hold,
manage and convey land , caves , cave rights , leasehold interests ,
money and any other property deemed necessary by it.
(2) The Parks Board shall be composed of three members ,
appointed by the Governor , for terms of four years each and until
successors are appointed and qualified . Before entering upon
their duties the members shall each take and subscribe to the
constitutional oath of office , which shall be filed in the office
of the Secretary of State . The board shall elect from
itsmem-
bership
achairman
. The members
ofthe board shall serve with-
out compensation but shall
bereimbursed for necessary expenses
incurred
inthe performance
oftheir duties
.

Section 3. ( 1 ) The Parks Board shall act in an advisory
capacity to the Commissioner of Parks in regard to the planning ,
development , operation , and administration of the state park
system . It shall also act in an advisory capacity to the Com-
missioner of Parks in regard to the establishment and revision of
park standards and on the acquisition of land , properties or
sites for state and national parks .
(2) All of the functions of the National Park Commission
relating to acquiring and conveying lands to the United States
for the establishment of national parks in Kentucky ; receiving
grants and contributions ; and establishing regulations for ad-
ministration and disposition of property prior to conveyance
thereof to the United States , provided in KRS 148.110 , 148.120 ,
148.130 , 148.140 , and 148.150 are hereby transferred to the Parks
Board .
Section 4. The records, property , and any funds held by
the National Park Commission are hereby transferred to the
Parks Board .

ARTICLE XI . DIVISION OF BUSINESS AND
PROFESSIONAL REGULATION
Section 1. ( 1 ) There shall be established in the Depart-
ment of State a Division of Business and Professional Regulation .
(2 ) The head of the division shall be appointed by the
Secretary of State in accordance with the provisions of KRS
12.050 .
Section 2. ( 1 ) All of the functions of the Department
of Business Regulation relating to prize - fighting and wrestling
contained in Chapter 229 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are

298 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
hereby transferred to and shall be exercised by the Division of
Business and Professional Regulation .
(2 ) ( a ) The Division of Business and Professional Regu-
lation is authorized to provide clerical , secretarial or other ad-
ministrative services to any board established by law for the
purpose of licensing , registration , examination or regulation of
a business or a profession .
(b) Such services shall be provided upon the request of
the board and upon the approval of the Secretary of State .
( c) The Division of Business and Professional Regulation
shall maintain accurate records reflecting the cost of providing
such services and shall bill each board for the cost of such serv-
ices on a quarterly basis .
( d) Boards receiving such services shall remit to the Divis-
ion of Business and Professionel Regulation payment for services
received in the manner provided by law.
Section 3. The records , property , personnel and any unex-
pended appropriations balances and trust and agency funds
maintained for the purpose of carrying out the functions of the
Department of Business Regulation relating to regulation of
prize -fighting and wrestling are hereby transferred to the Divi-
sion of Business and Professional Regulation .
Section 4. Sections 286.010 , 286.020 , 286.030 , and 286.070
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are hereby repealed .
ARTICLE XII . PUBLIC SERVICE COMMISSION
Section 278.060 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1 ) Each commissioner shall be a resident and qualified
voter of this state, not less than twenty - five years of age at the
time of his appointment and qualification , and shall have resided
in this state for at least three years prior to his appointment and
qualification . Each commissioner shall take and subscribe to
the constitutional oath of office , which shall be recorded in the
office of the Secretary of State .
(2) No person shall be appointed to or hold the office of
commissioner who holds any official relationship to any utility , or
who owns any stocks or bonds thereof , or who has any pecuniary
interest therein .

CHAPTER 68 299
(3) No commissioner shall receive any rebate , pass , per-
centage of contract or other thing of value from any utility .
(4) No commissioner shall engage in any occupation or
business inconsistent with his duties as such commissioner .
(5) If any commissioner becomes a member of any politi-
cal party committee , his office as commissioner shall be thereby
vacated .

COMMISSION

and the Commissioner of Revenue .

ARTICLE XV . REORGANIZATION OF STATUTORY
ADMINISTRATIVE DEPARTMENTS
OF STATE GOVERNMENT
A new section to Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is added to read as follows :
Recognizing the problems resulting from a rapid expansion
of programs in many departments and in order to promote greater
economy, efficiency and improved administration in the statutory
administrative departments of state government by grouping
related functions and establishing more effective organizational
patterns , both of which require extensive studies extending be-
yond the regular legislative session , the Governor may :
( 1 ) Establish , abolish , or alter the organization of any
statutory administrative department including changing the
name of a department to explain more clearly the functions
performed by it . Also included in this authority shall be permis

304 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sion to transfer functions , personnel , funds , equipment , facilities ,
and records from one department to another . Reorganization
made under this section shall be set forth in an executive order ,
signed by the Governor and filed in the office of the Secretary of
State, which shall explain the changes made and designate the
functions , personnel , funds , equipment , facilities , and records ,
as applicable , to be transferred . The Governor shall recommend
legislation to the 1962 General Assembly to confirm re-organiza-
tions effected under provisions of this article .
(2) Appoint advisory or study committees to assist and
recommend to him on problems concerning the various functions
and departments of state government . Members of such com-
mittees shall serve without pay, but shall be reimbursed for
necessary and actual expenses .
ARTICLE XVI . APPROPRIATION
To carry out the purposes of this Act, there is appropriated
out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury to a
special Governor's emergency fund the sum of $340,000 for the
1960-61 fiscal year , and the sum of $365,000 for the 1961-62
fiscal year .
ARTICLE XVII . GENERAL PROVISIONS
Section 1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal
the provisions of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
except those sections specifically repealed by this Act .
Section 2. In order that appropriate accounts and budgets
may be established , all transfers of funds , functions , personnel ,
records , and facilities , made pursuant to this Act shall be super-
vised and approved by the Commissioner of Finance .
Section 3. All provisions of this Act, except Article XI ,
shall become operative July 1, 1960. Article X, Department of
Parks , shall become operative immediately upon passage and ap-
proval of this Act by the Governor .
ARTICLE XVIII . EMERGENCY CLAUSE
Whereas it is necessary that certain provisions of this Act
become effective immediately , an emergency is declared to exist ,
and this Act shall become effective upon
itspassage and ap-
proval by the Governor
.
Approved March 17
, 1960

CHAPTER 69
(Н. В. 56)
AN ACT relating to zoning and planning in cities of the third through
sixth classes and in counties containing such cities , and declaring
an emergency . This Act shall be known at the Kelly - Jackson-
Conley Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) Any city of the third , fourth , fifth or sixth
class and the county in which such city is located are hereby
authorized and empowered to enter into an agreement to
regulate the physical development , including planning and
zoning , of the incorporated area of such city and the unincorpo-
rated area of such county . Such agreement shall be in writing
and submitted to the legislative body of such city and the fiscal
court of such county , for approval or disapproval . If approved ,
the provisions of this Act and said agreement shall become
effective upon the adoption thereof by a mutual ordinance
and order passed by such legislative body and by such fiscal
court , respectively , setting forth therein said written agreement ,
and authorizing the execution and delivery of same . Thereupon
said agreement shall be recorded in the office of the clerk of the
county court of such county , and the city clerk or other appro-
priate official of such city.
(2 ) In the event that more than one city in such county
desires to avail itself of the benefits of this Act, such additional
city or cities are hereby authorized and empowered to enter into
an agreement or agreements as set out in subsection ( 1 ) of
this section with such county , with the territorial jurisdiction
of the joint city-county planning commissions terminating at a
line or lines equi -distant from the respective corporate limits of
the participating cities or in the event that such division is not
practical at a line or lines mutually agreed upon by the city-
county commissions involved . If agreement cannot be reached
by the commissions involved , then it shall be the duty of the
fiscal court to determine such line or lines .
( 3 ) The territorial jurisdiction of a joint city-county
planning commission shall not include the incorporated area of
any city which does not enter into an agreement with the county

three years , respectively , for the two citizen members appointed

duties as such , qualify by taking the necessary oath of office
before the county judge of such county . A citizen member may
be removed by the appointing official for inefficiency , neglect
of duty , malfeasance , or improper conduct . The appointing offi-

a recorded vote of not less than two-thirds of the entire member-
ship of the fiscal court or city legislative body shall be necessary
to overrule such disapproval . The failure of the commission to
act within ninety days after the official submission of a proposed
amendment to the commission shall be deemed approval .
Section 6. The commission shall designate and appoint an
enforcement officer who may be a member of the commission ,
who shall be charged with and provided with the authority to
enforce the ordinances , regulations , and orders of the commission
and to issue zoning permits and certificates of occupancy . The
commission may fix a reasonable schedule of fees for the issuance
of such permits and certificates .
Section 7. ( 1 ) The board of zoning adjustment and
appeals shall be composed of six members, none of whom shall
hold any other city or county office , except that one may be a
citizen member of the commission . Three of the members shall
be apointed by the city legislative body and the other three by
the fiscal court . Terms of office shall be three years , except that
of the original appointments by the city legislative body one shall
be for one year , one for two years , and one for three years , and
that of the original appointments by the fiscal court one shall
be for one year , one for two years , and one for three years . The

such county . A member may be removed for cause by the ap-

(2) The board shall elect a chairman and a vice -chairman .

CHAPTER 69 311
Section 9. ( 1 ) In the event that no city in such county
desires to avail itself of the benefits of this Act , the fiscal court of
such county is hereby authorized and empowered to regulate the
physical development , including planning and zoning , or the
unincorporated areas of such county . The intention to so regulate
by such county shall be indicated by an order passed by such
fiscal court which shall : create a county planning commission
and a county board of zoning adjustment and appeals , the func-
tions , powers and duties of which shall be as nearly as possible
comparable to and with the functions , powers and duties here-
inbefore provided for joint city-county commissions and boards ;
provide for the personnel and staff of such county commission and
board including an enforcement officer ; and provide for the
appointment , organization and financing thereof , and for appro-
priate rules , regulations and procedure .
(2) The fiscal court , in order to determine that no city in
such county desires to participate in a joint city- county planning
commission shall submit a petition of inquiry to the legislative
body of the third through sixth class city or cities , which must be
answered in writing by the city legislative body within sixty days .
If no answer to the petition is forthcoming from the city within
sixty days , the fiscal court shall interpret this as a negative reply .
(3 ) A county planning commission shall be composed of
two ex officio members, who shall be the county judge , and
either a second member of the fiscal court appointed by the fiscal
court or the county road engineer, and six citizen members
appointed by the county judge , who shall be residents of the
unincorporated areas of the county. The term of each ex officio
member shall correspond to his respective official tenure . The
terms of the citizen members shall be four years , and until their
successors take office except that the original terms shall be for
one year for one member , two years for two members , three years
for one member , and four years for two members . The county
judge shall apoint successors of citizen members within thirty
days after the expiration of their terms , or after a vacancy other-
wise occurs . The members of the commission shall , before enter-
ing upon their duties as such, qualify by taking the necessary oath
of office before the county judge of such county . A citizen mem-
ber may be removed by the appointing official for inefficiency ,

CHAPTER 72 315
CHAPTER 71
(S. B. 82)
AN ACT relating to sanitation districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The board of directors of a sanitation district may make
contracts or other arrangements to provide for the collection , dis-
posal and treatment of sewage and other liquid wastes produced
outside of the district , as the board of directors may determine ;
provided , however , that this does not authorize a sanitation dis-
trict to provide sewer service within a city of the first class , ex-
cept with permission of the board of aldermen of said city .
Approved March 21 , 1960
CHAPTER 72
(S. B. 91 )
AN ACT relating to the Thirtieth Circuit Court District.
WHEREAS , an enumeration reveals that the population of Jefferson
County is such as to justify the authorization of two additional
judges to the circuit court of the Thirtieth Circuit Court District ,
and
WHEREAS , each of the two existing Chancery Divisions of the Thirtieth
Circuit Court District has had before it the following number of
cases in each of the following years :
1954
1955
1956
1957
1958
1983 cases
1998 cases
2047 cases
2064 cases
2080 cases
WHEREAS , the Judicial Council of Kentucky has determined that the
average annual case load of each circuit court is between 650 and
700 cases per annum , although some of such courts handle as few
as 250 cases per annum , which facts show a serious need for two
additional chancery courts in the thirtieth judicial district .
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
(1) Two additional judges of the circuit court for the
Thirtieth Circuit Court District are hereby authorized and added
to said district .

316 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) Two additional divisions are added to the chancery
branch of the circuit court for the Thirtieth Circuit Court Dis-
trict, to be named the Chancery Branch Third Division and the
Chancery Branch Fourth Division, and one of the additional
judges provided for in this section shall be the judge of the
Chancery Branch Third Division and one of the additional judges
provided for in this section shall be the judge of the Chancery
Branch Fourth Division .
( 3) At the regular election to be held on the first Tuesday
after the first Monday in November , 1961 , there shall be elected
for the Thirtieth Circuit Court District , in addition to the judges
of the chancery branch provided for in KRS 24.190 , a judge of
the Chancery Branch Third Division and a judge of the Chancery
Branch Fourth Division. Such elections shall be held in accord-
ance with the provisions of KRS 24.190 .
(4) The courts in the Thirtieth Circuit Court District shall
be held and the business therein conducted in the manner now
provided by law for circuit courts having nine judges .
(5 ) This Act shall become effective on September 1, 1960 .
Approved March 21 , 1960
CHAPTER 73
( S. B. 155 )
AN ACT relating to business development corporations .
WHEREAS , the great changes that have taken place and are continuing
in the general economy of the United States have produced hard-
ship and economic distress to great areas and many citizens of the
Commonwealth ; and
WHEREAS , many people of the Commonwealth can no longer follow
their traditional way of life ; and
WHEREAS , the small independent businesses of the Commonwealth
are among the chief sufferers from this change of economy ; and
WHEREAS , the health , vigor and opportunity of the small independent
businesses are of vital importance and interest to all the people of
the Commonwealth ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. This Act shall be known as "The Business De-
velopment Corporation Act " .

CHAPTER 73 317

330 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 299.010 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The provisions of this chapter do not apply to secret
or fraternal societies , lodges , or councils that are under the
supervision of a grand or supreme body and secure members
through the lodge system exclusively , and pay no commissions
and employ no agents except in the organization and super-
vision of the work of local subordinate lodges or councils , nor
do they apply to companies , societies or associations organized
under the authority and patronage of any church or religious
denomination for the exclusive purpose of insuring the property
of churches or religious denominations and the personal property
of the pastors and ministers thereof against loss or damage by
fire , lightning or storm .
(2) As used in this chapter , unless the context requires
otherwise :
(a) "Director" or "commissioner " means the Commis-
sioner of the Department of Insurance .
( b ) "Policy" means any policy, certificate of membership ,
or contract of insurance .
( 3 ) " Company ," as used in KRS 299.020 to 299.300 , means
any corporation , association or society transacting in this state a
life or casualty insurance business , or both , upon the cooperative
or assessment plan , as defined in KRS 299.020 .
(4 ) "Company ," as used in KRS 299.310 to 299.470 , means
any corporation organized under KRS 299.310 and 299.320 for the
purpose of transacting , and any company heretofore organized
under any similar law of this state or under the general corpo-
ration laws of this state that is transacting , the business of insur-
ance against physical loss or damage of property by such hazard
or hazards as it may provide in
itspolicies
,upon the cooperative
orassessment plan
.

that set out above .
CHAPTER 76

Approved March 21 , 1960
AN ACT to promote better understanding between the races , discourage
discrimination , and to establish a Commission on Human Rights ,
designating the members thereof , defining its duties and powers ,
and making an appropriation thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. As used in this Act , discrimination shall mean
any unfair treatment based on race or national ancestry .
Section 2. There is hereby created a Commission on Human
Rights . The commission shall consist of eleven members , one
to be appointed from each Congressional district and three
from the state at large . The Governor shall appoint all mem

CHAPTER 76 333
bers , and shall name one member as chairman . Of the eleven
members first appointed , three shall serve for one year , four
for two years and four for three years . Thereafter all mem-
bers of the commission shall be appointed for three years . In
the event of the death or resignation of a member , his suc-
cessor shall be appointed to serve the unexpired term for which
such member had been appointed .

courage fair treatment for, to foster mutual understanding and
respect among and to discourage discrimination against any
racial or ethnic group or

CHAPTER 80
(Н. В. 244 )
AN ACT conferring upon counties having a population of 300,000 or
more, the right and power to levy , assess and collect license taxes
for county purposes ; and the issuance and enforcement of licenses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The fiscal court of each county having a popu-
lation of 300,000 or more , may by order or resolution impose
li-
cense fees
onfranchises
,provide for licensing any business
, trade
,
occupation
orprofession

,
orexhibiting

, article
,

onsuch business

,

be

at

orrates not
toexceed 14 per cent
of
(a)salaries
,wages
, commissions and other compensation earnedby
persons within the county for work done and services performed
orrendered
inthe county
, and
(b)the net profits
ofbusinesses
,
trades
, professions
,
oroccupations from activities conducted
in
the county
. Licenses imposed for regulatory purposes shall not

CHAPTER 80 339
be subject to such limitations as to form and amount . No com-
pany that pays an ad valorem tax and a franchise tax shall be
required to pay a license tax , and no license tax shall be imposed
upon or collected from any bank , trust company , combined bank
and trust company or combined trust , banking and title business
in this state , or in other cases where the county is prohibited by
statute from imposing a license tax .
Section 2. The fiscal court of said counties may provide
for the levy , the assessment and the collection of such license
taxes , provide for the issuance and enforcement of licenses , and
specify the county governmental purposes to which the revenue
therefrom shall be applied .
Section 3. Any amount paid to any city of the first class
within such county as a license fee, for the same privilege and
for the same period , shall be credited against the county license
fee payable hereunder . Any amount paid to any other city within
such county as a license fee , for the same privilege and for the
same period , shall be credited against the county license fee
payable hereunder , provided that such city , at least thirty (30)
days prior to the beginning of any county fiscal year , has con-
tracted with the fiscal court to contribute annually to the sup-
port of joint agencies of such county and one or more cities in
the county , an amount which bears the same ratio to the annual
appropriation made for such joint agencies by a city of the first
class in the county , as the assessed valuations for county tax
purposes , as determined by the county tax commissioner , of
the real and tangible personal property , excluding franchises ,
located within the corporate limits of such other cities , respec-
tively , bears to the same assessed valuations within a city of the
first class in said county .
Section 4. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal ,
amend, or affect in any way the provisions of KRS 243.060 .
Section 5. This Act shall not in any wise appeal , amend,
affect or apply to any existing statute exempting property from
local taxation or fixing a special rate on proper classification or
imposing a state tax which is declared to be in lieu of
alllocal
taxation
, nor shall
itbe construed
toauthorize
acounty
torequire

340 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
any company that pays both an ad valorem tax and a franchise
tax to pay a license tax .
Section 6. This Act shall not be construed as repealing
KRS 91.200 , or KRS 92.281 or any of the laws of the Common-
wealth relating to the levy of license taxes , but shall be held and
construed as ancillary and supplemental thereto .
Section 7. If any section, paragraph , clause, or provision
of this Act shall be held invalid , the invalidity of such section ,
paragraph , clause or provision shall not affect the validity of any
of the remaining provisions hereof .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 81
(Н. В. 273 )
AN ACT creating the Kentucky Public School Authority; constituting
the same an independent corporate agency and instrumentality of
the Commonwealth for the purpose of assisting boards of educa-
tion of county and independent school districts in the financing of
school projects upon request of such boards ; designating member-
ship; defining powers ; establishing procedures ; providing for co-
operation of the Department of Education ; declaring bonds of the
authority to be eligible investments for all purposes ; and prescribing
the manner of advertising public sales of bonds of the authority .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. There shall be an agency and instrumentality of
the Commonwealth for the purpose of assisting the boards of
education of any county or independent school districts , upon
request of such boards , in the financing of public school building
projects and undertakings ; and the same is hereby created and
established as a public corporation under the name Kentucky
Public School Authority, with perpetual succession , and with
power in that name to contract and be contracted with , sue and
be sued, have and use a corporate seal, and exercise the usual
powers of private corporations except as expressly limited in
this Act.

CHAPTER 82
(S. B. 102
)
AN ACT relating to higher education
.
WHEREAS
, there
is
a serious need for the creation of an extension

350 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
center of the University of Kentucky in Hardin County , for the
purpose of alleviating the serious shortage of trained teachers within
the Commonwealth , and to carry more fully into effect the provi-
sions of Section 183 of the Constitution of Kentucky , by affording
to the teachers and students of central Kentucky such training in
the common school system , in the science and art of teaching , and
in other areas as may be deemed necessary and advisable , as will
enable them to improve and render more efficient the administra-
tion of the common school system and raise the standards of in-
struction therein throughout the Commonwealth ;
WHEREAS , there are now established five off-campus centers of the
University of Kentucky at Covington , Ashland , Cumberland , Hen-
derson , and Fort Knox , and the establishment of other institutions
is under consideration ;
WHEREAS , a number of communities have indicated an interest in
having a junior college or an off-campus center located in these
communities ;
WHEREAS , Kentucky's desires for the strengthening and improving of
education must be approached in an effective and efficient manner ;
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. There shall be established and maintained an
extension center of the University of Kentucky in the city of
Elizabethtown , Kentucky , or the environs thereof , to be known as
the "Elizabethown Extension Center " . The "Elizabethtown
Extension Center " shall be maintained and operated in the same
manner as the other extension centers presently maintained by
the University of Kentucky .
Section 2. There may be appointed an advisory board for
each extension center maintained by the University of Kentucky .
The boards shall be appointed by the Governor to advise the
Board of Trustees of the University of Kentucky in the operation
of the centers .
Section 3. There is hereby created a commission of five
qualified persons appointed by the Governor to study the need
for and placement of additional universities , colleges , junior col-
leges and off-campus centers which might best serve the Com-
monwealth , the types of programs they should encompass and
the organizational pattern which would best insure their con-
tinuing contribution to the Commonwealth and the particular

CHAPTER 83 351
area served . The commission will report to the Governor on or
before July 1 , 1961 in order that sufficient planning may be com-
pleted prior to the meeting of the 1962 General Assembly .
Approved March 22, 1960

AN ACT relating to the Judicial Department of the state government ;
including provisions for judicial administration ; financial adminis-
tration and facilities of the Court of Appeals ; compensation of com-
missioners ; and a retirement system for judges and commissioners
of the Court of Appeals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
Section 1. An Administrative Office of the Courts is created,
to be headed by a director who shall be appointed by and hold
office during the pleasure of the Court of Appeals , which court
also may appoint necessary employes of the office . The salaries
of the director and employes shall be fixed by order of the Court
of Appeals and paid monthly out of the State Treasury . While

the office shall practice law in any court of this Commonwealth .
