Section 14. Unlawful representations concerning registra-
tion or exemption .
Neither the fact that an application for registration under
Section 2 or a registration statement under Sections 4, 5 or 6 has
been filed, nor the fact that a person or security is effectively
registered , constitutes a finding by the director that any docu-
ment filed under this Act is true , complete , and not misleading .
Neither any such fact nor the fact that an exemption or exceр-
tion is available for a security or a transaction means that the
director has passed in any way upon the merits or qualifications
of, or recommended or given approval to, any person , security ,
or transaction . It is unlawful to make , or cause to be made , to
any prospective purchaser , customer , or client any representa-
tion inconsistent with this section .
Section 15. Investigations and subpoenas .
(A) The director in his discretion ( 1 ) may make such
public or private investigations within or outside of this state as
he deems necessary to determine whether any registration should
be granted , denied or revoked or whether any person has vio-
lated or is about to violate any provision of this Act or any rule
or order hereunder , or to aid in the enforcement of this Act or in
the prescribing of rules and forms hereunder , ( 2 ) may require
or permit any person to file a statement in writing, under oath
or otherwise as the director may determine , as to all the facts
and circumstances concerning the matter to be investigated , and
(3 ) may publish information concerning any violation of this
Act or any rule or order hereunder .
( B ) For the purpose of any investigation or proceeding
under this Act, the director or any officer designated by him
may administer oaths and affirmations , subpoena witnesses ,
compel their attendance, take evidence , and require the pro-
duction of any books , papers , correspondence , memoranda , agree-
ments , or other documents or records which the director deems
relevant or material to the inquiry.
(C) In case of contumacy by, or refusal to obey a subpoena

CHAPTER 110 483
issued to , any person, any court of competent jurisdiction , upon
application by the director , may issue to that person an order
requiring him to appear before the director , or the officer desig-
nated by him, there to produce documentary evidence if so
ordered or to give evidence touching the matter under investi-
gation or in question ; and any failure to obey the order of the
court may be punished by the court as a contempt of court .
(D) No person is excused from attending and testifying
or from producing any document or record before the director ,
or in obedience to the subpoena of the director or any officer
designated by him, or in any proceeding instituted by the direc-
tor, on the ground that the testimony or evidence ( documentary
or otherwise ) required of him may tend to incriminate him or
subject him to a penalty or forfeiture ; but no individual may be
prosecuted or subjected to any penalty or forfeiture for or on
account of any transaction , matter , or thing concerning which
he is compelled, after claiming his privilege against self-incrimi-
nation , to testify or produce evidence ( documentary or other-
wise ) , except that the individual so testifying shall not be exempt
from prosecution and punishment for perjury committed in so
testifying .
Section 16. Injunctions .
Whenever it appears to the director that any person has
engaged or is about to engage in any act or practice constituting
a violation of any provisions of this Act or any rule or order
hereunder , he may in his discretion bring an action in any court
of competent jurisdiction to enjoin any such acts or practices
and to enforce compliance with this Act or any rule or order
hereunder . Upon a proper showing a permanent or temporary
injunction , restraining order , or writ of mandamus shall be
granted and a receiver or conservator may be appointed for the
defendant or the defendant's assets . The director may not be
required to post a bond .
Section 17. Criminal penalties .
(A) Any person who willfully violates any provision of
this Act except Section 13, or who willfully violates any rule or
order under this Act , or who willfully violates Section 13 know-
ing the statement made to be false or misleading in any material

484 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
respect , shall upon conviction be fined not more than $5,000 or
imprisoned not more than three years , or both ; but no person
may be imprisoned for the violation of any rule or order if he
proves that he had no knowledge of the rule or order . No
indictment or information may be returned under this Act more
than five years after the alleged violation .
(B) The director may refer such evidence as may be
available concerning violations of this Act or of any rule or order
hereunder to the Attorney General or the proper district attorney ,
who may in his discretion , with or without such a reference ,
institute the appropriate criminal proceedings under this Act .
( C ) Nothing in this Act limits the power of the state to
punish any person for any conduct which constitutes a crime by
statute or at common law .
Section 18. Civil liabilities .
(A) Any person , who offers or sells a security in violation
of Section 3 or 4 or offers or sells a security by means of any
untrue statement of a material fact or any omission to state a
material fact necessary in order to make the statements made
in the light of the circumstances under which they are made not
misleading ( the buyer not knowing of the untruth or omission )
and who does not sustain the burden of proof that he did not
know and in the exercise of reasonable care could not have
known of the untruth or omission , is liable to the person buying
the security from him , who may sue either at law or in equity
to recover the consideration paid for the security , together with
interest at six per cent per annum from the date of payment ,
costs , and reasonable attorneys' fees , less the amount of any
income received on the security, upon the tender of the security ,
or for damages if he no longer owns the security . Damages are
the amount that would be recoverable upon a tender less ( 1 )
the value of the security when the buyer is disposed of it and
( 2 ) interest at

and to be adduced upon the hearing in such manner and upon
such conditions as the court may consider proper . The director
may modify his findings as the facts , by reason of the additional
evidence so taken ; and he shall file any modified or new findings ,
which if supported by substantial evidence shall be conclusive ,
and any recommendation for the modification or setting aside
of the original order . The commencement of proceedings un-
der this action does not , unless specifically ordered by the court ,
operate as a stay of the director's order . An appeal may be
taken from the judgment of the Franklin Circuit Court on any
such appeal to the Court of Appeals on the same terms and con-
ditions as an appeal is taken in civil actions .
Section 20. Administration of Act .
(A) The administration of the provisions of this Act shall
be under the Division of Securities in the Department of Banking .
(B) It is unlawful for the director or any of his officers
or employes to use for personal benefit any information which
is filed with or obtained by the director and which is not made
public. No provision of this Act authorizes the director or any of
his officers or employes to disclose any such information except
among themselves or when necessary or appropriate in a pro-
ceeding or investigation under this Act . No provision of this
Act either creates or derogates from any privilege which exists
at common law or otherwise when documentary or other evidence
is sought under a subpoena directed to the director or any of
his officers or employes .
( C ) The director may from time to time make , amend ,
and rescind such rules and forms as are necessary to carry out

CHAPTER 110 487
the provisions of this Act . No rule or form may be made unless
the director finds that the action is necessary or appropriate in
the public interest or for the protection of investors and con-
sistent with the purposes fairly intended by the policy and
provisions of this Act . In prescribing rules and forms the direc-
tor may cooperate with the securities administrators of the other
states and the Securities and Exchange Commission with a view
to effectuating the policy of this statute to achieve maximum
uniformity in the form and content of registration statements ,
applications , and reports wherever practicable . All rules and
forms of the director shall be published .
(D) No provision of this Act imposing any liability applies
to any act done or omitted in good faith in conformity with any
rule , form , or order of the director , notwithstanding that the
rule or form may later be amended or rescinded or be determined
by judicial or other authority to be invalid for any reason .

CHAPTER 111

(S. B. 266 )
AN ACT relating to unemployment insurance .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 341.070 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , unless the context clearly requires
otherwise , “subject employer " means :
(1) Any employing unit which, in each of three calendar
quarters in the preceding calendar year , had in covered employ-
ment four or more workers ( whether or not the same workers ) ,
to as many as four of whom at least fifty dollars in wages was
payable to each with respect to such covered employment in
each such quarter .
(2) Any employing unit which for some portion of a day
( but not necessarily simultaneously ) in each of twenty different
weeks ( whether or not such weeks are consecutive ) within
either the current or the preceding calendar year , has or had
in covered employment four or more workers ( whether the
same workers are or were employed in each such day) .
(3) Any employing unit that succeeds to the organization ,
trade or business of another employing unit which at the time
of such succession is a subject employer ; and any employing
unit which succeeds to the organization , trade , or business of
any previously separate portion of another employing unit,
which portion , if treated as a separate employing unit, would
be , at the time of the succession , a subject employer under sub-
sections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section .

CHAPTER 111 491
(4) Any employing unit that succeeds to any of the
assets of a subject employer's business by any means whatever ,
otherwise than in the ordinary course of trade , is a subject
employer if :
(a) the transferee has continued or resumed the business
of the transferor , either in the same establishment or elsewhere ;
and
(b ) the transferee has employed substantially the same
workers as those the transferor had employed in connection
with the assets acquired ; and
( c) the transferee has acquired a previously separate
portion of the transferor's assets , which portion , if treated as
a separate employing unit , would be, at the time of the succes-
sion, a subject employer under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this
section .
(5) Any other employing unit which has elected to be-
come subject to this chapter , pursuant to subsection ( 3 ) of
KRS 341.250 .
(6) Any employing unit which has in covered employ-
ment within this state workers subject to the Federal Unem-
ployment Tax Act .
(7) Any employing unit which acquires the organization ,
trade , or business , or substantially all of the assets thereof , of
another employing unit, if the covered employment of such
employing unit subsequent to such acquisition , together with
the covered employment of the acquired unit prior to such
acquisition , both within the same calendar year , would be suf-
ficient to constitute such employing unit a subject employer
under the provisions of subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section .
For the purpose of KRS 341.260 wages paid by the acquired
unit shall be considered as having been paid by the acquiring
unit .
(8) Any employing unit which, together with one or
more other employing units , is owned or controlled ( by legally
enforceable means or otherwise ) directly or indirectly by the
same interests , or which owns or controls ( by legally enforce-
able means or otherwise ) one or more other employing units ,
and which , if treated as a single employing unit with such other

492 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
employing units or interests , or both , would be a subject em-
ployer under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section .
(9) Any employing unit which, having become a subject
employer under subsections ( 1 ) to ( 8 ) of this section , has not
ceased to be a subject employer under KRS 341.250 .
( 10 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of this section or any
other provision of this chapter , no employing unit shall be
initially determined a subject employer on the basis of covered
employment performed more than six calendar years prior to the
year in which such determination is made , unless the com-
missioner can show that the records of such employment experi-
ence were fraudulently concealed or withheld for the purpose of
escaping liability under this chapter .

AN ACT relating
tothe Board
ofTrustees of the University of Kentucky
,
amending Sections 164.130 and 164.170 of the Kentucky Revised

, and declaring an emergency
.

494 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 164.130 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The government of the University of Kentucky is
vested in a board of trustees consisting of the Governor , who
shall be chairman of the board , the Superintendent of Public
Instruction , the Commissioner of Agriculture , Labor and Sta-
tistics , twelve competent citizens of Kentucky appointed by the
Governor , and two non -voting members of the teaching faculty
of the University of Kentucky . The terms of appointed members
shall be for four years and until their successors are appointed
and qualify . Three of the twelve appointed members shall be
appointed each year . Three of the appointed members shall be
representative of agricultural interests, three shall be alumni of
the University and six shall be other distinguished citizens of the
state representative of the learned professions . The Governor
shall so make the appointments as to divide the representation
upon the board , including the ex officio members , as nearly
equally as possible between the two leading political parties of
this state . Appointments to fill vacancies shall be made for the
unexpired term in the same manner as provided for original ap-
pointments .
( 2) The non -voting faculty members shall be teaching or
research members of the faculty of the University of Kentucky
of the rank of assistant professor or above . They shall be elected
by secret ballot by all faculty members of the university of the
rank of assistant professor and above . Faculty members shall
serve for terms of three years and until their successors are
elected and qualify, except that of the first two members elected
one shall serve for a term of two years and one for a term of
three years . Faculty members shall be eligible for re-election ,
but they shall be ineligible to continue to serve as members of
the board of trustees if they cease to be members of the teaching
staff of the university . Elections to fill vacancies shall be for
the unexpired term in the same manner as provided for original
elections .
(3 ) No member of the board of trustees or
itsadminis-
trative staff shall
bedirectly
orindirectly interested
inany

CHAPTER 113 495
contract with the university for the sale of property , materials ,
supplies , equipment or services , with exception of compensation
to the two faculty members .
Section 2. Section 164.170 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The board of trustees of the university shall meet
on Tuesday preceding the regular annual commencement of the
university , on the third Tuesday in September , on the second
Tuesday in December , and on the first Tuesday in April of each
year . Special meetings may be called by the chairman or by
any three members upon giving ten days' written notice to each
member of the board of trustees . The business to be trans-
acted at special meetings shall be specified in the notice of the
meeting . All meetings shall be held on the campus of the uni-
versity unless otherwise specified by a majority vote of the
board of trustees . A majority of the voting members of the
board of trustees shall constitute a quorum . All necessary ex-
penses incurred by the trustees in going to , returning from and
while attending meetings of the board of trustees shall be paid
out of the funds of the university .
( 2 ) The meetings of the board of trustees shall be open
to the public . The board of trustees shall cause a stenographic
transcript of each of
itsmeetings
tobe prepared and filed
. An
agenda for each
ofthe meetings shall
besent
toeach member
ofthe board
oftrustees and
acopy thereof made available
to
the press
.
Section
3. An emergency
ishereby declared
toexist and
this Act shall become effective when passed and approved
.

CHAPTER 113

)
AN ACT relating
tointerstate cooperation in the field of nuclear energy
.
Be
itenacted by the General Assembly
ofthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky
:
Section
1. As used
inthis Act
, unless the context requires
otherwise
:

496 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1) "Compact " means the Southern Interstate Nuclear
Compact ;
( 2 ) " Board " means the Southern Interstate Nuclear Board .
Section 2. The Southern Interstate Nuclear Compact is
enacted into law and entered into by the Commonwealth of
Kentucky as a party , and is of full force and effect between the
Commonwealth and any other states joining therein in ac-
cordance with the terms of the compact . The compact is sub-
stantially as follows :
Article I. Policy and Purpose
The party states recognize that the proper employment of
nuclear energy , facilities , materials , and products can assist sub-
stantially in the industrialization of the South and the develop-
ment of a balanced economy for the region . They also recognize
that optimum benefit from the acquisition of nuclear resources
and facilities requires systematic encouragement , guidance , and
assistance from the party states on a cooperative basis . It is the
policy of the party states to undertake such cooperation on a con-
tinuing basis ; it is the purpose of this compact to provide the
instruments and framework for such a cooperative effort to im-
prove the economy of the South and contribute to the individual
and community well being of the region's people .
Article II . The Board
( a ) There is hereby created an agency of the party states
to be known as the "Southern Interstate Nuclear Board " ( here-
inafter called the board ) . The board shall be composed of one
member from each party state designated or appointed in ac-
cordance with the law of the state which he represents and serv-
ing and subject to removal in accordance with such law. Any
member of the board may provide for the discharge of his duties
and the performance of his functions thereon ( either for the
duration of his membership or for any lesser period of time ) by
a deputy or assistant , if the laws of his state make specific pro-
vision therefor . The federal government may be represented
without vote if provision is made by federal law for such repre
sentation .
(b ) The board members of the party states shall each be
entitled to one vote on the board . No action of the board shall

Section 7. The members of the board shall not receive
compensation for their services , but shall be reimbursed for their
actual expenses necessarily incurred in the performance of their
duties , upon vouchers duly approved by the board , signed by
the secretary and countersigned by the president .
Section 8. ( 1 ) The board shall establish , equip and mair-
tain libraries or contract with existing libraries for the furnishing
of library service for the district and do all things necessary to
provide efficient library service . No district shall establish a
library unless the plans for the establishment , equipment and
maintenance have been approved by the Library Extension Divi-
sion . No contract shall be made unless the libraries contracting
to furnish service are libraries approved by the Library Extension
Division for this purpose . The board shall meet annually and at
such other times as it deems necessary .
( 2 ) The district , as a body corporate , by and through the
board may:
( a ) Sue and be sued, complain and defend , purchase , or
lease grounds , purchase , lease , occupy or erect appropriate build-
ings for the use of the district libraries and their branches , sell
and convey real and personal property for and on behalf of
the district , receive gifts of real and personal property for the
use and benefit of the district , the same when accepted to be held
and controlled by the board according to the terms of the deed ,
gift , devise or bequest of such property .
(b ) Borrow money on the credit of the board in anticipa-
tion of the revenue to be derived from taxes levied by the district
for the fiscal year in which the money is borrowed , and to
pledge the taxes levied for the district for the payment of the
principal and interest of the loan . The interest paid shall not
exceed six per cent per annum and the principal shall not exceed
fifty per cent of the anticipated revenue for the fiscal year in
which the money is borrowed .
( c ) Establish bylaws it deems necessary and expedient
to define the duties of officers , assistants or employes and make
allnecessary rules and regulations governing libraries and library
service within the district

CHAPTER 115
(S. B. 284)
AN ACT relating to nuclear energy .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 152.110 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 152.105 to 152.190 , unless the context other-
wise requires :
( 1 ) "Atomic energy " means
allforms
ofenergy released
in the course of nuclear fission or nuclear transformation
.
(
2)
"By
-product material
”means any radioactive material
,
except special nuclear materials
, yielded
in
ormade radioactive

512 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by exposure to the radiation incident to the process of pro-
ducing or utilizing nuclear materials .
( 3 ) "Production facility ” means ( a) any equipment or
device capable of the production of special nuclear material in
such quantity as to be of significance to the common defense and
security , or in such manner as to affect the health and safety
of the public ; or ( b ) any important component part especially
designed for such equipment or device as determined by the
United States Atomic Energy Commission .
(4) "Special nuclear material " means ( a) plutonium and
uranium enriched in the isotope 233 or in the isotope 235 and
any other material after the United States Atomic Energy Com-
mission has determined the material to be such, or (b) any
material artifically enriched by any of the foregoing .
( 5 ) "Utilization facility" means ( a) any equipment or
device , except an atomic weapon , capable of making use of
special nuclear materials in such quantity as to be of significance
to the common defense and security , or in such manner as to af-
fect the health and safety of the public, or peculiarly adapted
for making use of atomic energy in such quantity as to be of
significance to the common defense and security , or in such
manner as to affect the health and safety of the public , or (b)
any important component part especially designed for such
equipment or device as determined by the United States Atomic
Energy Commission .
Section 2. Section 152.130 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) All rules and regulations , except emergency regulations
adopted pursuant to Chapter 13 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
which are approved by the Governor , proposed in accordance
with KRS 152.120 , shall be submitted to the Governor and to the
Advisory Committee on Nuclear Energy , not less than sixty days
prior to the filing of such rules and regulations with the Legisla-
tive Research Commission . Within such sixty -day period and after
consultation with the advisory committee and the holding of any
public hearings which may be deemed advisable , the Governor
may determine that any such proposed rules and regulations , or
parts thereof , are not in accord with the policy of this chapter and

CHAPTER 115 513
may issue an order to that effect . In such event any such pro-
posed rules and regulations , or parts thereof , shall not become
effective . The Governor may within sixty days after the
effective date of this Act and after a similar determination , de-
clare null and void any or all rules and regulations issued in ac-
cordance with KRS 152.120 prior to such effective date .
( 2 ) The Governor may delegate to any state official the
power to perform the functions of the Governor as set out in
subsection ( 1 ) and the actions of such official with respect to
regulations of any state agency proposed under KRS 152.120 shall
be valid as if done by the Governor .
Section 3. Section 152.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) There shall be a committee , attached to the Depart-
ment of Economic Development for administrative purposes , to
be known as the Advisory Committee on Nuclear Energy to
advise the Governor on atomic eneregy developments and regula-
tions within the Commonwealth and to perform other functions
set forth in this chapter . The committee shall consist of not
more than seventeen members and shall include persons
from private life and representative of state governmental
activities concerned with nuclear energy as determined by the
Governor . The Governor shall appoint the members of the com-
mittee , who shall be residents of the Commonwealth , and
its
chairman
;
allmembers shall serve
atthe pleasure
ofthe Gover-
nor
.
(2) The Director
ofthe Division
ofNuclear Information
,
Department
ofEconomic Development
, shall
be
exofficio the
executive secretary
ofthe committee
, and shall serve
asco-
ordinator and provide administrative services for accomplishing
through the Department
ofEconomic Development
, the adopted
directives of the committee
.
(3) The members
ofthe committee shall receive
nosalary
for services performed
onsuch committee
, but may
bereimbursed
for necessary and actual travel expenses incurred
inconnection
with attendance
atofficial meetings
ofthe committee
orsub-
committees thereof
.

514 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
The Governor of the Commonwealth shall have power and
authority to enter into agreements with the United States
Atomic Energy Commission or other appropriate federal agency
or authority regarding any matter the subject of KRS 152.105 to
152.190 .
Section 5. Section 152.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Any person who violates any of the provisions of KRS
152.105 to 152.190 or any regulation of a state agency pro-
mulgated in accordance with KRS 152.120 and 152.130 shall be
fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more than five
thousand dollars , or imprisoned in the county jail not less than ten
days nor more than six months , or both fined and imprisoned .
For the purposes of this subsection , each day of violation shall
be deemed a separate offense .
( 2 ) Whenever , in the opinion of the Attorney General ,
any person is violating or is about to violate any of the provisions
of KRS 152.105 to 152.190 , or is violating or is about to violate
any of the rules and regulations promulgated under KRS 152.105
to 152.190 , the Attorney General may apply to the appropriate
court for an order enjoining the person from engaging or con-
tinuing to engage in the violative act, and upon a showing that
such person has engaged , or is about to engage in such activity ,
a restraining order or permanent or temporary injunction , or any
other appropriate order may be granted .
Section 6. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
The department and agencies named in KRS 152.120 may in
their discretion provide by regulation for the licensing , insofar as
relates to their particular fields of regulatory activity , of the
possession or use of any source of ionizing radiation and the
handling and disposal of radioactive wastes . Such regulations
may specify the form of application for license , the qualifications
therefor , grounds for revocation of same by the issuing agency,
and such other matters as may be needed to make licensing effec

CHAPTER 116 515
tive as a means of control . Promulgation of such regulations shall
be in accordance with the provisions of KRS 152.130 .
Section 7. Whereas the federal government by Public
Law 86-373 , enacted in 1959 , provided for the transfer of vari-
ous atomic energy regulatory functions to the states and , whereas ,
it is provided that the Governor of the Commonwealth and the
representative of the federal government shall negotiate an
agreement to effect such transfers and, whereas it is essential
to effect uniformity in the transfer and administration of such
functions , an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become effective upon
itspassage and approval by the Gov-
ernor
.
Approved March 25
, 1960

CHAPTER 119
(Н. В. 124)
AN ACT concerning service of process on nonresident motorists and
service of process on motorists resident in the state who after the
happening of the accident become nonresident .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 188.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is re -enacted to read as follows :
Any nonresident operator or owner of any motor vehicle
who accepts the privilege extended by the laws of this state to
nonresidents to operate motor vehicles or have them operated
within state shall, by such acceptance and by the operation of
such motor vehicle within this state , make the Secretary of State
the agent of himself or his personal representative for the service
of process in any civil action instituted in the courts of this state
against the operator or owner , or the personal representative of
the operator or owner , arising out of or by reason of any accident
or collision or damage occurring within this state in which the
motor vehicle is involved .
Section 2. Section 188.070 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is re - enacted to read as follows :

CHAPTER 120 519
The provisions of KRS 188.010 to 188.060 shall apply to any
operator or owner of any motor vehicle who is a resident of this
state at the time any accident or collision or damage occurs and
who thereafter becomes a nonresident .
Approved March 25 , 1960
CHAPTER 120
(Н. В. 169)
AN ACT relating to milk and cream .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) "Buyer " shall include any person , partnership , corpora-
tion , association , organization or concern who receives milk or
cream from producers and pays for it on basis of amount , weight ,
or test, except that it does not include persons who buy milk or
cream for their own consumption ;
(2 ) “Buy” , “buying ", or "bought " shall mean the purchase
of milk or cream from producers or their agents on basis of
amount , weight or test;
(3 ) "Milk tester" shall include any person who tests milk
or cream , bought by buyers from producers , to determine the
constituents or quality of such milk or cream and uses , or sub-
mit for use , these tests in determining the price paid for it;
( 4 ) "Sampler and weigher " shall include any person who
samples , weighs , or measures milk or cream purchased by buy-
ers from producers ;
(5) “License to buy" means the license issued to buyers
of milk or cream purchased from producers or their agents on
basis of
itsvolume
, weight
ortest
;
(6)
"License
totest
" means the license issued
tomilk and
cream testers
;
(
7)
"License
tosample and weigh
"means the license
is-
sued
tosamplers and weighers
;
(8)
"Board
"means the examining board
;

520 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 9 ) “Director ” means the Director of the Agricultural Ex-
periment Station , College of Agriculture and Home Economics ,
University of Kentucky ;
( 10 ) "Producer " means any person , partnership , corpora-
tion , association , organization , or concern keeping milk cows for
production of milk for sale or separation for the sale of cream ;
( 11 ) "Buying location " means each separate business
place where non -packaged milk or cream is purchased , such as
cream buying station , milk receiving station , milk plant , cream-
ery , cheese factory , condensery , or ice cream factory ;
( 12) "Stamped testware " means test bottles , test weights ,
and other test equipment inspected and stamped with an in-
delible and distinguishing mark by the experiment station ;
( 13) "Standard testware " means test bottles , test weights ,
pipettes and other measuring or calibrating devices approved by
the director for use in Kentucky ;
( 14) “ Cream station " means a place where cream is re-
ceived , weighed , sampled , consolidated and shipped to a cream-
ery or factory ;
( 15) "Milk equivalent " means the amount of whole milk
required to make other dairy products ; thus , to convert butterfats
to milk equivalent , multiply the pounds of butterfat by 25, for
4 per cent milk.
Section 2. It shall be unlawful for any buyer of milk or
cream in this state to :
( 1 ) Buy milk or cream from producers or their agents
without obtaining a license for each buying location where such
milk or cream is received ;
( 2) Receive and pay for milk or cream on basis of weights
or tests without having it sampled and weighed or measured by
a licensed weigher and sampler or tested by a licensed tester ;
( 3) Manipulate weights , measurements or tests ; that is , to
pay for milk or cream on basis of any weight or measurement
or test that is not the true and legal weight or test;
(4) Manipulate or falsify records or to use records of
weights or tests as basis for payment that are not correct and
legal weights , measurements or tests ;

CHAPTER 120 521
(5 ) Receive milk or cream purchased from producers by
a cream station operator or milk receiving station that does not
have a license to buy ;
(6) Use weighing , mixing , sampling , or testing equipment
which is inaccurate or non - standard ;
(7) Fail to comply with such regulations for weighing ,
measuring , sampling and testing milk or cream as may be issued
by the director .
Section 3. Except as provided in subsection ( 4) of this
section , it shall be unlawful for any milk sampler or weigher in
this state to :
( 1 ) Sample , weigh , or measure milk or cream without ob-
taining a license to sample and weigh ;
( 2 ) Inaccurately sample, weigh , or measure milk or cream ;
( 3) Fail to comply with such regulations for the sampling ,
weighing or measuring of milk or cream as may be issued by the
director ;
(4) Provided , however , that any milk tester who holds a
license to test or temporary permit to test may sample, weigh
and measure milk or cream according to the provisions of this
Act without obtaining a license to sample and weigh .
Section 4. It shall be unlawful for any milk tester in this
state to :
( 1 ) Weigh , measure, sample or test milk or cream bought
from producers on basis of such measures , weights or tests with-
out obtaining a license or permit to test ;
(2 ) Inaccurately weigh , measure, sample , or test milk or
cream;
( 3 ) Use inaccurate unstamped or non -standard equipment
in weighing , measuring , sampling or testing , of milk or cream.
Section 5. Every creamery , milk shipping station , milk fac-
tory , cheese factory , condensery , ice cream factory or other milk
processing or manufacturing plant or person , or agent, firm , com-
pany , association or corporation buying milk or cream from pro-
ducers or their agents shall secure and hold a license to buy for
each buying location where such milk and cream is received .
Application for a license shall be made to the director , the ap

522 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
plication to be accompanied by the license fee provided for in
Section 9 of this Act . Upon receipt of the application and fee ,
the director shall issue to the buyer a license to buy milk or
cream provided the buyer is in compliance with the provisions
of this Act and the regulations relating to the sampling , weigh-
ing , testing or buying of milk or cream as may be issued by the
director ; the license to expire on June 30 of the license year for
which it is issued .
Section 6. ( 1 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of
this section , all persons who test milk or cream bought by buy-
ers from producers shall secure and hold a license to test.
(2 ) Any person who tests milk or cream bought by buyers
from producers not holding a current license to test shall dem-
onstrate his proficiency in testing by satisfactorily passing an
examination prescribed by the director . Prior to taking an ex-
amination for a license , he may apply for and secure a temporary
permit under one of the following conditions :
( a ) submit evidence of having successfully completed a
recognized course in dairying ;
(b ) has held a valid tester's license in another state ;
( c) has worked with a licensed tester at least two weeks
and has a certification from this licensed tester that he is quali-
fied to test;
( d ) has a certificate signed by a field representative of a
cream company stating that the applicant is a cream station
operator and has been properly instructed in testing procedures
and is competent in performing milk or cream tests .
( 3 ) Application for a license or temporary permit to test
shall be on a form prescribed by the director and accompanied
by the license fee or permit fee provided for in Section 9 of the
Act .
Section 7. ( 1 ) All persons who weigh , measure or sam-
ple milk or cream bought by buyers from producers shall secure
and hold a license to sample and weigh ; provided , however , that
licensed testers may sample , measure , and weigh under the au-
thority of their license to test . Application for such license shall
be made on a form prescribed by the director and accompanied
by the license fee provided for in Section 9 of this Act . Upon

CHAPTER 120 523
receipt of the application and fee , the director shall issue to the
sampler and weigher a permit to sample and weigh , then after
investigations show him qualified , a license to sample and weigh
milk and cream, provided that the sampler and weigher is in
compliance with the provisions of this Act and such regulations
relating to weighing , measuring , and sampling of milk and
cream as may be issued by the director ; the license to expire on
June 30 of the license year for which it is issued .
( 2 ) All persons who transfer milk from farm bulk tanks
to bulk tank trucks for transporting to a buying location shall
secure and hold a license to sample and weigh .
Section 8. Test equipment including test bottles , pipettes ,
weights , scales and other measuring or calibrating devices used
in testing milk and cream under the provisions of this Act which
are calibrated or adjusted so as to determine the results or ac-
curacy of results of such test shall be inspected by the experi-
ment station . Equipment found accurate shall be stamped,
sealed or permanently marked so as to be distinguishable . The
charge for initial inspection of test equipment shall be as pre-
scribed by the director but not to exceed three cents per piece .
Equipment to be stamped for use in Kentucky shall be limited
to those items listed and described on an approved list published
periodically by the director .

( 1) No person shall turn a vehicle from a direct course
upon a highway unless and until such movement can be made
with reasonable safety , and only after a clearly audible signal has
been given by sounding the horn if any pedestrian shall be
affected by the movement. No person shall so turn any vehicle

provided in the event any other vehicle may be affected by such
movement .

given continuously for not less than the last one hundred feet
traveled by the vehicle before turning .
(3 ) No person shall stop or suddenly decrease the speed

manner provided herein to the operator of any vehicle immedi-
ately in the rear when there is opportunity to give such signal .
(4) All signals required herein shall be given either by

mechanical signal device , except that the operator of a motor
vehicle used for the transportation of persons for hire with taxi-
cabs excluded , the operator of a school or church bus , and the

and arm signal would not be visible both to the front and rear
of such vehicle shall indicate an intention to turn by use of a
signal lamp or lamps or mechanical signal device and must indi-
cate an intention to stop or suddenly decrease speed by illumi-
nating at least one red or yellow light with the word " Stop" on
it on the rear of the vehicle .

( a) left turn -

hand and arm extended horizontally ;
hand and arm extended upward ;

utes is amended to read as follows :
Operators of motor vehicles transporting children to or from
school or church shall stop their motor vehicles before crossing
over any steam or electric interurban railroad main tracks at a
grade . The stop shall be made at not less than ten feet nor more
than thirty feet from the nearest railroad over which the highway
crosses , except where the crossing is protected by gates or a flag-
man employed by the railroad . After making the stop, the oper-
ator shall carefully look in each direction for approaching cars or
trains and shall not start his vehicle until it is ascertained that
no cars or trains are approaching in either direction .

CHAPTER 125
(Н. В. 254 )
AN ACT relating to the bag limit and possession of game birds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 150.340 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) No person shall take more wild birds in any one day
than the bag limit prescribed for the species , but the department
shall not prescribe bag limits in excess of the following :

CHAPTER 125 535
Quail 10
Ruffed Grouse or Native Pheasant 4
Doves Federal Regulations Apply
Ducks Federal Regulations Apply
Geese Federal Regulations Apply

(2) No person shall have in his possession at any time any

as set by regulation for any one day for such birds , and a person

CHAPTER 126
(Н. В. 255 )
AN ACT relating to traps and other devices used for the purpose of
taking wild animals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 150.400 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) No person shall set , use or maintain , for the purpose
of taking wild animals , any steel trap unless the size and type
of the trap has been approved by the commissioner and the
commission by regulation .
( 2 ) The commissioner may approve , by regulation , any
commercially manufactured trap which is designed to take ani-
mals alive and unhurt or to kill instantly .
(3) Subject to the provisions of KRS 150.410 it shall be
lawful to use snares , deadfalls , wire cage or box traps , but no

536 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
person shall set , use or maintain a snare large enough to take
deer , elk or bear .
(4) Any manufacturer designing a new trap may send a
sample to the commissioner for approval or disapproval .

AN ACT relating to voting machines , and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , it is deemed essential to eliminate the antiquated manner
in which elections are now being conducted in many parts of the
state where voting machines are not used, and to provide a public
project and plan for the purchase of such machines by the various
counties under a rent -purchase , lease - option contract with a state
agency, so that they will be available for use while being paid for
through annual rentals by said counties ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,

of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 128 539
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context otherwise
requires :
(1) "Commission " means the State Property and Buildings
Commission , or , if said commission shall be abolished , the depart-
ment , board , body or commission succeeding to the principal
functions thereof , or to whom the powers given by this Act shall
be given by law.
(2) "Project , " or the words , "voting machine project , "
mean a public project as defined in KRS Chapter 58.
(3) " Bonds , " or the words , "voting machine first mortgage
revenue bonds ," mean Commonwealth of Kentucky , State Prop-
erty and Buildings Commission , voting machine first mortgage
revenue bonds issued in accordance with the provisions of this
Act.
(4 ) "Sinking fund " means a fund to be set aside and held
separate and apart from all other funds of the State or of the
commission , into which the income and revenues of a voting
machine project , including rents , royalties , fees and proceeds of
sale of voting machines , are to be deposited and earmarked to
meet the principal and interest requirements on any bonds issued
under this Act.
(5) "Sinking fund reserve " means a fund to be set aside
and held separate and apart from all other funds of the State
or of the commission , to be held as a reserve against any default
in principal or interest payments on such bonds , and to be ear-
marked , to the extent not otherwise used, to payment of require-
ments on such bonds during the latest years of maturities thereof ,
as may be specified in the instruments provided for in this Act .
(6) "Cost" means the cost of purchase of voting machines ,
any advertising expenses in connection therewith , the cost of
freight and transportation , the cost of preparing , issuing , selling
or exchanging first mortgage revenue bonds as provided for in

all

CHAPTER 132
( Н. В. 331 )
AN ACT relating to the Kentucky Employes ' Retirement System .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
A new section of KRS 61.510 to 61.700 is created to read as
follows :

CHAPTER 133 555
The effective date of participation of the circuit clerk's de-
partment in the Kentucky Employes ' Retirement System is de-
clared to be July 1, 1956. Both employer and members shall make
contributions , and members receive benefits , based on that date .
Approved March 25, 1960

CHAPTER 134
(Н. В. 159 )
AN ACT relating to the Legislative Research Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 7.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) There is hereby created a Legislative Research Com-
mission composed of the Lieutenant Governor , the President Pro
Tem of the Senate , the Speaker of the House of Representatives ,
the majority and minority Floor Leaders of the Senate and House
of Representatives , and the majority and minority Caucus Chair-
men of the Senate and House of Representatives . Any vacancy
in the commission shall be filled by the remaining members
thereof , provided , if the vacancy is in the membership of the
House of Representatives then the successor shall be from the
House , and if the vacancy is from the Senate membership of the
commission then the successor shall be from the Senate . A mem-
ber thus elected to fill any vacancy shall hold office for the un-
expired term of his predecessor . The Lieutenant Governor shall
be ex officio chairman of the commission .
(2) The Legislative Research Commission shall meet dur-
ing regular and special sessions of the General Assembly , and
during the intervals between sessions at such times and places as
the chairman may determine . Meetings of the commission shall
be called by the chairman on his own initiative , or at the written
request of any three members of the commission . For attend-
ing meetings between sessions , whether regular or special , of the
General Assembly , the members of the commission , or any sub-
committee thereof , shall be paid their necessary traveling ex-
penses and in addition thereto twenty -five dollars per day .

558 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 3 ) The commission shall appoint a director , who shall
have had graduate training in government in a recognized uni-
versity or college or practical experience in governmental admin-
istration , and who shall hold office at the pleasure of the com-
mission . The salary of the director shall be determined by the
commission . The commission shall have exclusive jurisdiction
over the employment of such personnel as may be necessary to
effectuate the provisions of KRS 7.090 to 7.110 .
(4 ) Any professional , clerical or other employes required
by any committee appointed by the General Assembly shall be
provided to the committee by the Legislative Research Com-
mission . The chairman of the committee shall advise the director
of the Legislative Research Commission of his need for such per-
sonnel . In the event that the personnel required by any com-
mittee cannot be met by the staff of the Legislative Research
Commission , the director shall employ such personnel as are
necessary to meet the needs of the committee , and shall fix the
rate of compensation of such employes .
( 5 ) The director shall , at the discretion of the commis-
sion and under
itssupervision and control
, provide for the allo-

ofthe work and activities

AN ACT relating
toextradition of fugitives from justice
.

CHAPTER 135 559
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. This Act may be cited as the Uniform Criminal
Extradition Act .
Section 2. Where appearing in this Act , the term “ Gov-
ernor " includes any person performing the functions of Governor
by authority of the law of this state . The term “ executive au-
thority " includes the Governor , and any person performing the
functions of Governor in a state other than this state , and the
term " state , " referring to a state other than this state , includes
any other state or territory , organized or unorganized , of the
United States of America .
Section 3. Subject to the provisions of this Act , the pro-
visions of the Constitution of the United States controlling , and
any and all acts of Congress enacted in pursuance thereof , it is
the duty of the Governor of this state to have arrested and de-
livered up to the executive authority of any other state of the
United States any person charged in that state with treason, fel-
ony, or other crime, who has fled from justice and is found in
this state .
Section 4. No demand for the extradition of a person
charged with crime in another state shall be recognized by the
Governor unless in writing alleging , except in cases arising under
Section 7, that the accused was present in the demanding state
at the time of the commission of the alleged crime , and that there-
after he fled from the state , and accompanied by a copy of an in-
dictment found or by information supported by affidavit in the
state having jurisdiction of the crime , or by a copy of an affidavit
made before a magistrate there, together with a copy of any
warrant which was issued thereupon ; or by a copy of a judg-
ment of conviction or of a sentence imposed in execution thereof ,
together with a statement by the executive authority of the de-
manding state that the person claimed has escaped from confine-
ment or has broken the terms of his bail, probation or parole.
The indictment , information , or affidavit made before the magis-
trate must substantially charge the person demanded with having
committed a crime under the law of that state ; and the copy of
indictment , information , affidavit , judgment of conviction or

560 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sentence must be authenticated by the executive authority mak-
ing the demand .
Section 5. When a demand shall be made upon the Gov-
ernor of this state by the executive authority of another state
for the surrender of a person so charged with crime , the Governor
may call upon the Attorney General or any other officer of this
state to investigate or assist in investigating the demand , and
to report to him the situation and circumstances of the person
so demanded , and whether he ought to be surrendered .
Section 6. ( 1 ) When it is desired to have returned to this
state a person charged in this state with a crime , and such person
is imprisoned or is held under criminal proceedings then pend-
ing against him in another state , the Governor of this state may
agree with the executive authority of such other state for the ex-
tradition of such person before the conclusion of such proceed-
ings or his term of sentence in such other state , upon condition
that such person be returned to such other state at the expense
of this state as soon as the prosecution in this state is terminated .
(2) The Governor of this state may also surrender on de-
mand of the executive authority of any other state any person
in this state who is charged in the manner provided in Section 4
of this Act with having violated the laws of the state whose
executive authority is making the demand , even though such
person left the demanding state involuntarily .
Section 7. The Governor of this state may also surrender ,
on demand of the executive authority of any other state , any
person in this state charged in such other state in the manner
provided in Section 4 with committing an act in this state , or
in a third state , intentionally resulting in a crime in the state
whose executive authority is making the demand , and the pro-
visions of this Act not otherwise inconsistent , shall apply to such
cases , even though the accused was not in that state at the time of
the commission of the crime , and has not fled therefrom .
Section 8. If the Governor decides that the demand should
be complied with , he shall sign a warrant of arrest, which shall
be sealed with the state seal , and be directed to any peace of-
ficer or other person whom he may think
fit
toentrust with the

CHAPTER 135 561
execution thereof . The warrant must substantially recite the
facts necessary to the validity of
itsissuance
.

Section 13. (1 ) The officer or persons executing the Gov-
ernor's warrant of arrest, or the agent of the demanding state
to whom the prisoner may have been delivered may , when
necessary , confine the prisoner in the jail of any county or city
through which he may pass; and the keeper of such jail must
receive and safely keep the prisoner until the officer or person
having charge of him is ready to proceed on his route , such
officer or person being chargeable with the expense of keeping .
(2) The officer or agent of a demanding state to whom a
prisoner may have been delivered following extradition proceed-
ings in another state , or to whom a prisoner may have been de-
livered after waiving extradition in such other state , and who
is passing through this state with such a prisoner for the purpose
of immediately returning such prisoner to the demanding state
may , when necessary, confine the prisoner in the jail of any
county or city through which he may pass ; and the keeper of
such jail must receive and safely keep the prisoner until the
officer or agent having charge of him is ready to proceed on his
route , such officer or agent , however , being chargeable with the
expense of keeping ; provided , however , that such officer or
agent shall produce and show to the keeper of such jail satis-
factory written evidence of the fact that he is actually trans-
porting such prisoner to the demanding state after a requisition
by the executive authority of such demanding state . Such
prisoner shall not be entitled to demand a new requisition while
in this state .
Section 14. Whenever any person within this state shall be
charged on the oath of any credible person before any judge or
magistrate of this state with the commission of any crime in
any other state and, except in cases arising under Section 7,
with having fled from justice , or with having been convicted
of a crime in that state and having escaped from confinement ,
or having broken the terms of his bail, probation or parole ,
or whenever complaint shall have been made before any judge
or magistrate in this state setting forth on the affidavit of any
credible person in another state that a crime has been committed
in such other state and that the accused has been charged in
such state with the commission of the crime , and , except in cases
arising under Section 7, has fled from justice , or with having

CHAPTER 135 563

or parole and is believed to be in this state , the judge or
magistrate shall issue a warrant directed to any peace officer

wherever he may be found in this state , and to bring him before
the circuit judge , the judge of the county court or the police

and affidavit upon which the warrant is issued shall be attached

Section 15. The arrest of a person may be lawfully made

Section 18. If the accused is not arrested under warrant
of the Governor by the expiration of the time specified in the
warrant or bond , the judge may discharge him or may recommit
him for a further period not to exceed sixty days , or may again
take bail for his appearance and surrender , as provided in Sec-
tion 17 , but within a period not to exceed sixty days after the
date of such new bond .
Section 19. If the prisoner is admitted to bail, and fails
to appear and surrender himself according to the conditions of
his bond , the judge , by proper order , shall declare the bond for-
feited and order his immediate arrest without warrant if he be
within this state . Recovery may be had on such bond in the
name of the state as in the case of other bonds given by the
accused in criminal proceedings within this state .
Section 20. The Governor may in his discretion surrender ,
upon demand of the executive authority of another state , any
person found in this state , notwithstanding the fact that a crim-
inal prosecution or charges under the laws of this state are
pending against such person , or that such person has been con-
victed in this state and is serving a sentence in any penal in-
stitution of this state . The Governor may condition the release
of such prisoner to the demanding state upon such terms as he
may stipulate , including the condition that the prisoner be
returned to this state immediately after trial and before com-
mencing the service of sentence , if any , in the demanding
state ; provided , however , in no case shall surrender of such
prisoner be construed as a complete relinquishment of juris-
diction by this state, but such prisoner shall forthwith be
returned to the custody of this state at expense of the demanding
state , immediately after trial in the demanding state , or the
completion of sentence therein , as the case may be .
Section 21. The guilt or innocence of the accused as to
the crime of which he is charged may not be inquired into by
the Governor or in any proceeding after the demand for extra

CHAPTER 135 565
dition accompanied by a charge of crime in legal form as above
provided shall have been presented to the Governor , except as
it may be involved in identifying the person held as the person
charged with the crime .
Section 22. The Governor may recall his warrant of arrest
or may issue another warrant whenever he deems proper .
Section 23. Whenever the Governor of this state shall
demand a person charged with crime or with escaping from
confinement or breaking the terms of his bail , probation or parole
in this state , from the executive authority of any other state ,
or from the chief justice or an associate justice of the Supreme
Court of the District of Columbia authorized to receive such
demand under the laws of the United States, he shall issue a
warrant , under the seal of this state , to some agent, command-

convey him to the proper officer of the county in this state in
which the offense was committed .

agent of the state demanding him before the agent is permitted to
remove him or receive him into custody .

of the same facts as the criminal proceeding to answer which he
is being or has been returned , until he has been convicted in the
criminal proceeding , or , if acquitted , until he has had reasonable
opportunity to return to the state from which he was extradited .
Section 27. (1) Any person arrested in this state charged

CHAPTER 138
(Н. В. 340)
AN ACT relating to liquefied petroleum gas .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 234.100 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) As used in KRS 234.100 to 234.160 and 234.990 the
term "liquefied petroleum gas " means and includes any material
which is composed predominantly of any of the following hydro-
carbons , or mixtures of them , whether in the liquid or in the
gaseous states : propane , propylene , butane (normal butane or
isobutane ) , and butylene .
( 2 ) " Commissioner ” means Commissioner of Public Safety.
Section 2. Section 234.110 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
No person shall transport in any manner within this state
liquefied petroleum gas in containers of a type not approved by
the commissioner .

Section 5. Section 234.140 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

( 1 ) The commissioner , after a public hearing thereon ,
shall make , promulgate , and enforce regulations setting forth
minimum general standards covering the design , construction ,
location , installation , and operation of equipment for storing ,
handling , transporting by tank truck, tank trailer , and utilizing
liquefied petroleum gases , and specifying the odorization of said
gases and the degree thereof . Said regulations shall be such as
are reasonably necessary for the protection of the health , welfare
and safety of the public and persons using such materials , and
shall be in substantial conformity with the generally accepted
standards of safety concerning the same subject matter .
Section 6. ( 1 ) There is created in the Department of
Public Safety the Liquefied Petroleum Gas Bureau , to administer
provisions of KRS 234.100 to 234.160 . The bureau shall consist
of a chief inspector and not less than three nor more than six
field inspectors , and an administrative assistant , and such other
employes as are found necessary to properly administer provi-
sions of this section .

inspector , and administrative assistant shall be made to the

who has not passed the examination but who has presented other

Section 11. Section 234.160 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
All moneys collected under the provisions of KRS 234.100
to 234.160 shall be paid into the State Treasury and credited to a
trust and agency fund to be used by the Department of Public
Safety solely for the administration and enforcement of KRS
234.100 to 234.160 and 234.990 .
Section 12. Section 234.990 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended and re - enacted to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who violates any of the provisions of
KRS 234.100 to 234.160 shall be fined not less than twenty -five
dollars nor more than two hundred dollars .
( 2 ) Any person who violates any regulation promulgated
under provisions of KRS 234.100 to 234.160 and after having
been given notice to cease and desist unless said violation is
corrected within ten days shall be fined not less than twenty-

CHAPTER 139 577
five dollars nor more than two hundred dollars for each day
said violation continues subsequent to the ten day period .
(3 ) When any person violates any provision of this Act
or regulations , which violation constitutes a public danger , an
immediate notice to cease and desist may be issued, and failure
to comply with notice shall be held in violation , and failure to
comply immediately upon presentation of said notice shall result
in a fine of not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than two
hundred dollars for each day said violation continues subsequent
to the day of issuance of notice .
(4) Any person violating the provisions of KRS 234.200
to 234.260 shall be fined not less than one hundred dollars or
more than five hundred dollars , or imprisoned in the county jail
for not more than sixty days .
(5) Any firm or corporation violating the provisions of
KRS 234.200 to 234.260 shall be fined not less than one hundred
dollars or more than five hundred dollars .

AN ACT relating to motor vehicles , motor transportation and the revenue
and taxation thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby created to read as follows :
All certificates authorizing the transportation of persons
over regular routes shall be so construed as to authorize the
transportation in the same vehicle of baggage , light express ,
newspapers and mail .
Section 2. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby enacted to provide as follows :
wise:
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires other-
( 1 ) The term “automobile utility trailer " shall mean any
trailer or semi -trailer designed for use with and towed behind a
passenger automobile .

578 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) The term " automobile utility trailer lessor " shall mean
any person who is engaged in the business of leasing or renting
automobile utility trailers , but shall not include the agents of
such person .
(3) The term "utility trailer permit " means a permit au-
thorizing a person to engage in the business of automobile utility
trailer lessor , and such permit shall be subject to the same fees ,
renewal requirements and suspension and revocation powers as
are imposed in respect to other permits issued pursuant to the
provisions of this chapter .
Section 3. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby enacted to provide as follows :
(1) No person shall act as an automobile utility trailer
lessor without first obtaining a permit from the department .
(2 ) A permit authorizing a person to act as an automo-
bile utility trailer lessor shall also authorize the agents of such a
person to act on his behalf during the period of their agency.
(3) Permits authorizing a person to act as an automobile
utility trailer lessor may be issued by the department without a
hearing . In the event a hearing be held by the department
upon an application for a permit authorizing a person to act as
an automobile utility trailer lessor , the question for determination
shall be the fitness, willingness and ability of the applicant , and
whether the operation proposed is bona fide .
(4) Before a permit authorizing a person to act as an auto-
mobile utility trailer lessor is issued by the department , the ap-
plicant therefor must comply with the provisions of KRS 281.655 .
Section 4. For the purpose of the interstate and intrastate
reciprocal provisions of Chapters 186 and 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes, automobile utility trailers shall be classified
the same as private passenger automobiles and extended the
same privileges , provided , however , that every automobile utility
trailer lessor shall register with the department , license and pay

CHAPTER 139 589
( 1 ) Upon the death of a certificate or permit holder , the
properly qualified administrator or executor of the estate of the
deceased certificate or permit holder may hold, control and
transfer any certificate held by the decedent upon application
and approval by the department , and further provided that
should the administrator or executor find it necessary to hold the
certificate or permit in excess of six months , he must first obtain
written approval of the commissioner of the department . Should
the executor or administrator seek to transfer the certificate or
permit to an heir or heirs of the deceased certificate or permit
holder , such transfer shall be accomplished by application regu-
larly filed, except that the commissioner may waive any notice
requirements otherwise applicable .
( 2 ) Any individual or partnership holding a certificate or
permit and desiring to operate as a corporation and having ap-
plied for and received their charter as a corporation shall file
an application with the department seeking to transfer the cer-
tificate or certificates from one business organization to the other
and in which event the commissioner may waive any notice re-
quirements otherwise applicable and assign the same for hear-
ing as in other cases required by statute or rules and regulations .
Section 14. Section 281.710 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Any motor carrier who is a nonresident of this state
may , prior to operating any motor vehicle for hire in this state ,
appoint a person residing in this state as agent upon whom pro-
cess may be served , and shall maintain such agent so long as
the carrier operates a motor vehicle for hire in this state . The
appointment shall be made by filing in the office of the depart-
ment a statement giving the name and residence of the agent.
If the motor carrier is an individual , the statement shall be sub-
scribed by him ; if the motor carrier is a partnership , corporation
or an unincorporated association or company , the statement shall
be subscribed by a partner or by an officer . When any change
is made in the agent, the motor carrier shall immediately file with
the department a statement of the change , and the former agent
shall remain agent for the purpose of service until the statement
of appointment of the new agent is filed.
(2) Any motor carrier who fails to appoint a resident of

590 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
this state as agent upon whom process may be served or fails to
maintain such agent after his original appointment shall there-
upon become subject to the provisions of Chapter 188 , of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes, and the Secretary of State shall be his
agent for the service of process .
Section 15. Section 281.826 and Section 424.320 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .
CHAPTER 140
(Н. В. 366)
AN ACT relating to roads and highways .
Approved March 25, 1960
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 179.420 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) The funds apportioned the several counties under the
provisions of KRS 179.410 shall be expended for the construction
and maintenance of county roads under the supervision of the
Department of Highways .
( 2) Each year the Department of Highways , by agreement
with the fiscal court of the county , shall plan the road con-
struction , repair or maintenance to be done during the year with
the funds herein apportioned , taking into consideration the
mileage of county roads in the county , the condition and needs
of such roads , giving priority to the maintenance of county roads
and bridges heretofore constructed .
(3) If for any reason the Department of Highways and
the fiscal court of any county are unable to reach agreement
upon a plan for the repair , maintenance , construction or recon-
struction of the roads of the county for any year , the Department
of Highways is empowered to use such of the funds allotted for
the county roads and bridges of such county as may be required
for the maintenance of such roads and bridges , which amount
shall not exceed twenty -five per cent of the allotment to the
county for the fiscal year, and such maintenance costs shall be
charged to the allotment of the particular county affected .
(4) If thirty days after the beginning of the fiscal year of
the Department of Highways in which the funds were appor

CHAPTER 140 591
tioned , the department and the fiscal court of any county have
failed to agree on a plan , the funds so apportioned to such county ,
less the funds , if any, expended or obligated for the maintenance
of theretofore constructed county roads and bridges shall be
expended by the Department of Highways for the construction
or reconstruction of the county roads and bridges according to
the priority schedule designated by the fiscal court , but the
amount of money to be expended on each of the roads or
bridges so designated and the type of roads or bridges and the
material used shall be in accordance with the specifications of
the Department of Highways .
(5) Any funds apportioned to any county , less the funds ,
if any, expended or obligated for the construction , reconstruction
or maintenance of county roads or bridges in any county shall
be held for the use and benefit of said county , and shall not be
deducted from the apportionment to that county in any sub-
sequent year , and shall not be expended in any other county
or used for any other purpose .
(6) Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the De-
partment of Highways may enter into agreements with the fiscal
court of any county respecting the use of county - owned equip-
ment or materials , and the use of county employes , and the use
of equipment owned by the department , personnel of the depart-
ment, or materials of the department , in accomplishing the con-
struction , reconstruction , maintenance and repair of county roads
and bridges from the funds apportioned to such county under
the provisions of KRS 179.410 .
Section 2. Section 179.430 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) All the funds apportioned under provisions of KRS
179.410 shall remain under the control of the Department of
Highways and be expended under the direction of the Depart-
ment of Highways .
(2) In order to provide for the simplification of the ad-
ministration , and facilitate the uniform operation of KRS 179.410
to KRS 179.440 , the Department of Highways may prescribe
such reasonable rules and regulations , not inconsistent with law,
as it deems necessary and proper to carry out the provisions of
those sections .
Approved March 25 , 1960

592 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 141
(Н. В. 383 )
AN ACT establishing a commission for the study and evaluation of the
system of public education in Kentucky .
WHEREAS , a major portion of the appropriations from the General Fund
are made in support of the public education program ; and
WHEREAS , Kentucky's growth in population will continue in the decade
to come, resulting in the need for further expansion of educational
facilities and programs ; and
WHEREAS , no comprehensive study has been made with regard to the
school foundation program or other public education programs ; and
WHEREAS , Kentucky's desire for a continuous strengthening and im-
proving of public education necessitates the continuing evaluation
of the programs by educators and lay people alike ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) In order to provide for the continuous
study and evaluation of the Commonwealth's system of public
education , there is hereby created a commission to be known as
the Commission on Public Education . The commission's staff
constitutes an agency independent of state administrative de-
partments .
(2) The commission shall consist of nine members known
for their interest in and knowledge of the state's education sys-
tem , said members to be appointed by the Governor . Not more
than four of these members shall be educators . Three members
shall be appointed for a term of two years ; three for a term of
three years ; and three for a term of four years . These appoint-
ments shall be effective July 1, 1960. At the expiration of these
terms appointments shall be for terms of four years . Appoint-
ments shall be made by the Governor to fill existing vacancies .
( 3 ) The commission shall study the problems and evaluate
programs of public education within the Commonwealth . Such
studies and evaluations shall include but not be limited to
present policies concerning public school transportation , free
textbooks programs , school personnel , and public school physical

CHAPTER 141 593
plants . The commission shall submit an annual report to the
Governor and such special reports on
itsfindings
asthe Governor
may require
.
(
4) The chairman and vice chairman
ofthe commission

beelected
atthe first meeting and each year thereafter
