CHAPTER 175
(Н. В. 250
)
AN ACT relating to advertising and other informational devices along
or adjacent to public roads and highways
.
WHEREAS
, interstate highways
, limited
-access highways and turnpikes
are designed and controlled for maximum safety and maximum
efficiency in the movement
ofarterial traffic
, and by reason of the
high speed limits permitted on such highways
tofacilitate the
movement of traffic all distracting influences or devices intended
todivert attention
ofmotorists constitute hazards contrary
tothe
interests of public safety
; and

CHAPTER 175 795
WHEREAS , in the location , design and construction of such high-
ways consideration is given to preservation and enhancement of
natural scenic beauty and aesthetic values , at a substantial expendi-
ture of public funds , and it is in the public interest that the values
accruing to the public from such investment of public funds be
preserved and protected ; and
WHEREAS , for the reasons stated , public policy dictates that adver-
tising devices be prohibited along or adjacent to such highways , but
to avoid undue hardships it is desirable that a reasonable opportun-
ity be afforded for realization of existing private investments ; and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly has found that the greatest portion
of the value of billboards and other highway advertising devices is
not in the cost of the materials of the structure but rather is in an
intangible value based upon the real and potential numbers of high-
way travelers who will view the advertising devices ; and
WHEREAS , such value is created by the public funds of the Common-
wealth in constructing and maintaining the highways , and exists
only by reason of the highways ; and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly has found that a period of five years
is a reasonable maximum period for realization of investments in
existing advertising devices , considering the factors hereinabove
mentioned ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) "Limited-access highway " means a road or highway
or bridge constructed pursuant to the provisions of KRS 177.220
through 177.310 .
( 2 ) " Interstate highway " means any highway , road , street ,
access facility , bridge or overpass which is designated as a por-
tion of the National System of Interstate and Defense Highways
as may be established by law , or as may be so designated by
the Department of Highways in the joint construction of the
system by the Department of Highways and the United States
Department of Commerce , Bureau of Public Roads .
(3 ) "Turnpike ” means any road or highway or appurtenant
facility constructed pursuant to the provisions of KRS 177.390
through 177.570 , or pursuant to the provisions of any other defi-
nition of " turnpike " in the Kentucky Revised Statutes, or any

796 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
other highway , road , parkway , bridge or street upon which a
toll or fee is charged for the use of motor vehicular traffic .
(4) "Advertising device " means any billboard, sign , no-
tice , poster , display or other device intended to attract the at-
tention of operators of motor vehicles on the highways , and shall
include a structure erected or used in connection with the display

the highways and otherwise to achieve the objectives set forth in

of the following advertising devices , if they comply with such
regulations , shall not be deemed a violation of this Act :
( 1 ) An advertising device which is to be erected or main-

( 3 ) An advertising device indicating the sale or leasing

tary of Commerce for the purpose of carrying out the national
policy of promoting the safety, convenience and enjoyment of
public travel and the free flow of interstate commerce and the
protection of the public investment in the National System of
Interstate and Defense Highways within the Commonwealth .
Section 8. Any person who willfully violates any of the

of vehicular travel , notwithstanding that they may be tempor-
arily closed or travel thereon restricted for the purpose of con-
struction , maintenance , repair or reconstruction ;
(6) "Liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealer " means
any person who imports or causes to be imported into this state
for resale or use , or any person making sales in this state, of
liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel for resale or use in this state
by a licensed liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel user-seller ;
(7) "Liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel user-seller "
means any person , not licensed as a liquefied petroleum gas motor
fuel dealer , who dispenses liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel
into the fuel tanks of, or attached to , motor vehicles for the
propulsion of such motor vehicles on the public highways , and
shall include any such person who so dispenses liquefied
petroluem gas motor fuel for consumption in such motor vehicles
owned , leased , or operated by him .

Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
( 1) A license shall be required of each liquefied petroleum
gas motor fuel dealer and each liquefied petroleum gas motor
fuel user -seller .
( 2 ) Application for a license shall be made to the depart-
ment upon forms prepared and furnished by the department .
The application shall contain such information as the depart-
ment deems necessary .
(3 ) Concurrently with the filing of an application for a
license , a liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealer shall file
with the department a bond as required under Section 5 of
this Act . No license shall be issued to any person unless such
person has furnished a bond as provided in Section 5 of this
Act to secure payment of taxes , penalties and interest imposed
by this Act. No bond shall be filed with an application for a
liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel user-seller license .
(4 ) The application in proper form having been accepted
for filing, the bond , if required , having been accepted and
approved and the other conditions and requirements of this
section having been complied with, the department shall issue a
license. However , if an application for a license is filed by any
person whose license has at any time previously been revoked for
cause by the department , or if the department is of the opinion
that the person who makes the application as a subterfuge for
the real party in interest whose license, prior to the time of
filing the application , has been revoked for cause, or that the
application is not for any other reason filed in good faith or is
not for sufficient cause, the department may , after a hearing of
which the applicant shall be given ten days' notice in writing
and in which he shall have the right to appear in person or by
counsel and present testimony , refuse to issue a license to that
person .
(5) All licenses shall be valid and remain in full force and
effect until suspended or revoked for cause or otherwise can-
celled.

Section 9. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
Liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealers using , selling ,
and/or delivering liquefied petroleum gas to motor vehicles ,
or into storage for use in motor vehicles , shall report and

and number of taxable gallons sold or delivered . The number

Section 11. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
The department may audit the books and records of
each licensee and make such other investigations as it deems
necessary to determine whether or not the tax and other require-
ments imposed by this Act have been met .
Section 12. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
The reports required by Section 8 of this Act shall be
accompanied by a certified or cashier's check payable to the
State Treasurer , for the amount of tax due for the preceding
calendar month computed as provided in Section 9 of this Act ,
except that the department may waive this requirement and ac-
cept the check of the licensee if he is of sound financial condi-
tion and has established a good record of compliance with the
requirements of this Act .
Section 13. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
( 1 ) If any liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealer
neglects or refuses to make the return required by Section 8 of
this Act or to pay the tax at the time provided , a penalty of
twenty per cent thereof and interest at six per cent per annum
from the date when due shall be paid as a part of the tax . If
collected by proceedings in court , an additional penalty of twenty
per cent shall be collected and distributed as is authorized by
KRS 134.400 and 135.060 .
(2) If any taxpayer subject to the penalty provided in
subsection ( 1 ) of this section submits in writing to the depart-
ment his reasons for failure to comply with Section 8 of this
Act , and if the department finds the reasons evidence of justifia-
ble cause for modifying the penalty provided in subsection ( 1 )

CHAPTER 176 807
of this section, it may modify the penalty exacted therein to five
per cent of the amount of the tax due and delinquent , provided
the five per cent penalty may be reduced to a one per cent
penalty if the violation is the first violation by the taxpayer within
twelve months .
(3 ) Any liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealer who
fails to make any return required under Section 8 of this Act
within the time required shall pay a penalty of ten dollars to
be assessed and collected in the manner provided for the assess-
ment and collection of taxes under this Act or in a civil action
initiated by the department . In addition such delinquent tax-
payer may be compelled to make the required report or return .
Section 14. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
With respect to this Act , it is unlawful for any person to :
( a) Fail to pay the taxes due ;
(b) Fail , neglect or refuse to make and file any statement
required in the manner or within the time required ;
(c) Make any false statement or conceal any material fact
in any record , report or affidavit ;
(d) Conduct any activities requiring a license without a
license or after a license has been surrendered , cancelled or
revoked ;
( e) Assign or attempt to assign a license to act as a
liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel dealer ;
(f) Violate any other provisions .
Section 15. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
( 1) The department shall administer the taxes provided
in this Act and may prescribe , adopt and enforce regulations
relating to the administration and enforcement thereof .
(2) The department shall , upon the request of the officials
to whom are entrusted the enforcement of the liquefied petro-
leum gas motor fuel tax law of any other state , the United
States , the provinces of the Dominion of Canada , forward to
such officials any information which it may have relative to
the manufacture , receipt , sale , use, transportation , shipment ,

808 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or delivery by any person of liquefied petroleum gas motor fuel,
provided such other state or states provides for the furnishing
of like information to this state .
Section 16. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
Any person violating any provision of Section 13 of this
Act shall be fined not more than one thousand dollars or im-
prisoned in the county jail for more than six months , or both .
Upon conviction of a second offense , he shall be imprisoned for
not less than one year nor more than five years in the state
penitentiary .
Section 17. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is enacted to read as follows :
The effective date of this Act shall be July 1, 1960 .

Section 5. There hereby is created and established the
Department of Aeronautics , which shall constitute a statutory
administrative department of the State government within the
meaning of KRS Chapter 12 , and which department shall exer-
cise all of the administrative functions of the Commonwealth in
relation to aeronautics , including aircraft , air transportation ,
airports , air navigation , and all matters related to said functions .
The Department of Aeronautics shall be headed by the Commis-
sioner of Aeronautics , who shall be the executive officer and
have sole charge of the administration of the department and the
performance of all functions of the department . No person
shall be appointed as commissioner unless at the time of his
appointment he holds a valid commercial license , or its
equivalent , issued by the Federal Aviation Agency for the pilot-
ing of aircraft , and unless he shall have had at least five years '
experience in aeronautics .
Section 6. The Commissioner of Aeronautics , with the
advice and approval of the Governor , may appoint an Advisory
Board of Aeronautics to advise with the commissioner in carrying
out the functions of the department . Such board , if appointed ,
shall consist of not less than three nor more than nine members ,
who shall serve for such terms , not exceeding four years , as may
be fixed by the commissioner , and who shall be removable at the
will of the commissioner . The commissioner shall serve as
chairman of the board , and the board shall meet at the call
of the chairman . The members of the board shall be allowed
their necessary expenses attendant upon their duties .

CHAPTER 179 833
Section 42. Bonds issued pursuant to Section 39 to Section
51 shall be negotiable and shall not be subject to taxation . If any
officer whose signature or countersignature appears on the bonds
or coupons ceases to be such officer before delivery of the bonds ,
his signature or countersignature shall nevertheless be valid and
sufficient for all purposes the same as if he had remained in office
until delivery . The bonds shall be sold in such manner and upon
such terms as the legislative body of the governmental unit or
the Department of Finance deems best . In no event shall any
bonds be negotiated on a basis to yield more than six per cent .
The bonds shall be payable solely from the revenue derived from
airport facilities or taxes levied for airport development and shall
not constitute an indebtedness of the governmental unit or the
Commonwealth within the meaning of the Constitution . It shall
be plainly stated on the face of each bond that it has been issued
under the provisions of Section 39 to Section 51 and that it does
not constitute an indebtedness of the governmental unit or the
Commonwealth within the meaning of the Constitution .
Section 43. All money received from the bonds shall be
applied solely for the acquisition of the airport facilities , and
the necessary expense of preparing , printing and selling said
bonds , or to advance the payment of the interest on the bonds
during the first three years following the date of the bonds .
Section 44. If there is any default in the payment of
principal or interest of any bond, any court having jurisdiction
of the action may appoint a receiver to administer the airport
facilities on behalf of the air board , governmental unit or the
Commonwealth with power to charge and collect rates , charges
and fees sufficient to provide for the payment of any bonds or
obligations outstanding against the airport facilities , and for the
payment of operating expenses , and to apply the income and
revenue in conformity with Section 39 to Section 51 .
Section 45. At or before the issuance of bonds the legisla-
tive body of the governmental unit shall , by ordinance or resolu-
tion , set aside and pledge the income and revenue of the airport
facilities into a separate and special fund to be used and applied
in payment of the cost thereof and in the maintenance , operation
and depreciation thereof . The ordinance or resolution shall

834 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
definitely fix and determine the amount of revenue necessary to
be set apart and applied to the payment of principal and interest
of the bonds and the proportion of the balance of the income and
revenue to be set aside as a proper and adequate depreciation
account , and the remaining proportion of such balance shall be
set aside for the reasonable and proper operation and mainte-
nance of the airport facilities . The rates and fees to be charged
for the services of the airport facilities shall be fixed and revised
from time to time so as to be sufficient to provide for payment
of interest upon all bonds and to create a sinking fund to pay
the principal thereof when due , and to provide for the operation
and maintenance of the airport facilities and an adequate de-
preciation account . The provision of Section 37 ( 2 ) that the
money derived from the use of the airport shall be first applied
to the maintenance of the airport shall not apply in the case of
airport facilities for which revenue bonds have been issued under
Section 39 to Section 51 .
Section 46. If a surplus is accumulated in the operatingang
and maintenance funds equal to the cost of maintaining and
operating the airport facilities during the remainder of the
calendar , operating or fiscal year , and during the succeeding
like year , any excess over such amount may be transferred at
any time by the legislative body of the governmental unit to
the depreciation account , to be used for improvements , exten-
sions or addiitons to the airport facilities .
Section 47. The funds accumulating to the depreciation
account shall be expended in balancing depreciation in the air-
port facilities or in making new constructions , extensions or
additions thereto . Any such accumulations may be invested as
the legislative body of the governmental unit may designate ,
and if invested the income from such investment shall be carried
into the depreciation account .
Section 48. The governmental unit may issue new bonds
to provide funds for the payment of any outstanding bonds , in
accordance with the procedure prescribed by Section 39 to Sec-
tion 51. The new bonds shall be secured to the same extent and
shall have the same source of payment as the bonds refunded .

CHAPTER 179 835
Section 49. If the legislative body of the governmental
unit finds that the bonds authorized will be insufficient to ac-
complish the purpose desired , additional bonds may be author-
ized and issued in the same manner .
Section 50. Any governmental unit acquiring any airport
facilities pursuant to the provisions of Section 39 to Section 51
may , at the time of issuing the bonds for such acquisition , pro-
vide for additional bonds for extensions and permanent improve-
ments to be placed in escrow and to be negotiated from time to
time as proceeds for that purpose may be necessary. Bonds
placed in escrow shall , when negotiated , have equal standing
with the bonds of the same issue .
Section 51. Section 39 to Section 50 shall constitute a
method for the acquisition of airport facilities by governmental
units or the Commonwealth in addition or as an alternate to
any other method authorized by statute. It is intended to au-
thorize the acquisition of additional facilities for an airport ac-
quired under authority of Section 33 as well as the original ac-
quisition of land and other facilities for an airport . Governmental
units shall have all authority conferred by Section 33 for the
acquisition of airport facilities . Except as otherwise provided in
Section 39 to Section 50, the provisions of Section 33 to Section
38 shall apply to the operation of airport facilities acquired under
Section 39 through Section 50 .
Section 52. There is hereby created and established within
the department , a commission to be known as the "Kentucky
Airport Zoning Commission " which, notwithstanding the pro-
visions of Chapter 100 and 147 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
shall be empowered to issue such orders , rules and regulations
pertaining to use of land within and around all publicly owned
airports within the state as will promote the public interest and
protect and encourage the proper use of such airports and their
facilities .
Section 53. ( 1) Such commission shall consist of nine
members , with one appointed from each congressional district of
the state and the Commissioner of Aeronautics who shall be the
ex officio chairman of such commission . The members of such
commission shall be appointed by the Governor for a term of

836 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
four years . The members shall serve for a term of four years ,
and until their successors are appointed , provided , however that
the first two members appointed shall serve for a term of one
year ; the next two for two years ; the next two for three years ;
and the final two for four years . Upon the expiration of the first
terms , successors shall be appointed for a term of four years .
( 2 ) Each member shall be a resident of the congressional
district from which he is appointed and shall be a person ex-
perienced in and familiar with the field of aeronautics .
(3) Members of the commission shall serve without com-
pensation except that they may be reimbursed for travel ex-
penses and other reasonable expenses incident to performance
of their duties .
Section 54. The commission shall be a body politic and
corporate with the usual corporate attributes , including , but not
limited to the power to sue and be sued , contract and be con-
tracted with and to do all things reasonable or necessary to
effectively carry out the duties prescribed . It shall meet on the
call of the chairman or four or more members . A quorum shall
consist of four members .
Section 55. The Franklin Circuit Court shall hold con-
current venue with the courts of this Commonwealth in all civil
and injunctive actions instituted by the commission for the
enforcement of applicable statutes , rules , regulations and orders
issued .
Section 56. All of the powers , provisions and duties re-
lating to the zoning and use of land , structures and air space
within and around public airports within the state are hereby
conferred upon , delegated to and vested in the commission . The
commission shall also exercise

Section 58. ( 1 ) The commission shall require that every
public airport in the state file with it, from time to time , as
required , maps showing the airport and area surrounding such
airport used for approcah or landing purposes . The commission
shall thereafter designate on such maps , by reference to the
Technical Standard Orders of the Federal Aviation Agency con-
cerning the area required for the safe maneuvering , approach
and landing of aircraft , the area over which jurisdiction will be
assumed for zoning purposes . The commission shall notify any
local zoning bodies of the area so designated and shall exercise
exclusive jurisdiction of such area insofar as it pertains to the
safe and proper manuevering of aircraft and the safe and proper
use of the airport involved . The local zoning body may retain
jurisdiction of zoning in such areas as to all other matters .
(2) The commission may adopt such regulations pertain-
ing to the zoning of areas over which jurisdiction is assumed as
will provide for the proper and safe use of such area and airport .
(3) The commission shall maintain a public file in the
offices of the department showing maps of each public airport
within the state and the area around such airport over which it
has assumed jurisdiction for zoning purposes and such shall con-
stitute public notice of the restrictions and zoning applicable to
such areas or airport . The commission shall also maintain a
public file showing any regulations adopted pertaining to land
uses in areas zoned and such shall constitute public notice of
same .
Section 59. In addition to considering the Technical Stand-
ard Orders of the Federal Aviation Agency in zoning the use of
land and structures in areas over which jurisdiction is assumed ,
the commission shall consider among other things the safety of
airport users and surface persons and property , the character of
flying operations conducted at the airport , the nature of the
terrain , the height of existing structures and trees above the level

Section 68. In order to bring about , in conformity with the

future needs of civil aeronautics , the department is authorized

governmental unit in the development of an airport owned or
controlled or to be owned or controlled by such governmental
unit out of appropriations made by the General Assembly for
such purposes . Such financial assistance may be furnished in
connection with federal aid , local or other funds available for
airport purposes .

monwealth of Kentucky , or by the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the United States government , or by the United States

Section 81. The Commissioner of Aeronautics may appoint
a board to be known as the "Capital City Air Board " to advise
with the commissioner in the supervision and operation of the
Capital City Airport , owned by the state and located at Frankfort .
Such board , if appointed , shall consist of five members , who shall
serve for such terms , not exceeding four years , as may be fixed
by the commissioner , and who shall be removable at the will of
the commissioner . The commissioner shall serve as chairman of
the board , and the board shall meet at the call of the chairman .
Members of the board shall serve without compensation but
shall be allowed any reasonable expenses incurred in the conduct
of the affairs of the board .
Section 82. ( 1 ) Any person violating any of the provi-
sions of this chapter with respect to operation of aircraft shall
be fined not less than ten dollars nor more than one hundred
dollars or imprisoned not more than ninety days or both .
( 2 ) Every person coming within the provisions of Section
24 through Section 30 and every officer , agent or employe of the
holder of any certificate issued under Section 24 to Section 30
and every other person who violates or causes , aids or abets any
violation of any order , rule or regulation lawfully issued pur-
suant to the authority granted by Section 24 to Section 30 shall ,
upon conviction , be punished by a fine of not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars . The prose-
cution may be had in the circuit court of any county in which the
offense or any part thereof shall have occurred .
(3 ) Each violation of the statutes pertaining to the State
Airport Zoning Commission or of any order , rule or regulation
promulgated pursuant thereto shall be punishable by a fine of
not less than one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred
dollars or imprisonment for not more than thirty days or both,
and each day a violation continues to exist shall constitute a
separate offense .
Section 83. If any section , paragraph , sentence or clause
of this Act is invalid , the invalidity thereof shall not affect the
validity of the remaining sections , paragraphs , clauses and sen-
tences hereof .
Section 84. Whereas , the development of a statewide air

legislative program , and whereas , it is necessary to take advant-
age of federal laws in as short a time as possible , an emergency

and approval by the Governor
.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :

standards to guide public officers and employes in the conduct of

3. No member

CHAPTER 181 847
dered , either by himself or another , in negotiations with the State
or an agency for the purchase by the State or an agency of an
interest in real property . This section shall not apply to appear-
ances before any court , except that negotiations shall be pro-
hibited as aforesaid at any time .
Section 4. No member of the General Assembly , or officer
or employe of an agency , may be in any manner interested , either
directly or indirectly , in his own name or in the name of any
other person , association , trust , or corporation , in any contract
or the performance of any work in the making or letting or ad-
ministration of which such officer or employe may be called upon
to act or vote . No such officer or employe may represent, either
as agent or otherwise , any person , association, trust or corpora-
tion , with respect to any application or bid for any contract or
work in regard to which such officer or employe may be called
upon to act or vote . Nor may any such officer or employe take ,
solicit or receive, either directly or indirectly , any money or other
thing of value as a gift or bribe or means of influencing his vote
or action in his official character. Any contract made and pro-
cured in violation hereof is void. For the purposes of this sec-
tion the holding of less than five per cent of the stock of a cor-
poration is not considered an interest .
Section 5. No member of the General Assembly , officer or
employe thereof , or officer or employe of any agency shall , for
compensation , appear before an agency as an expert witness.
Section 6. No member of the General Assembly , officer or
employe thereof, or officer or employe of any agency, shall act as
officer or agent for the Commonwealth or any agency in the
transaction of any business with himself , or with any corporation ,
company , association or firm in which he or his spouse has any
interest greater than five per cent of the total value thereof.
Section 7. No member of the General Assembly may ac-
cept any appointment as an officer or employe of the Common-
wealth or any agency unless he shall have first resigned his mem-
bership in the General Assembly , and it shall be unlawful for
the State Treasurer to pay any salary by reason of such appoint-
ment until the resignation has been accepted by the officer hav

848 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing a duty to call a special election to fill the particular vacancy
thereby created in the General Assembly .
Section 8. No member of the legislature , officer or em-
ploye of an agency or appointee shall knowingly himself or by
his partners or through any corporation which he controls or in
which he owns or controls more than ten per cent of the stock , or
by any other person for his use or benefit or on his account , un-
dertake , execute , hold or enjoy , in whole or in part , any contract ,
agreement , sale or purchase of the value of twenty - five dollars or
more , made , entered into , awarded or granted by any agency ,
unless said contract , agreement, sale or purchase was made or
let after public notice and competitive bidding.
Section 9. No officer , employe or appointee of an agency ,
including persons who serve without salary or other payment
for their services , shall knowingly receive or agree to receive ,
directly or indirectly , compensation for any services rendered
or to be rendered , either by himself or another , in any cause , pro-
ceeding , application or other matter which is before said agency
or before the department of State government in which said
agency functions .
Section 10. Any person who violates any provision of this
Act, upon conviction , shall be punished by confinement in the
penitentiary for not less than six months , nor more than two
years , or fined not less than two hundred dollars nor more than
one thousand dollars , or both , and in addition he shall be ad-
judged to have forfeited any statutory office or employment
which he may hold .
1961 .
Section 11. The effective date of this Act shall be July 1 ,

858 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 185
(Н. В. 502)
AN ACT relating to registration and purgation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Not less than ninety days prior to each primary
election for statewide officers the county registration and
purgation board shall notify by first class mail each registered
voter in the county at the address listed on his registration
record that if he moves from his present precinct to another
within the county without having his registration record trans-
ferred as provided in KRS 117.645 by
the day on which the registration books are closed , he will be
disqualified from voting .
Section 2. The name and address of the county registra-
tion and purgation board shall be indicated in the upper left
hand corner of the envelope with the following notation : “If un-
deliverable at address shown , return to sender immediately with-
out recourse to directory service. " When the mailed notice is
returned as provided herein , the county purgation board shall
immediately cancel the registration record of the voter and the
names of the purged voters shall be published in a newspaper
of the largest general circulation within the county not less than
seventy days prior to the primary election . Any voter so purged
may , however , have his name reinstated by submitting sufficient
proof to the board that he is still a legal resident of the precinct .
Reinstatement may be made during the period the registration
books are closed but not later than twenty days before the
primary .
Section 3. If any county registration and purgation board
fails or refuses to perform
itsduties
asrequired by this Act
, any
citizen
orcandidate may
, and the Commonwealth's attorney
or
the Attorney General shall when
sorequested by the State Regis-
tration and Purgation Board
, apply
tothe circuit court
ofthe
county for
asummary mandatory order
torequire compliance
.
Ifthe circuit court finds that the county board has not complied
with the provisions
ofthis Act
itshall issue such summary manda-
tory order
.
Approved March 25
, 1960

deposit in any bank , trust company or combined bank and trust
company organized under the laws of this state, or in any
national bank of this state, shall pay an annual tax to the state
equal to one-thousandth of one per cent upon the amount of the
deposit , and no deduction therefrom shall be made for any

the depositor on account of such deposits .

Section 8. Section 132.310 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Any person who has failed to list for taxation any
property omitted from assessment consisting of real property
or tangible personal property , except such as is subject to assess-
ment by the Kentucky Tax Commission , may at any time list
such property with the county tax commissioner . The tax
commissioner shall proceed to assess the property and shall
within ten days from the date the property was listed notify the
taxpayer of the amount of the assessment . The notice shall be
given as provided in KRS 132.450 ( 2 ) .
(2) The county tax commissioner may at any time list and

866 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
assess any real or tangible property which may have been omitted
from the regular assessment . Immediately upon listing and
assessing omitted real or tangible property , the county tax com-
missioner shall notify the taxpayer of the amount of the
assessment . The notice shall be given as provided in KRS
132.450 ( 2 ) .
(3) The taxpayer may appeal the assessment to the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission within fifteen days from the date of
notice , in the same manner as an appeal from the final action
of the county board of supervisors and may appeal from the
action of the Kentucky Tax Commission as provided in KRS
133.120. If no appeal is taken within fifteen days from the date
of notice , the assessment as made by the county tax commissioner
shall become final and shall be certified by the tax commis-
sioner to the county clerk .
(4) Any property voluntarily listed as omitted property
for taxation under this section shall be subject to penalties pro-
vided in KRS 132.290 ( 3 ) . Omitted property listed for tax-
ation under this section by the county tax commissioner shall
be subject to the penalties provided in KRS 132.290 ( 4 ) .
Section 9. Section 132.370 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) There shall be a county tax commissioner in each
county in lieu of a county assessor .
( 2 ) County tax commissioners shall be elected in the year
1961 and each four years thereafter and shall enter upon the dis-
charge of the duties of their office on the first Monday in
December after their election and continue in office for a
period of four years , and until the election and qualification
of their successors . County tax commissioners shall possess the
qualifications required by Section 100 of the Constitution and
by KRS 132.380 and shall be eligible for re-election .
(3) A county tax commissioner may be removed from
office by the circuit court of his county , upon petition of the
department or any taxpayer , for willful disobedience of any just
or legal order of the department , or for misfeasance or malfeas-
ance in office or willful neglect in the discharge of his official
duties , but he or the petitioner may appeal to the Court of
Appeals .

Section 16. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
(1) All taxable real property or interest therein sub-
ject to assessment by the county tax commissioner shall be re-
valued during the second year of each term of office by the
county tax commissioner in accordance with standards prescribed
by the Department of Revenue .
(2) The right of any individual to appeal the assessment
on his property in any year as provided in KRS 133.120 shall
in no way be affected by this statute .
(3) Should the county tax commissioner fail to revalue
property as required by this statute the Department of Revenue
shall have the authority to order an emergency revaluation in
the same manner as provided for emergency assessments by KRS
132.660 . Any county tax commissioner willfully violating the
provisions of this section shall be subject to removal from office
as provided by KRS 132.370 ( 3 ) .
Section 17. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Department of Revenue is hereby directed to re-
vise the Kentucky Real Property Appraisal Manual dated Janu-
ary , 1952 , on or before June 30, 1962 , according to the following
standards:
(a) All valuation data , classifications , and tables shall be
to the standard of the current manual .
(b) Valuation data shall be based on a current date set
by the Department of Revenue .
( c ) The manual shall be so constructed that supplements
may be issued from time to time to make the valuation data cur-
rent .
(d) Additional building classifications shall be added
based upon need .
( 2) The Commissioner of Revenue is hereby authorized
to contract for the revision of the Kentucky Real Property Ap-
praisal Manual if he deems it necessary,

874 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 18. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to real as follows :
The Department of Motor Transportation shall not issue a
certificate or permit with respect to any motor vehicles other
than those required to be registered by the county clerk for
any year until the owner has presented proof of the following :
(a) Payment of ad valorem tax based on assessment of
the motor vehicle as of March 1 of the year preceding the year
for which the certificate or permit is to be issued, or
(b) The motor vehicle was not subject to assessment for
ad valorem tax on March 1 of the year preceding the year for
which the certificate or permit is to be issued .
Section 19. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
(1) The county clerk shall not issue a registration receipt
for any motor vehicle for any year under the provisions of KRS
186.020 to 186.260 until the owner has presented to the clerk
proof of the following :
(a) Payment of all ad valorem taxes based on the assess-
ment of the motor vehicle as of March 1 of the year preceding
the year for which the vehicle is to be licensed , or
(b ) The motor vehicle was not subject to assessment for
ad valorem tax on March 1 of the year preceding the year for
which the vehicle is to be licensed .
( 2) The county clerk shall not issue a registration receipt
or a transfer of registration for any motor vehicle upon which the
state or any other taxing district has filed a notice of lien under
the provisions of Section 28 of this Act until the tax lien shall
have been properly released.
Section 20. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
(1) Any city of the first, second, or third class that has
adopted the county assessment pursuant to KRS 132.285 for
property situated within such city or any independent school
district may contract with the county sheriff for the collection
of ad valorem taxes on motor vehicles . The sheriff shall receive
as compensation the actual cost of collecting and reporting funds
due any city or independent school district , not to exceed four

CHAPTER 186 875
per cent of said taxes . The compensation of the sheriff for col-
lecting all taxes shall not exceed that which is authorized by
KRS 134.290 . In collecting city and school taxes pursuant to
contract , the sheriff shall have the same powers as applicable
to state and county taxes .
(2) Any city or independent school district which has
contracted with the county sheriff as authorized in subsection
( 1) may direct the sheriff to prepare a notice of lien and file
the same in the same manner as provided for other taxing dis-
tricts in Section 28 of this Act.
(3) Any city or independent school district for which the
county sheriff does not collect any ad valorem taxes may pre-
pare a notice of lien for tax on a motor vehicle and request the
county clerk to file and record the notice for the same fee and
in the same manner as provided by Section 28 of this Act .

Section 29. Section 131.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The Department of Revenue shall be headed by the
Commissioner of Revenue , who shall be the executive officer
and shall have sole charge of the administration of the depart-
ment , and shall perform all functions of the department .
(2) The Commissioner of Revenue , with two associate
members , shall constitute the Kentucky Tax Commission . The
Commissioner of Revenue shall be chairman of the commission .
( 3 ) The Department of Revenue shall be organized for
administrative convenience into divisions in accordance with
Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Section 30. Section 131.040 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The Governor shall appoint two associate members of the
Kentucky Tax Commission . The term of office of each associate
member shall be four years . One associate member shall be
appointed each four years beginning with 1937 and the other
shall be appointed each four years beginning with 1938. The
term of each shall begin on July 1. The commission shall be
allotted such funds out of Department of Revenue appropria-
tions as is necessary for
itsoperations
.

Section 3. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The estimated tax provided for in Section 2 shall be
paid as follows :
(a) If the declaration is filed on or before May 15 of the
taxable year , the estimated tax shall be paid in three equal in-
stallments . The first installment shall be paid at the time of the
filing of the declaration . The second and third installments shall
be paid on September 15 and December 15, respectively , of the
taxable year .
(b) If the declaration is filed after May 15 and not after
September 15 of the taxable year and is not required by Section 2
of this Article to be filed on or before May 15 of the taxable year ,
the estimated tax shall be paid in two equal installments . The
first installment shall be paid at the time of the filing of the
declaration and the second installment on December 15 of the
taxable year .
(c) If the declaration is filed after September 15 of the
taxable year and is not required to be filed on or before Septem-
ber 15 of the taxable year , the estimated tax shall be paid in full
at the time of the filing of the declaration .

Anew section
ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes

(1) City and suburban bus companies operating under
a

CHAPTER 186 905
certificate of convenience and necessity issued pursuant to Chap-
ter 281 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes shall be entitled to a
refund of two - sevenths of the amount of KRS Chapter 138 taxes
paid on motor fuels used in their regularly scheduled operations
in Kentucky .
(2) No person shall be entitled to a refund hereunder
unless he shall have first filed with the Department of Revenue
a bond with approved surety in an amount of not less than one
thousand dollars nor more than five thousand dollars to be deter-
mined by the Department of Revenue , conditioned upon faithful
compliance with this section and upon the payment to the Com-
monwealth of any refunds to which he was not entitled .
(3 ) Applications for refund shall on or before the last day
of each month following the quarter , September 30, December
31 , March 31 , and June 30, be made to the Department of
Revenue on forms and in the manner prescribed by it for the
refund of tax paid on motor fuel used by buses during the
quarter . Each application for a refund shall show the number
of gallons of motor fuel purchased during the quarter for use
in buses ; the date and quantity of each purchase ; the vendor from
whom such fuel was purchased ; the number of gallons on which
refund is claimed ; and such other information as the Department
of Revenue may require . The application shall be made under
oath .
(4) The Department of Revenue shall audit the applica-
tion and make such other investigation as it deems necessary to
determine whether it constitutes a proper claim . When the
Department of Revenue is satisfied that a refund is proper it
shall authorize two - sevenths of the amount of the tax paid to be
refunded as other refunds are made and the amount refunded
shall be deducted from current motor fuel tax receipts .
(5) When the Department of Revenue finds that an ap-
plication for a refund contains a false or fraudulent statement or
that a refund has been fradulently obtained , the Department of
Revenue shall refuse to grant any refunds to the person making
such false or fraudulent statement or fraudulently obtaining a
refund for a period of two years from the date of such findings .
( 6 ) The Department of Revenue may prescribe , adopt and
enforce regulations relating to the administration and enforce-
ment thereof .

906 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

fuel purchased July 1, 1960 and thereafter .
Section 10. Section 186.070 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Every manufacturer of , or dealer in , motor vehicles in
this state shall register with each county clerk in which his prin-
cipal office or place of business and any branch office or sub-
agent or agency is located , and pay an annual registration fee of
twenty -five dollars to each clerk . Upon receipt of this fee, the
clerk shall issue the manufacturer or dealer a certificate of reg-
istration and one dealer plate . Every manufacturer or dealer
registered under this section shall be furnished additional dealer's
plates upon the payment of five dollars for each plate so desired .
A motor vehicle bearing dealer's plates may be used only by a
member of the firm registered or by a bona fide salesman or
employe of the firm for any purpose reasonably connected with
the sale or demonstration for sale and delivery of the dealer's
motor vehicles , or by any manufacturer or dealer licensed as pro-
vided above in transporting any motor vehicle over the highways
of this state to his place of retail business from a manufacturer or
wholesale dealer in motor vehicles . Any dealer in motor vehicles
may allow any bona fide customer or the customer's employes to
use the dealer's tag on the motor vehicle that is being demon-
strated . This provision shall be limited to one trip or demonstra-
tion to the same prospective customer .
( 2) Each manufacturer and dealer when making applica-
tion for dealer's plates shall file a verified statement with the
clerk , giving the name and address of each member of the firm
and each bona fide salesman or employe entitled to the use of
the plates for demonstration purposes only . When any bona fide
salesman or employe so registered is no longer in the employ
of the firm , an amended verified statement shall be filed with
the clerk showing that fact , and when any additional salesmen or
employes are employed , an amended verified statement showing
their names and addresses shall be filed with the clerk so that the
records in the clerk's office will at all times show the bona fide
salesmen and employes actually in the service of the registered
dealer or manufacturer .
(3 ) Any manufacturer or dealer violating any of the pro-

CHAPTER 186 907
visions of this section may be cited by the Department of Revenue
to show cause at a public hearing before the Kentucky Tax Com-
mission why his dealer's license should not be suspended and his
dealer's plates cancelled by the commission . The manufacturer
or dealer shall be notified by registered letter or summons . Upon
cause the Kentucky Tax Commission shall have the power to
suspend for the period of not less than thirty days nor more than
ninety days the manufacturer's or dealer's license and to cancel
his dealer's plates . The aggrieved manufacturer or dealer may
appeal from the ruling of the Kentucky Tax Commission accord-
ing to the provisions of KRS 131.120 . Such manufacturer or
dealer whose license has been suspended is prohibited from en-
gaging in the business of selling or buying motor vehicles . The
license of any manufacturer or dealer shall be revoked for a period
of one year and his dealer's plates cancelled in the event he
violates any of the provisions of this section during this suspen-
sion period or has been suspended by the commission more than
twice for violations of this section . At the end of the revocation
or suspension period the manufacturer or dealer whose license
has been revoked or suspended and dealer's plates cancelled may
follow the provisions of this section and again be registered and
secure dealer's plates from the clerk .

ARTICLE V
Section 1. Section 243.730 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) ( a ) Wholesalers of distilled spirits and wine shall pay
and report the tax levied by KRS 243.720 ( 1 ) ( 2 ) on or before
the fifteenth day of the calendar month next succeeding the
month in which possession or title of the alcoholic beverages is
acquired , in accordance with rules and regulations of the Depart-
ment of Revenue designed reasonably to protect the revenues of
the Commonwealth .
(b ) After March 31 , 1960 , brewers of malt beverages shall
pay and report the tax levied by KRS 243.720 ( 3 ) on or before
the fifteenth day of the calendar month next succeeding the
month in which the brewer sells , transfers , or passes title of the
malt beverage to a distributor , retailer or consumer within this
state , in accordance with rules and regulations of the Depart-
ment of Revenue designed reasonably to protect the revenues of
the Commonwealth .
(2 ) ( a ) Every wholesaler of distilled spirits or wine before
using , selling or distributing by sale or gift distilled spirits and
wine shall qualify with the Department of Revenue . In order
to so qualify , and in lieu of furnishing a bond or other security ,
each wholesaler who was licensed in this state on February 29 ,
1960 , shall make an advance payment of tax to the department

CHAPTER 186 909
in an amount equal to twice the average monthly liability of such
wholesaler for taxes imposed by KRS 243.720 ( 1 ) ( 2 ) during
the calendar year 1959 as shown by records of the department .
If such wholesaler shall have in his possession unused tax stamps
which he has already purchased from the department , he may
apply the unused stamps toward the amount of tax required by
this subsection according to the rules and regulations of the
department .
(b) Any person who acquired or who shall acquire a license
as a wholesaler of distilled spirits or wine on or after March 1 ,
1960 shall immediately prepare and file a report on a form pre-
scribed by the department . Such report shall contain an esti-
mate of the total number of wine gallons of distilled spirits and
the total number of gallons of wine for which the wholesaler
expects to become liable for the payment of tax under KRS
243.720 ( 1 ) ( 2 ) during the first twelve months that he is en-
gaged in business . Immediately upon the receipt of the report
from the wholesaler , the department shall determine the average
monthly amount of tax imposed by KRS 243.720 ( 1 ) ( 2 ) for
which it appears that the wholesaler may become liable and shall
bill the wholesaler for an amount of tax equal to twice the
monthly average . The department shall have the power to ad-
just the estimate in order to make it conform as nearly as prac-
ticable with the past experience of other wholesalers who have
been engaged in business for twelve months or more , and in no
event shall the advance payment required of any new wholesaler
be less than one-half of the average amount of advance payments
required under paragraph ( a ) of this subsection , as determined
by the department . The bill for advance tax shall be due and
payable immediately upon receipt by the wholesaler . He shall
make payment to the department by cashier's or certified check
before using , selling or distributing by sale or gift any distilled
spirits or wine .

Section 3. Section 243.990 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended to read as follows :
(1) Any person who, by himself or acting through another ,
directly or indirectly , violates any of the provisions of KRS
243.020 to 243.670 , for which no other penalty is provided , shall ,
for the first offense , be fined not less than one hundred nor more
than two hundred dollars , or be imprisoned in the county jail or
workhouse for not more than six months , or both ; and for the
second and each subsequent violation , he shall be fined not less
than two hundred nor more than five hundred dollars , or be im

912 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
prisoned in the county jail or workhouse for not more than six
months , or both . The penalties provided for in this subsection
shall be in addition to the revocation of the offender's license .
(2) Any person who, by himself or through another , di-
rectly or indirectly , violates subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 243.020 shall ,
for the first offense , be fined not less than one hundred nor more
than two hundred dollars , or be imprisoned in the county jail
or workhouse for not more than six months , or both ; for the sec-
ond offense he shall be fined not less than two hundred nor more
than five hundred dollars , or be imprisoned in the county jail
or workhouse for not more than six months , or both ; and for the
third and each subsequent offense, he shall be fined not less than
five hundred nor more than five thousand dollars , or be im-
prisoned in the penitentiary for not more than five years , or
both.
(3 ) Any person who violates subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
243.020 shall be fined not less than one hundred nor more than
five hundred dollars .
(4) Any person who violates KRS 243.620 with respect to
a license issued under KRS 243.050 shall be fined not less than
fifty nor more than five hundred dollars .
(5) Any person who violates any of the provisions of KRS
243.680 to 243.700 shall be fined not less than five hundred nor
more than one thousand dollars .
(6) Any person who fails to pay the taxes imposed by KRS
243.680 to 243.700 within fifteen days after they have become
due , shall pay a penalty of twenty per cent on the amount of the
tax due.
(7) Any person who violates any of the provisions of
KRS 243.720 or 243.730 or any regulation issued thereunder shall
be fined not less than fifty dollars nor more than one thousand
dollars , or be imprisoned for not less than thirty days nor more
than one year , or both .
(8) Any person required to pay any tax imposed by KRS
243.720 or 243.730 , who fails to pay the tax when due shall pay
a penalty equal to twenty per cent of the amount of the tax due
and delinquent together with interest at six per cent per annum .
( 9) If after withdrawing distilled spirits under KRS
243.870 any person sells same , or any part thereof , or places same

ARTICLE VI
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
When the Department of Revenue claims that a decedent
was domiciled in Kentucky at the time of death and the taxing
authorities of another state or states make a similar claim with
respect to their state or states , the Commissioner of Revenue may
enter into a written agreement with such taxing authorities and
the executor , administrator or trustee , fixing the sum acceptable
to the department in full settlement of the inheritance or estate
taxes imposable under Chapter 140 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes . Such agreement shall also fix the sum acceptable to
such other state or states in full settlement of the death taxes
imposable by such state or states .
If the aggregate amount payable under such agreement to
the states involved is less than the maximum sum allowable as
a credit to the estate against the federal estate tax imposed
thereon , then the executor , administrator or trustee shall also pay
to the State of Kentucky as an estate tax so much of the differ-
ence between such aggregate amount and the amount of such
credit as the amount payable to Kentucky under the agreement
bears to such aggregate amount .
Section 2. Section 395.230 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is repealed .
ARTICLE VII
Section 1. If any section , subsection , clause, sentence or
phrase of this Act which is reasonably separable from the re-
maining portions of this Act is for any reason held to be
unconstitutional , such decision shall not affect the remaining
portions of this Act . The legislature hereby declares that it
would have passed the remaining portion of this Act irrespective
of the fact that any such section, subsection , clause, sentence or
phrase of this Act be declared unconstitutional .

914 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

I of this Act there is hereby appropriated to the Department of
Revenue out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State Treas-
ary the sum of thirty- five thousand dollars for fiscal year 1960-61 .
Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 45.230 , any unexpended
or unencumbered balance of such appropriation for fiscal year
1960-61 remaining at the end of such fiscal year shall be carried
forward and be available for expenditure at any time during the
1961-62 fiscal year and no portion shall lapse to the General Ex-
penditure Fund until after the 1961-62 fiscal year .
Section 3. The provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of Section 14,
Section 16 , Section 18 , and Section 19 of Article I of this Act shall
become effective the first Monday in December , 1961 .
Section 4. The provisions of Section 20 and Section 23 of
Article I of this Act shall become effective March 1, 1961 .
Section 5. The provisions of Section 37 of Article I of this
Act shall become effective July 2, 1960 .
Section 6. The provisions of Article IV of this Act shall
become effective July 1, 1960 .
Section 7. All revenues accruing under this Act shall be
credited to the General Expenditure Fund , except Article IV.
Section 8. All revenues which accrue to the General Ex-
penditure Fund from Sections 2 and 3 of Article III of this Act
during 1960-61 and 1961-62 not otherwise appropriated are
hereby appropriated to the Capital Construction Fund to be used
under the provisions of Chapter 56 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .

nues of the local taxing districts of the Commonwealth , an emer-
gency is declared to exist , and Article II of this Act shall become

itspassage and approval by the Governor

CHAPTER 190
(Н. В. 162)
AN ACT relating to the terms of court in the nineteenth judicial district.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The preamble and subsection ( 19 ) of section
23.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
The regular term of each court shall commence in each
county at the following named time , and continue , if the business
requires it , the number of days hereinafter prescribed :
( 19) ( a) Nineteenth District : Bracken County , at
Brooksville , on the first Monday in March, the first Monday in
July , and the first Monday in November , eighteen juridicial days
each;
( b ) Fleming County , at Flemingsburg , on the first Mon-
day in February , the first Monday in June , and the first Monday
in October , eighteen juridical days each ;
( c) Mason County , at Maysville , on the first Monday in
January , the first Monday in May , and the first Monday in
September , twenty -four juridical days each .
Section 2. Whereas , the next term of court in Mason
county commences on the fourth Monday in March, an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall become effective
May 1, 1960 .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

CHAPTER 192
(Н. В. 184)
AN ACT permitting fiscal courts to provide liability and indemnity insur-
ance for benefits of patients of county operated hospitals and pre-
scribing certain policy conditions , and amending Section 67.186 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 67.186 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) The fiscal court of any county in which there is a
county operated hospital may provide for liability and indemnity
insurance for the benefit of the hospital against the negligence
of the employes of such hospital .
( 2) The insurance policies so purchased by the fiscal court
shall be purchased only from insurance companies authorized to
transact business in this state , and any such policy shall bind
the insurer to pay , subject to the terms and conditions of the
policy, any final judgment , not in excess of the policy limits ,
rendered against the insured hospital or hospital employes for
the death or injury of any patient , or damage to the property
of any patient , resulting from the negligence of the hospital,

oremployes

Section 7. ( 1 ) No distributor , processor, bulk milk handler ,
store or producer -handler , either directly or indirectly , or through
a subsidiary or affiliate corporation , or by any officer , director ,
stockholder , employe , partner , agent or representative thereof ,
shall for the purpose or with the effect of restraining , lessening or
destroying competition or injuring one or more competitors or in-
juring one or more persons dealing in milk, cottage cheese , or
frozen dairy products or to impair or prevent fair competition
in the sale of milk, cottage cheese or frozen dairy products in this
state , threaten or engage in any marketing practice established
to be unreasonable by the commission , including , but not lim-
ited to, the marketing practices or methods of doing business
described in this section .
(a) Advertise for sale any article or product at less than the
cost thereof to such vendor , or give , offer to give or advertise
the intent to give away any article or product , for the purpose of
injuring competitors and destroying competition , or with the
effect of otherwise injuring a competitor , or of destroying com-
petition , or of creating a monopoly .
(b) Discriminate in price in the sale of any milk, cottage
cheese , or frozen dairy products between different towns , cities ,
municipalities , counties , sections or communities , or portions
thereof or locations therein , in this state , by advertising , offering
to sell or selling milk, cottage cheese or frozen dairy products
at a lower price in one town , city municipality , county , section ,
or community , or portions thereof or locations therein , than in
another , after making allowance for difference , if any , in the
grade or quality , and in the actual cost of transportation to
point of delivery .
(c) Give , or offer to give , or allow any secret rebate , un-
earned discount , free service or services , financial aid , free equip

CHAPTER 193 927

connection with the sale or consignment of milk, cottage cheese

(d) The provisions of subsections (a), (b) and (c) shall not

Section 9. ( 1 ) The commission is authorized and em-
powered :
( a) To call together milk dealers or handlers and pro-
ducer -handlers for the purpose of inquiry into marketing prac-
tices prevailing from time to time in this state , and if need be ,
to conduct investigations and/or hearings in connection with
such practices for the purpose of making findings relative to
any marketing practice found to exist within this state involving
the sale or distribution of milk, cottage cheese , or frozen dairy
products covered by this Act, and thereafter to promulgate rea-
sonable rules or regulations for the elimination of any such
practice found to be contrary to the provisions of this Act ; and
( b ) To establish as a public record the minimum prices
applicable to the milk deliveries of producers to a processor or a
distributor . Such prices shall be used solely for the purpose of
computing the cost of a processor or a distributor pursuant to
Section 2 ( 12 ) ( b ) hereof . These minimum prices shall , in the
discretion of the commission , be established for a processor or a
distributor located or distributing milk or milk products in mar-
keting areas as defined by counties or other appropriate geo-
graphical subdivision .
(2 ) Upon receipt of a written complaint filed with the
commission by any milk dealer or handler , producer -handler or
recognized association thereof , the commission may initiate pro-
ceedings, for the purpose of hearing and investigating evidence
of the failure or refusal of any person to comply with the pro-
visions of this Act or the rules , regulations and orders of the
commission issued thereunder after first giving five days' notice
to the alleged violator as to the date and time fixed for the
hearing and the nature of the charge or charges . Each witness
required to attend the hearing shall be entitled to receive mileage
customarily paid to the witnesses in the courts of the Common-
wealth . The commission shall not be bound by technical rules
of evidence , except as may be required by the applicable ad-
ministrative procedure laws of the Commonwealth .

CHAPTER 193 929
(3 ) The commission shall have the power during the
course of an investigation or hearing to issue subpoenas requir-
ing the attendance and testimony of, or the production of books
and records by, any person believed to have information of
importance to the hearing or investigation , or possessing any
books or records of importance to the hearing or investigation .
(4) Any person who refuses to obey a subpoena issued
by the commission , or refuses to be sworn or affirmed , or tes-

may be punished as for contempt of court . For this purpose , an
application may be made by the commission to the court of the
circuit within whose territorial jurisdiction the hearing or in-
vestigation is being conducted .
(5 ) Every rule , regulation or order of the commission
shall be posted for public inspection in the main office of the
commission and copies of such a rule , regulation or order shall
be made available and mailed to all licensees and producer
groups and organizations affected thereby . Such rule , regulation
or order of the commission , when duly posted or when properly
served, shall have the force and effect of law.

Section 12. ( 1 ) Every milk dealer or handler who owns or
operates facilities at which milk , cottage cheese and frozen dairy
products are received , or processed for distribution or sale within
the Commonwealth , or who sells milk, cottage cheese or frozen
dairy products within the Commonwealth , shall make application
for a license as a milk dealer or handler with the commission
within thirty days after this Act takes effect, or prior to engaging
in business , and annually thereafter , on or before January 1, upon
a form to be prescribed by the commission . Such application
shall be accompanied by a license fee of one dollar. Renewal
fee shall be one dollar . Any licensee failing to renew his license
before the expiration date thereof shall be penalized two dollars
in addition to his license fee .

to any other license or licenses which are now or may hereafter
be required by law.

tity of milk , cottage cheese , and frozen dairy products , not in
excess of an equivalent of two thousand pounds of whole milk
in any one day , shall be exempted from the license require-
ment .
(4) Licenses may not be assigned or transferred .

CHAPTER 194
(Н. В. 216)
AN ACT relating to the merger or consolidation of cities . This Act shall
be known as the Davis - Lyon Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Any two or more contiguous cities in the Com-
monwealth may merge or consolidate into one city by a majority
vote of each city at elections called for such purpose .
Section 2. The legislative bodies of each of the cities de-
siring to merge or consolidate shall enact ordinances proposing
the merger or consolidation and calling elections in the respective
cities to determine the desire of the voters .
Section 3. Such elections shall be held not less than thirty
days nor more than sixty days after the last of said ordinances
is enacted . The qualifications of voters and all other matters in
regard to the election shall be governed by the general election
laws . The question shall be submitted in substantially the fol-
lowing form :
Are you in favor of merging or consolidating the city of
and the city of into one city, to be
known as the city of ----- -?
yes----
no ----

orconsolidation

ofthe
results
ofsuch election said cities shall become one city
ofthe
class
ofthe largest
ofthe old cities
, but
if
amajority
ofthe legal
voters
ineither city vote
"No
",the merger
orconsolidation shall
fail
.

5. The members
ofthe legislative bodies
ofthe
merging cities shall continue

inthe new

ofoffice expire
, and shall con-
stitute
acombined legislative body

.

authorized for the payment of interest and the retirement of

governing body in accordance with the laws under which the

allbonded obligations
ofthe
old city have been retired
.

7. An emergency

CHAPTER 196
(Н. В. 301 )
AN ACT relating to benefits of members of the police or fire departments
in cities of the fourth and fifth classes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 95.773 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
If any member of the police or fire departments , while in
the performance of his duty, becomes temporarily , totally dis-
abled , physically or mentally , for services by reason of service in
such departments , the board of trustees shall order the payment
to such disabled member , monthly during such disability , not to
exceed one year , from such pension fund, not exceeding sixty
dollars per month , the said amount to be determined by the
board of trustees , provided such member , during the same period ,
is paid no salary as such member . If any member of the police
or fire departments , while in the performance of his duty, be-
comes mentally or physically permanently disabled by reason of
service in either of his departments so as to render necessary his
retirement from service in either of said departments , said board
of trustees shall retire such disabled member from service in
either of said departments , provided no such retirement on ac

said disability while on duty in the service of said police or fire
department , and upon such retirement the board of trustees shall
order the payment to such disabled member of such police or
fire department , monthly , from such pension fund, a sum equal
to one-half of the monthly salary such member was receiving at
the date of his retirement , provided such member has had twelve
or more years' constant service in either of said departments . Any
member of the said police or fire departments who has less than
twelve years of constant service in the said department and be-
comes disabled for service and eligible for retirement under this
section shall receive monthly such sum from such pension fund
fixed by the board of said trustees any amount not to exceed one-

partment was receiving in monthly salary at the date of his

CHAPTER 197
(H. R. 63
)
AJOINT RESOLUTION authorizing and directing the fencing of the
cemetery and marking the grave of Jenny Wiley
.

940 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , Jenny Wiley, Eastern Kentucky's most famous borderland
heroine , is buried in an open pasture in Johnson County ; and
WHEREAS , there is no evidence this grave has received suitable atten-
tion since her interment in 1831 ; and
WHEREAS , this courageous pioneer mother forged onward even in the
face of the savage Indian massacre of her family and horrors of
Indian captivity ; and
WHEREAS , in this cemetery rows of graves of pioneers lie in the same
state of neglect ; and
WHEREAS , there would have been no Kentucky -no conquering the
western woods but for such valiant pioneer efforts ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. That the Department of Conservation , Division
of Parks be directed to mark suitably the grave of Jenny Wiley ,
at a cost not exceeding one thousand dollars .
Section 2. That the Department of Highways be directed
to fence the cemetery in which the grave of Jenny Wiley lies ,
according to department specifications at a cost not to exceed
five hundred dollars .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 198
(S. B. 136 )
AN ACT relating to sewer construction district and an alternate method
of financing same .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Any sewer construction district created in the
manner provided in KRS 76.300 to 76.325 may , if the commis-
sioners of such district deem it advisable , use the authority and
procedures granted to incorporated municipalities by KRS
107.010 to 107.220 .
When applied to sewer construction districts , terms used
in KRS 107.010 to 107.220 shall be construed to mean the follow-
ing: "city" means "district " ; " ordinance " means "resolution ” ;

CHAPTER 199 941
“clerk " means "secretary of district "; "governing body " means
"district commissioners "; "mayor or chairman of board of di-
rectors " means " chairman of the district " .
Section 2. The provisions of Section 1 of this Act shall
not repeal or reduce any existing rights or duties of sewer con-
struction districts or the commissioners thereof , but shall con-
stitute an additional method of financing which may be used
in conjunction with KRS 76.300 to 76.385 , or may be an alternate
method .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

ofnot less than five nor more
than fourteen cents on each one hundred dollars worth of

ofsuch

operating the college

CHAPTER 203 949
be construed to be school taxes and shall be in addition to all
other taxes authorized by law to be used for municipal or school
purposes .
Section 3. ( 1 ) The fiscal court of a county containing a city
of the second class in which is located a municipal college , having
obtained the authorization of the legislative body of such city for
the college to accept tax support from a municipal college
support district and for appointment of three members of the
college board of trustees by the governing body of the district ,
may establish a municipal college support district consisting of
the territory in the county outside the limits of the city in which
is located the college. The members of the fiscal court shall
constitute the governing body of the district . Subject to the
provisions of subsection (2 ), the district may levy a tax of not
less than five nor more than fourteen cents on each one hundred
dollars of the assessed valuation of all property in the district .
The funds raised by this tax shall be used solely to support the
college .
(2) A certified copy of the order of the fiscal court creat-
ing the district shall be filed by the governing body of the district
with the county clerk at least forty-five days before a regular
election , and the clerk shall cause the question whether the
tax is to be imposed to be placed upon the ballots to be given
to voters residing in the district . The question shall be so
phrased as to ask the voter whether he favors the imposition of a
tax of not less than five nor more than fourteen cents on each one
hundred dollars of the assessed valuation of all property in the
district for the purpose of supporting the municipal college in
the second class city in the county . If a majority of those voting
on the question favor the imposition of the tax, the governing
body of the district shall levy the tax .
(3 ) The sheriff shall collect the taxes due the district at
the same time and in the same manner in which he collects the
state and county ad valorem tax. He shall be allowed a fee not
to exceed four per cent for collection of this tax. The money col-
lected shall be paid to the college board of trustees .
Section 4. The board of trustees of a municipal college in
a city of the second class may acquire , by purchase or gift, lands
and improvements for the purpose of expanding the plant and

950 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
extending the usefulness of the college, and when unable to agree
with the owner of land and improvements necessary for the pur-
poses of the college may proceed to condemn the land and im-
provements . The condemnation proceedings shall be conducted
in the manner provided by KRS 416.230 to 416.310 .
