CHAPTER 204
(S. B. 250)
AN ACT to define , license , and regulate the business of making loans or
advances in the amount or of the value of eight hundred dollars
or less , secured or unsecured, at a greater rate of interest , con-
sideration or charges than six per centum per annum , prescrib-
ing the rates of interest and charges therefor and penalties for the
violation thereof , regulating the assignment of wages or salaries ,
earned or to be earned, when given as security for any such loan
or as consideration for the payment of eight hundred dollars
or less , providing for insurance and limitations thereon , providing
for the administration of this Act , authorizing the making of ex-
aminations and investigations and the publication of reports thereof ,
providing for a review of decisions and findings of the Commissioner
of Banking and repealing Chapter 288 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes as amended , and all Acts and parts of Acts , whether general ,
special or local , which relate to the same subject matter as this Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Construction of Act . (1) As used in this Act,
unless the context requires otherwise :
( a ) “Director ” means the Commissioner of Banking ;
(b) "Licensee ” means a person licensed under this Act ; and
( c ) "Person " means an individual , partnership , association ,
trust, corporation and any other legal entity .
(2) This Act shall not apply to any person doing business
under and as permitted by any law of this state or of the United
States relating to banks , savings banks , trust companies , trust ,
banking and title insurance companies , building and loan associa-
tions , cooperative marketing associations , credit unions , loan and
investment companies , or licensed pawnbrokers . This Act does
not apply to the purchase or acquisition , directly or indirectly ,
of notes , chattel mortgages, installment or conditional sales con-
tracts , embodying liens or evidencing title retention arising from

CHAPTER 204 951
the bona fide sale of goods or services by a seller of such goods
or services.
Section 2. License required for consumer loan companies .
No person shall , without first obtaining a license from the direc-
tor, engage in the business of making loans in the amount or of
the value of eight hundred dollars or less at a greater rate of
interest, or consideration therefor than six per cent per annum .
Section 3. Form of application ; contents . ( 1) Each ap-
plication for a license under this Act shall be made in writing,
under oath or affirmation , in such form as the director prescribes .
(2) The application shall contain :
(a) in the case of an individual , his name and the address
of his residence and place of business ;
(b) in the case of a partnership or association , the name
and address of every member thereof and the address of the place
where the business is to be conducted ;
( c) in the case of a corporation , the names and addresses
of the officers and directors thereof and the address of the place
where the business is to be conducted ; and
( d) such additional information as the director prescribes .
Section 4. Fees . Each applicant at the time of making
application shall pay fifty dollars to the director as a fee for in-
vestigating the application and the additional sum of two hun-
dred and fifty dollars as an annual license fee for the period
terminating on the last day of the current calendar year . If the
application is filed after June 30 in any year , the payment shall be
one hundred and twenty -five dollars as a license fee in addition
to the fee for investigation . In addition to the annual license fee
every licensee hereunder shall pay the same fees for each exami-
nation as are allowed by statute for the examination of banks .
Section 5. Approval or rejection of application ; time limit .
(1) The director shall , after investigation , issue to the applicant
a license to make loans in accordance with this Act , if the
director:
(a) approves the form of the application ;
(b) finds that the financial responsibility , experience ,
character , and general fitness of the applicant , and of the mem

952 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bers thereof if the applicant is a partnership or association , and
of the officers and directors thereof if the applicant is a corpora-
tion, are such as to command the confidence of the community
and to warrant the belief that the business of the applicant will
be operated honestly , fairly, and efficiently in accordance with
the purposes of this Act ;
(c) finds that permitting such applicant to engage in such
business will promote the convenience and advantage of the com-
munity; and
(d) finds that the applicant has complied with Section 4
hereof.
(2) If the director does not so find, he shall not issue a
license and shall notify the applicant of the denial and return the
sum paid by the applicant as a license fee, retaining the fifty
dollars investigation fee to cover the cost of investigating the
application .
(3 ) The director shall approve or deny every such applica-
tion for license within sixty days from the filing thereof with the
fees unless the time is extended by a written agreement between
the applicant and the director .
(4) All findings of the director , together with a summary of
the evidence supporting them, shall be filed in the office of the
director as public records, open to public inspection .
Section 6. License ; contents ; to be displayed ; change in
place of business . (1) Every license shall state the address of the
office at which the business is to be conducted , the name of the
licensee , and if the licensee is a partnership or association , the
names of the members thereof , and if a corporation , the date and
place of
itsincorporation
.
(2) The license shall
bekept displayed
inthe office
ofthe
licensee and shall not be transferable
orassignable
. Not more
than one place
ofbusiness shall be maintained under the same

tothe
same licensee upon compliance with
allthe provisions
ofthis Act
for each license
; provided
, however
, nothing herein shall
be

alicense for any place
ofbusiness devoted
to

oradministrative purposes

3) Whenever
alicensee desires
tochange his place

toanother location within the same county such licensee

CHAPTER 204 953
shall give written notice to the director , who , if he finds that
the interest of the community will be served thereby , shall
endorse on the license a transfer to the new place of business ,
with the date of transfer , which endorsement shall be authority
for the operation of such business at the new location . No change
in the place of business of a licensee to a location outside of the
original county shall be permitted under the same license.
Section 7. Regulations as to place of business . (1 ) No
licensee shall conduct the business authorized by this Act in any
office , room , or place of business in which any other business is
solicited or engaged in , or in association or conjunction therewith ,
except upon a written authorization from the director .
(2) Nothing in this Act shall be construed to limit the loans
of any licensee to residents of the community in which the
li-

ofbusiness

954 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(2 ) The director may suspend any license for a period not
exceeding thirty days , pending investigation of possible grounds
for revocation under sub-paragraphs ( b ) or (c ) of subsection ( 1 )
of this section.
(3) Before the revocation of a license under subsection
( 1 ) , or suspension under subsection ( 2 ) , the licensee shall be
given ten days' written notice of the contemplated revocation or
suspension of his license, the grounds therefor stated specifically ,
and a reasonable opportunity to be heard .
(4) The director may reinstate suspended licenses or issue
new licenses to a licensee whose license has been revoked if no
fact or condition then exists which clearly would have warranted
him in refusing originally to issue such license under this Act .
(5) Any licensee may surrender any license by delivering
it to the director together with written notice that he thereby
surrenders such license.
Section 10. Written order of denial or revocation ; right of
appeal. Whenever the director denies any application for a
license under the provisions of this Act or revokes any license
issued pursuant to this Act, he shall forthwith file in his office a
written order to that effect , stating his findings with respect
thereto and the reasons for his action . The director shall also
forthwith serve upon the applicant for license or licensee a copy
of the order , and the applicant or licensee may appeal to the cir-
cuit court of Franklin county , within thirty days after the service
of a copy of the order .
Section 11. Financial condition of licensee . Each licensee
shall have at all times a net worth of at least twenty thousand
dollars and shall maintain at all times assets of at least twenty
thousand dollars either in liquid form available for or actually
used in the making of loans under this Act at the location speci-
fied in the license .
Section 12. Prohibited advertisements and statements . No
licensee shall advertise, print, display , publish , distribute , broad-
cast or televise, or permit to be advertised , printed , displayed ,
published , distributed , broadcast or televised , any statement or
representation with regard to the rates, terms , or conditions for

CHAPTER 204 955
making loans in the sum of eight hundred dollars or less , which
is false , misleading , or deceptive .
Section 13. Interest rate ; maximum charges allowed licen-
see; loans not to be divided . (1 ) Every licensee may lend any

(3) Lien upon real estate as security prohibited. No

Section 19. Licensee to make annual report . Each licensee
shall annually on or before April 15, file with the director a re-
port for the preceding calendar year . Such report shall give in-
formation with respect to the financial condition of such licensee
and shall include : the name and address of the licensee ; balance
sheets at the beginning and end of the accounting period ; a
statement of income and expenses for said period ; a reconcilia-
tion of surplus or net earnings with the balance sheets ; a sched-
ule of assets used and useful in the consumer loan business ; an
analysis of charges, and types of security on loans of eight hun-
dred dollars or less ; an analysis of delinquent accounts ; analysis
of suits, repossessions and sales of chattels and such other relevant
information as the director may reasonably require concerning
the business and operations during the preceding calendar year
of each licensed place of business conducted in the state . In the
event any person or affiliated group of corporations hold more
than one license in the state , he or they may file a composite
annual report in lieu of separate reports for each licensed office .

CHAPTER 204 963
Such report shall be made under oath and in the form prescribed
by the director , who shall make and publish annually an analysis
and recapitulation of such reports .
Section 20. Records , extent and preservation of. Each
licensee shall keep and use in his business and shall preserve for
at least two years after making the final entry therein , such books ,
accounts , records, or card systems in accordance with sound ac-
counting principles and practices to enable the director to de-
termine whether the licensee is complying with the provisions
of this Act , and with the regulations made pursuant thereto .
Section 21. Director to make regulations ; examination of
licensee . (1 ) The provisions of this Act shall be enforced by
the director , who may, after notice to licensees and a hearing ,
promulgate regulations , referenced to the section or sections
which set forth the legislative standards they interpret or apply ,
for the proper conduct of the business licensed under this Act .
All regulations of general application shall state the date of pro-
mulgation and the effective date. A copy of every such regula-
tion shall be sent to all licensees before the effective date thereof
and a copy shall be kept in an indexed permanent book in the
office of the director as a public record .
(2) The director shall make an annual examination of the
affairs , business , office , and records of every licensee , and such
further examinations or investigations as he deems necessary for
the purpose of discovering violations of this Act or of securing
information necessary for
itsproper enforcement

.
(
1) No person

964 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing, contracting for or receiving any interest , discount or consid-
eration greater than six per cent per annum upon any loan in the
amount of or the value of eight hundred dollars or less .
(2 ) Any loan in the amount of eight hundred dollars or
less for which there has been charged , contracted for or received
a greater rate of interest , discount or consideration , except as
provided for by statute , is against the public policy of this state.
( 3 ) No such loan made outside this state shall be enforced
in this state and every person participating therein in this state
shall be subject to the provisions of this Act , but this section does
not apply to loans legally made in any state, country , common-
wealth , territory or district .
Section 23. Review . In addition to any other available
remedy, any person considering himself aggrieved by any act or
omission of the director may , within thirty days from the date of
such act or failure to act, bring an action in the circuit court in
and for Franklin county to review such act or omission . The
hearing before the court shall be based on the record before the
director and his findings , if any, and on such new evidence as
may be introduced .
Section 24. Impairment of contracts . This Act or any part
thereof may be modified , amended or repealed so as to effect the
cancellation or authorization of any license or right of a licensee
hereunder , provided that such modification , amendment or repeal
shall not impair or affect the obligation of any pre - existing lawful
contract between any licensee and any borrower .
Section 25. Pre -existing licensees . Any person having a
license under Chapter 288 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes in
force when this Act becomes effective shall be deemed to have
a license under this Act . The director shall , within a reasonable
time after the effective date of this Act , issue new licenses to such
persons .
Section 26. Penalties . ( 1) Any person who shall engage
in the business regulated by this Act without first securing a
license therefor shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon con-
viction thereof shall be punished by a fine of not less than five
hundred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars . Any such
loan contract made in violation of this Act shall be void and the

CHAPTER 205 965
lender shall have no right to collect any principal , charges or
recompense whatsoever .
( 2 ) Any licensee who , after a cease and desist order shall
have been issued by the director , shall violate any provision of
this Act shall , upon conviction thereof , be punished by a fine of
not less than one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred
dollars , in addition to the penalty prescribed in Section 13 hereof ,
if applicable , provided , however , this section shall not be deemed
to limit the power of the director to revoke any license as pro-
vided in Section 9 hereof .
Section 27. Repeal . (1) Chapter 288 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby repealed .
(2) Nothing in this Act shall be construed so as to impair
or affect the obligation of any contract of loan between any licen-
see under Chapter 288 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, and any
obligor , which was lawfully entered into prior to the effective
date of this Act .
Section 28. Separability . If any one or more of the clauses ,
sentences , phrases, words , sections, provisions or parts of this
Act be adjudged to be unconstitutional or invalid for any reason
by a court of competent jurisdiction , such shall not impair , affect
or invalidate the remainder of this Act , which shall remain in
force and effect .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

Section 4. Any action authorized by the provisions of this
Act to be accomplished by a city or county may be undertaken
by the city or county jointly . The provisions of this Act may
only be accomplished jointly by city or county where a joint
resolution is passed , both by the fiscal court and the governing
body of the city and in such instance where a joint resolution
fails , the provisions of this Act may be accomplished separately
by the city and the county .
Section 5. ( 1 ) A city or county may require any person
to obtain permits before commencing construction , alteration or
repairs of any electrical wiring, and require such inspection as
it deems necessary for the safety of life and property .
(2 ) Reasonable standards for the construction , alteration
and repair of any electrical wiring shall be adopted by the city
or the county and shall have as a minimum standard the require-
ments of the national electric code which is incorporated into
the state standards of safety . The standards so adopted by the
city or the county shall be used by the electrical inspector in
making his inspections .
Section 6. ( 1 ) A city or county may require all electrical
contractors and electricians , except those who have had at least
five years of experience as an electrical contractor and/or
electrician , to be examined by an examining board before be-
ing issued a license to engage in their occupation . An elec-
trician desiring to become a contractor may apply for an ex-

(4) In order to administer this Act , the board may issue

and how it shall apply in their area ; conduct and make regula-

(5 ) Electrical contractors and electricians may be required
to pay a reasonable fee before issuance of a license to them .
Section 7. A city or county may fix as a penalty for the
violation of an ordinance or resolution passed under the provisions
of this Act , a fine of not less than ten dollars nor more than one
hundred dollars , or imprisonment for not more than fifty days ,
or both .

CHAPTER 210
(S. B. 99)
AN ACT relating to auxiliary police forces in cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The legislative body of a city of the second and third class
may by ordinance provide for the establishment or abolishment
of an auxiliary police force to perform special duties within the
city on terms it deems proper . The ordinance shall prescribe the
number of officers and men of such force and the manner of their
appointment , and rules and regulations governing the powers
and duties of members of such force .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

Said declaration of taking shall contain , or have annexed
thereto, the following :
(a) a statement of the authority under which, and the
public use for which, said lands are taken ;
(b) a description of the lands taken sufficient for identi-
fication thereof ;
(c) a statement of the estate or interest in said lands taken
for said public use ;
(d) a plat showing the lands taken .
(4) At any time after the report of the commissioners has
been made , the metropolitan sewer district may file said decla-
ration of taking and make a deposit with the clerk of the court of
the sum of money stated in the award of the commissioners ap-
pointed . Title to said lands in fee simple or such lesser estate as
is specified in said declaration or to said personal property shall
then vest in such metropolitan sewer district , and the right to

982 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
just compensation for said land or interest therein or personal
property shall vest in the persons entitled thereto . Said compen-
sation shall be ascertained and awarded in said proceeding as
otherwise provided in KRS 416.230 to 416.310 inclusive .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 213
(S. B. 153)
AN ACT amending Section 212.710 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to state aid for city -county health departments .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 212.710 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Any city-county department of health established under
KRS 212.350 or 212.640 shall be entitled to the same state aid
as is provided for county and district health departments under
KRS 212.120 , upon notice of the establishment of the department
being given to the State Board of Health as provided in KRS
212.120 .
This Act shall not become effective until July 1, 1962 .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 214
(S. B. 157 )
AN ACT relating to the taxation of liquid fuels used in motor boats .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. A new section is created in Chapter 138 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who buys and uses any liquid fuel for the
purpose of operating or propelling motor boats shall be reim-
bursed and repaid ninety per cent of the tax paid by him pursuant
to the provisions of KRS 138.210 to 138.340 upon presenting to
the Department of Revenue an application made under oath ,
accompanied by the original invoices showing the payment of
such purchases , including the liquid fuel tax thereon . The ap-
plication shall set forth the total amount of the liquid fuel pur

CHAPTER 214 983
chased and used by the applicant in the operation or propulsion
of motor boats .
( 2) ( a ) When liquid fuel on which the tax has been
paid pursuant to the provisions of KRS 138.210 to 138.340 has
been used for the purpose of operating any motor boat and was
delivered directly to the fuel tanks installed in or attached to
the motor boat , the purchaser of the liquid fuel so used shall
be reimbursed to the extent of ninety per cent of the tax paid .
No tax shall be refunded except that paid upon such fuel used
exclusively in motor boat motors .
(b ) No person shall be entitled to a refund hereunder un-
less he shall have first filed with the Department of Revenue a
bond with approved surety in the amount of not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars to be de-
termined by the Department of Revenue and upon the payment
to the Commonwealth of any refunds to which he was not en-
titled.
(3) All refunds claims authorized by this section shall be
filed with the Department of Revenue within four months after
the date on which the liquid fuel shall have been purchased as
shown by the invoice . Where the application for refund is mailed
to the Department of Revenue , the date of mailing as shown
by the postmark shall be taken as the time and date of filing
with the department .
Section 2. A new section is created in Chapter 138 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes to read as follows :
(1) Any person who shall change or alter the date , name,
gallonage or other information shown on any invoice used to
support any claim for refund authorized in this Act shall forfeit
the right to such refund .
(2) Any person , firm or corporation who shall make any
false statement in connection with an application for the refund
of any money , as provided in this Act, or who shall collect or
cause to be repaid to him or to any person any such taxes with-
out being entitled to the same under the provisions of this Act
shall be fined not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than
one thousand dollars .

984 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 3. Refunds shall be made only on gasoline pur-
chased from locations designated by the Department of Revenue .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

CHAPTER 216
(
S.B. 185
)
AN ACT relating to the selection of jurors for police courts in counties
containing cities of the second class
.

CHAPTER 217 987
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 26.405 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1) In any county containing a city of the second class,
the judge of the police court shall each year select a list of not
less than 300 qualified citizens and residents from the tax records
of the city for service as police court jurors .
(2) The judge shall place the name and address of each
person so selected on a small slip of paper , each slip being as
near the same size and appearance as practicable . Each slip
shall then be deposited in a revolving drum . The drum shall be
emptied once each year prior to the depositing of slips selected
from the new list . The drum shall be kept locked and the key
to said drum shall be kept by the clerk of the police court .
(3 ) Whenever a jury is needed for jury service in police
court , the judge shall select twelve names from the drum for
service on the jury and said persons shall be summoned by a police
officer of the city.
(4) In the event that the jury panel is exhausted prior to
the expiration of the time when the judge must draw a new list ,
then the judge may compel any police officer of the city to
summon jurors whose names he may obtain from the tax rolls
of the city.
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 217
(S. B. 202 )
AN ACT relating to closing of public ways .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 94.360 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) The city legislative body in cities of the second , third,
fourth , fifth and sixth classes shall , by itself or through a depart-
ment of public works or a superintendent of public works as
provided by law, have and exercise exclusive control over the
public ways , landings , wharves and public grounds of the city.

988 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
It may establish , open , alter , widen , extend, close , grade , pave ,
repave , clean , sprinkle and repair the same , prevent and remove
encroachments thereon or obstructions thereof , put drains and
sewers in the same , regulate or prohibit the building of vaults
and areas under the sidewalks , and enforce and regulate connec-
tion with sewers , gas and water mains and conduits of all kinds
laid in or under the public ways of the city.
( 2) Upon the adoption of an ordinance by the city legis-
lative body closing the whole or any portion of a public way , the
city attorney , or city solicitor in cities of the second , third , fourth,
fifth and sixth classes , shall institute an action in the circuit
court to have it closed . All the owners of ground in the square
or lots divided by that public way, or the portion proposed to
be closed , shall be made defendants . If all defendants are com-
petent to act for themselves and fail to object to the closing
prayed for, the court shall render a decree accordingly , but if
any defendants object , or are under disability other than cover-
ture , the court shall empanel a jury, which shall hear evidence
and determine the amount of compensation in the form of dam-
ages to be paid to each defendant . The court shall then direct
that the public way be closed upon payment to each defendant
of the damages awarded to him, or , if any defendant refuses or
is unable to accept payment , upon payment into court of the
amount awarded that defendant . The court shall give these
proceedings precedence over other cases .
( 3) When any parcel of ground has been laid off as a
public way , and opened to the unrestricted use of the general
public for five consecutive years , it shall be conclusively pre-
sumed to have been dedicated to the city as a public way , sub-
ject to acceptance by the city. The city may , at any time after
the expiration of five years from the time the parcel of ground is
laid off and opened to the public, pass an ordinance declaring
it so dedicated , and accepting the dedication , whereupon it shall
be a public way of the city for
allpurposes
. The lack
of
anactual
dedication
tothe city
,
or
of
arecord title
onthe part
ofthe city
,
shall
be
nodefense against the collection
ofany tax that may be
levied against property abutting thereon for the payment
ofthe
cost

ofthe
city
. Nothing herein shall
beconstrued
torequire the expiration

CHAPTER 218 989
of five years to raise a presumption of dedication in any case
where , under any rule of law now in force in this state , a dedi-

CHAPTER 218
(S. B. 203)
AN ACT relating to the architectural profession .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 323.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires otherwise :
(1) " Board " means the State Board of Examiners and
Registration of Architects of Kentucky ;
(2) An " architect " is a natural person proficient in the art
of planning , designing , specifying , supervising and administering
the construction of buildings and who is licensed to practice
architecture ;
(3) The "practice of architecture " consists of performing
or offering to perform any professional service involving con-
sultations , planning , designing , specifying , responsible super-
vision or administration of the construction of any building or
additions or alterations thereto ;
(4) A "building " is a structure erected as a shelter for
human beings , their activities and possessions .
Section 2. Section 323.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Except as otherwise provided hereinafter , no person shall
practice architecture in the Commonwealth of Kentucky without
first obtaining a license under the provisions of this chapter , it
being the purpose of this chapter to safeguard the life , health ,
property and welfare of the public .
Section 3. Section 323.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) A nonresident licensed to practice architecture in his
own state or country but not in the Commonwealth and having

990 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
no established place of business in the Commonwealth may act
solely as a consulting associate of an architect licensed in the
Commonwealth .
(2) An architect acting solely as an officer or employe of
the United States Government is not required to be licensed in
the Commonwealth .
(3) A licensed professional engineer may prepare plans
and specifications for and supervise the construction of struc-
tures as an incident to the practice of his own profession .
(4) If the drawings and specifications are signed by the
authors thereof with the true titles of their occupations as may be
required by law , this chapter does not apply to :
( a) any building which is to be used for farm purposes
only ;
( b ) any building containing not more than 20,000 square
feet total floor area , except :
1. an asylum , hospital , nursing or convalescent home , or
home for the aged, regardless of capacity ;
2.

4.
a school or educational institution , regardless of capacity ;
a residential building containing more than twelve bed-
a place of assembly , regardless of capacity ;
5. a mercantile building having a capacity in excess of one
hundred persons ;
6. an industrial building having a capacity in excess of one
hundred persons ; and
7. an office building having a capacity in excess of one
hundred persons ;
( c) alterations to any building to which this chapter does
not apply , if the alterations do not involve changes affecting
structural safety .
Section 4. Section 323.050 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) To obtain a license to practice architecture in Ken-
tucky an applicant must satisfactorily pass an examination as
prescribed by the board , except as otherwise provided in this
chapter .

CHAPTER 218 991

lowing requirements :
(a) must be of good moral character ; and
(b) must be a citizen of the United States of America
and at least twenty -five years of age ; and either
(c) must be a graduate of an accredited school of
architecture with at least three years ' additional architectural
experience satisfactory to the board ; or
(d) must be a graduate of an accredited high school
or have had education equivalent thereto as determined by the
board , and , in addition , at least five years' varied architectural
experience satisfactory to the board .
(3) Examinations shall be given at least semi-annually , the
time and place to be established by the board .

Any person who is a licensed architect in another state or

where reciprocal licensing privileges satisfactory to the board

Section 12. Section 323.140 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

(1) The board , or any member thereof , may issue sub-
poenas to compel the attendance of witnesses and the production
of documents , and may administer oaths, take testimony , hear
proof and receive exhibits in evidence . In case of disobedience
to a subpoena the board may invoke the aid of the Franklin
Circuit Court . Any order or subpoena of such court requiring
the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the production
of documentary evidence may be enforced and shall be valid
anywhere in this state .
(2) At any hearing the accused may appear in person or
by counsel , produce evidence and witnesses on his own behalf ,
cross -examine witnesses , and examine such evidence and docu-

entitled , on application to the board , to the issuance of subpoenas

(3) The board shall not be bound by technical rules of

(4) A stenographic record of the proceedings shall be kept ,
but it shall not be necessary to transcribe shorthand notes unless
an appeal is taken from the order .
(5) The board's order shall be in writing and shall be ac-
companied by findings of fact and conclusions of law .
(6) A copy of an order suspending or revoking a license
shall be served upon the licensee by registered mail immediately
upon entry by the board . Any license that is suspended or re-
voked shall be surrendered to the board accompanied by the
licensee's certificate .

Section 2. Section 149.250 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes

(1) Any land which shall be acquired by any county for the
purpose of a permanent public forest under the provisions of

acquisition , shall be planted to trees within a period of ten years

raise a sufficient sum by taxation to reforest any such land within
the required period of time . All revenue derived from such
forest land by the sale of timber or otherwise shall accrue to
the use of the county owning and holding such land. Such reve-

court and the remainder of such revenue shall be used by the

otherwise may be expended by said board in the reasonable up-
keep and maintenance of such public forest .

CHAPTER 222

AN ACT relating to plumbers and plumbing .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 318.010 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
As used in this Act , unless the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Board " means the State Board of Health ;
(2 ) "Journeyman plumber " means a person who engages
or offers to engage , either as an occupation or otherwise , in the
construction , installation , alteration , maintenance , repair , re-
modeling or removal and replacement of plumbing under the su-
pervision , direction and responsibility of a master plumber ;
(3) "Master plumber " means a person who assumes re-
sponsible charge, supervision or direction of journeyman plumb-
ers , plumbers ' apprentices and other persons in the construction ,
installation or alteration of plumbing or who engages in, offers to
engage in or advertises or otherwise represents that he is per-
mitted or qualified to engage in the design , planning , superin-
tending , contracting for or responsible charge of plumbing ;
(4) "Plumbing " means the art of installing in buildings the
pipes for distributing the water supply , the fixtures for using
water and drainage pipes for removing waste water and sewage ,
together with fittings , appurtenances , and appliances of various
kinds ,
allwithin
oradjacent
tothe building
.

:
(a) the water service pipe which forms the connection
between the property line and the building

sewage from the building
tothe property line
orother points
of

of

areas , hospitals , nursing homes , hotels , motels , tourist courts ,
rooming houses , boarding houses , and other establishments fur-
nishing public sleeping accommodations ;
(7) “Maintenance man " means a person employed to
maintain and keep plumbing in good repair ;
(8) "Apprentice " means a person in the process of learn-
ing the plumbing trade who assists and is under the personal
supervision of a licensed master or licensed journeyman plumber ;
and
(9 ) "Farmstead " means a farm dwelling together with
other farm buildings and structures incident to the operation
and maintenance of the farm located outside the corporate limits
of a municipality and not connected to a public water supply .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) This Act applies and shall be in full force and effect in
counties containing a city of the first , second, or third class .
( 2 ) The local board of health of any county , including
those counties not containing a city of the first , second, or third
class , may adopt the State Plumbing Code promulgated by the
State Board of Health as a regulation for the county , including
the reasonable schedule of fees and charges fixed by the State
Board of Health as authorized by Section 18 ( 3 ) of this Act . After
adoption of the State Plumbing Code by a local board of health ,
such code shall apply and be in full force and effect in such
county and in all cities and towns therein .
( 3 ) The State Plumbing Code promulgated by the board
under the provisions of this Act applies and shall be in full force
and effect for all public buildings regardless of location in the
Commonwealth .
(4) This Act shall not apply to farmsteads.
Section 3. Section 318.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

Section 9. Section 318.060 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
An applicant who fails an examination shall be eligible upon
re - application for the next regular examination upon the pay-
ment of an additional application fee . Applications shall be can-
celled one year after receipt thereof , in the event the applicant
fails to appear for examination .
Section 10. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
The department may revoke or suspend any plumber's
license issued by it upon proof that the licensee has :
(1 ) Knowingly violated the provisions of this Act or the
Kentucky State Plumbing Code, or the rules and regulations of
the board;
( 2 ) Practiced fraud or deception in applying for or ob-
taining a license ;
(3 ) Is incompetent to perform services as a licensed mas-
ter plumber or a licensed journeyman plumber ;
(4) Permitted his license to be used directly or indirectly
by another to obtain or perform plumbing work or services ; or
(5) Is guilty of such other unprofessional or dishonorable
conduct of a character likely to deceive or defraud the public .
Section 11. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
(1 ) No license shall be suspended or revoked by the de-
partment unless a hearing has been conducted or an opportunity
afforded therefor , at which the holder of the license shall have
the right to be present in person or by counsel and to present
evidence and be heard in his own behalf . The holder of the li-
cense shall be given written notice of the hearing at least thirty
days prior to the date thereof . The notice shall state the time
and place of the hearing and set forth the ground or grounds for
the suspension or revocation in sufficient detail to apprise the

holder of the license of the charges preferred against him . Upon
failure of the holder of the license to respond to the complaint at
or before the time of the hearing thereon , the allegations set

fessed . Proceedings at such hearings shall be in accordance with
the rules and regulations of the board .
( 2 ) The department shall appoint and provide for a hear-
ing officer to conduct such hearings . The hearing officer shall
have authority to issue subpoenas for the attendance of witnesses
and the production of papers and records , administer oaths ,
schedule hearings and otherwise act in the name and on behalf

revoke licenses . He shall cause to be prepared and shall cer-

Section 12. Section 318.080 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

( 1 ) In order to conduct examinations for persons to qualify
as licensed master plumbers or journeyman plumbers , the depart-

of Engineering in the Department of Health , and three other per-

the plans . The construction , installation or alteration shall be
done in accordance with the approved plans .

Section 3. Section 249.260 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Every operator as defined in KRS 249.250 shall secure
a license from the department before engaging in business in
Kentucky . Written application shall be made to the department
stating the field of operation in which a license is desired . The
applicant shall then be examined by the examining board in the
field of operation specified in his application , and if found to be
competent and qualified , shall be issued a license in the specified
field , renewable annually without examination unless previously
revoked as hereinafter provided . The licensee must, however ,
be examined when application is made for a license in additional
fields of operation .
(2 ) Each operator shall register annually , and shall pay
an annual fee of one hundred dollars for each place of business
maintained in Kentucky . The registration and annual fee shall
also be paid by any owner maintaining a place of business outside
Kentucky but who engages in business within Kentucky . Every
nonresident owner shall designate a resident agent upon whom
service of notice of process may be made to enforce the pro-
visions of KRS 249.250 to 249.340 or any liabilities arising from
the operations hereunder .
(3) The " applicant or operator " is construed to mean the
person or persons who own or are in direct charge of and are
responsible for the conduct of the business named in the license .
(4) No applicant shall be eligible for examination unless
he has had at least two years' experience in the field in which
application is made , or has had in lieu thereof at least two years '
study in a recognized college or university , majoring in subjects
devoted to the professional field in which he proposes to engage .
Section 4. Each applicant for a license shall pay to the
department an examination fee of fifteen dollars which shall be
in full , and will entitle him to be examined in any or all fields
of operation . However , a subsequent application for a license in
any additional field requires the payment of an additional fifteen

CHAPTER 225 1019
dollar fee . If an applicant fails an examination in a particular
field, he shall have the right to take one additional examination
at a regularly scheduled date upon payment of a five dollar fee .
Section 5. Section 249.290 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The authorized representatives of the department shall have
the power of peace officers in the enforcement of KRS 249.250
to KRS 249.340 , and may enter upon public or private premises
for the purpose of inspection , and may examine during reason-
able hours the records , materials and equipment of any operator .
Section 6. Section 249.300 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
A license may be revoked by the board for any of the fol-
lowing causes :
( 1 ) The licensee, his solicitor or agent, making any ma-
terial misrepresentations for the purpose of defrauding any mem-
ber of the public, or using any method or material that is not
reasonably suited for the purpose for which it is employed , or
performing unsatisfactory work under his contract ;
( 2) Failure of a licensee to comply with any of the pro-
visions of KRS 249.250 to 249.340 , or any rule or regulation pro-
mulgated by the department ;
(3) Failure to pay any final judgment rendered against
any operator , his employe , solicitor or representative by reason
of liabilities resulting from activities under KRS 249.250 to KRS
249.340;
(4) Refusal to provide the department with reasonably
complete and accurate information upon request ;
(5) If license holder shall fail to make the registrations
and pay annual fees as required in KRS 249.260 .

Section 8. Advertisement by unlicensed operators pro-
hibited . It shall be unlawful for any person , firm , corporation ,
partnership or combination thereof , to advertise in any news-
paper , periodical , radio or in any other manner , that said person ,
firm , corporation , partnership or combination thereof is engaged
in the business of pest control or any of the fields thereof , unless
it has been first licensed in Kentucky .
Section 9. The board shall after denying an application
for a license , if petitioned by the applicant within ten days of
notification , or before suspending or revoking any license , set
the matter down for a hearing and shall at least fifteen days
prior to the date set for the hearing notify the applicant or
licensee in writing , which said notice shall contain an exact state-
ment of the charges made and the date and place of the hearing .
Such written notice may be served by delivery of it personally to
the applicant or licensee or by mailing it by registered mail to
his last known address . The hearing shall be at the time and
place prescribed by the board .
Section 10. ( 1 ) In the preparation and conduct of hearings
any member of the board shall have the power to administer
oaths and to require by subpoena the attendance and testimony
of witnesses and the production of the papers . The fees and
mileage shall be the same as prescribed by law for judicial pro-
cedure in civil cases . Any party to a hearing shall have the
right to the attendance of witnesses in his behalf upon making a
request thereof to the board and designating the person or per-
sons sought to be subpoenaed .
( 2 ) Testimony may be taken by depositions as in civil cases
and any person may be compelled to appear and depose in the
same manner as witnesses as hereinbefore provided .
(3 ) Any person who shall neglect or refuse to attend and
testify or to answer any lawful inquiry or to produce documen-
tary evidence , if in his power to do so , is in disobedience of a
subpoena or lawful requirement by the board , shall be subject
to prosecution under KRS 249.990 ( 6 ) .

CHAPTER 226
(S. B. 256)
AN ACT relating to financing of public improvements by assessments
against benefited properties according to the " assessed value " basis ;
amending and re -enacting KRS 107.020 , 107.030, 107.140 and 107.190 ;
enacting new sections in Chapter 107 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes, and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Legislative Purpose . It is the purpose of this
Act to extend permissive authority of municipalities for assess

CHAPTER 226 1023
ment financing of certain public improvements according to the
"assessed value " basis by ( a ) permitting such financing of sewage
treatment plants and appurtenances , as well as other sewer in-
stallations , ( b ) simplifying procedures under certain conditions ,
( c ) permitting assessment bonds for sewer projects to mature
over a period up to thirty years , and (d) amending and re - enacting
certain specified sections of Chapter 107 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes to make them consistent herewith .
Section 2. Section 107.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended and re-enacted as follows :
Definitions . ( 1 ) The term "governing body " , as used in this
chapter , means and includes the legislative body of any city,
whether the same be designated by applicable statutes as a
general council , a common council , a city council , a board of
trustees , a board of commissioners , or otherwise .
(2) The term " ordinance ” means and includes any ordi-
nance enacted in accordance with the general laws applicable
to ordinances of the class of city in question , and the form of
government thereof , and in accordance with the provisions of
this chapter .
( 3) The term "public way" means and includes streets ,
sidewalks , boulevards , avenues , roads , lanes , alleys , parkways ,
courts , terraces , and other courses of travel open to the general
public by whatsoever name designated .
(4) The terms "improvement " and "project " mean and
include ( a ) the new construction of public ways or the sub-
stantial reconstruction or widening thereof , (b ) the construction ,
installation or substantial reconstruction of sanitary , storm , or
combined sewers and appurtenances , ( c ) the new construction ,
enlargement or substantial reconstruction of sewage treatment
plants for rendering sewage less hazardous to public health ,
safety and general welfare or ( d) any combination of the same .
Bonds for improvements defined in ( b ) and ( c) of this sub-
section may be caused to mature as to principal in term or
serial maturities not to exceed thirty years from date of issue ,
instead of being limited to twenty years from date of issue as
otherwise provided in KRS 107.090 .
(5) The term "costs " as applied to any project undertaken
under this chapter includes the cost of labor , materials , and

1024 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
equipment necessary to complete the project in a satisfactory
manner , cost of land acquired , and every expense connected with
the project , including preliminary and other surveys , inspections
of the work, engineers ' fees and costs , attorneys' fees , preparation
of plans and specifications , publication of ordinances and notices ,
interest which will accrue on the bonds until the due date of
the first annual improvement assessment levied in connection
therewith , a sum equal to any discount in the sale of the bonds
(if discount bids are authorized and permitted by the governing
body ) , a reasonable allowance for unforeseen contingencies , the
printing of bonds , and other costs of financing which may
include the payment of a fee to a fiscal agent for advice and
assistance in the preparation and marketing of the bonds .
(6 ) The term "assessed value basis " means the plan for
the levying of annual improvement benefit assessments on the
basis of the assessed values of the benefited properties , as au-
thorized by this chapter .
(7 ) The term "front-foot basis " refers to the plan for fi-
nancing improvements by apportioning the cost among benefited
properties upon the basis of the number of linear feet thereof
abutting upon the improvement project , as otherwise provided
by law .
(8 ) The terms "property to be benefited ", "properties to
be benefited ", "benefited property " and "benefited properties "
allmean and refer
tothe property
orproperties defined
inKRS
107.140
.

Section 7. Declaration of emergency . It is hereby found
and declared that numerous municipalities are in critical need
of sewer facilities and sewage treatment plants which they
are having difficulty in financing under existing statutory
authority , and it is necessary and desirable in the interest of
the public health , safety and general welfare , that the authority
of this Act be made available to them during the entire period of
favorable construction weather in 1960 ; accordingly an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon
approval by the Governor .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 227
(S. B. 282 )
AN ACT relating to contracts for the sale of cemetery merchandise .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Whenever any person, firm, corporation , or
other business organization engaged in the business of owning ,
maintaining , or operating a cemetery ( hereinafter referred to as
"cemetery" ) , except a religious organization , shall accept money
in connection with a contract for the sale of cemetery merchandise
and other personal property such as may be used in connection
with disposing or commemorating the memory of a deceased
human being , delivery of which may be longer than ninety days
after the receipt of final payment on such sale , the cemetery shall

1030 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
deposit in a trust fund , established for that purpose with a bank
or trust company authorized by law to administer trust funds
( hereinafter referred to as "trustee" ) , an amount not less than
the procurement cost to the cemetery of the cemetery merchan-
dise and personal property so sold. The procurement cost shall
be determined and substantiated by a wholesale price list of a
manufacturer or supplier of the cemetery merchandise and per-
sonal property so sold or by an itemization of the cost of manu-
facture of the cemetery merchandise and personal property
manufactured and sold by the cemetery which cost determina-
tion , in either case , shall be verified by a certified public ac-
countant . The deposit in the trust fund shall be made not later
than forty - five days after the close of the month in which the final
payment on the purchase price was received .
Section 2. To insure the adequacy of the trust funds for
their intended purpose , the net income therefrom shall remain
therein and be reinvested and compounded , except that annually ,
as of December 31 , upon certification to the trustee by the ceme-
tery , in the manner prescribed in Section 1 , the trustee shall re-
turn to the cemetery any income of the trust fund, which when
added to the original principal and accumulated income is in
excess of the then requirements for the purchase of the cemetery
merchandise and other personal property . Except as provided
in Section 3 , none of the original principal shall be returned to the
cemetery even though such principal at the end of any annual
period , on December 31 , is in excess of the then requirements to
fulfill any particular contract or portion thereof .
Section 3. Except as provided in Section 2, the funds held
in trust shall remain intact until detailed certification has been
made by the cemetery to the trustee of the delivery of item or
items under a contract , at which time funds to cover the then
cost of such items shall be delivered to the cemetery. If, after
payment to the cemetery for items delivered pursuant to con-
tract , the contract is completed but there remain funds with
the trustee or there are still funds allocated to the particular item
which has been delivered , in either event, such excess funds shall
be delivered to the cemetery . All funds still in the trustee's hands
seventy - five years after their receipt shall be then returned to the
cemetery .

CHAPTER 227 1031
Section 4. Any monies returned to the cemetery by the
trustee , either pursuant to Section 2 or Section 3, except as such
may be used to reimburse the cemetery for items delivered under
contract , must be used by the cemetery only for the preservation ,
care , maintenance and improvement of the cemetery property
and
itsoperations
.

CHAPTER 229
(Н. В. 37)
AN ACT relating to assessment dates for city taxation .

of Kentucky :

amended to read as follows :
Property subject to city taxation shall be assessed for city

legislative body .
(3) In cities of the fourth class , January 1 .
(4) In cities of the fifth class, January 1 .
(5) In cities of the sixth class , January 1 .

CHAPTER 231

city.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 96.200 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :

legislative body of any city of the third through sixth classes in-
clusive may , by ordinance , provide in what manner and for what
purpose any profits , earnings or surplus funds arising from the

CHAPTER 233
(Н. В. 267)
AN ACT relating to beauty culture , including , but not exclusively , bar-
bering , cosmetology, hairdressing , and the grooming of the upper ex-
tremities of the human body for cosmetic purposes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act, unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) A "barber " is any person who engages in the practice
of "barbering " for the public generally or for consideration .
( 2 ) "Barbering " is the practice upon the human neck and
head, principally of shaving or trimming the beard or cutting the
hair but includes also
( a ) giving facial and scalp massage or treatments with
oils , creams , lotions or other preparations , either by hand or any
contrivance ,
(b) singeing , shampooing , pressing , arranging , dressing or
dyeing the hair or applying hair tonics ,
( c) applying to the neck or head cosmetics , lotions ,
powders , oils , clays or other preparations .
( 3 ) A "cosmetologist " is a person who engages in the
practice of " cosmetology " for the public generally or for con-
sideration , regardless of the name under which the practice is
conducted .
(4 ) The practice of "cosmetology " is the practice upon
the human neck and head , principally of the human female , of

CHAPTER 233 1035
cutting hair, permanent waving , hairdressing , marcelling but
may also include :
( a ) manicuring , finger waving ,
(b) giving facial and scalp massage or treatments with oils ,
creams , lotions or other preparations , either by hand or any
contrivance ,
(c) singeing , shampooing , pressing, arranging , dressing
or dyeing the hair or applying hair tonics ,
(d) applying to neck or head cosmetics , lotions , powders ,
oils , clays or other preparations .
( 5) “Barber shop " is any establishment of whatsoever kind
in which the practice of "barbering " is conducted for the gen-
eral public or for consideration .
(6) "Beauty shop " is an establishment of whatsoever kind
in which the practice of "cosmetology " is conducted for the
general public or for consideration .
(7 ) "Manicurist " is a person who engages only in the prac-
tice of cutting , trimming , polishing , coloring , cleansing, or mani-
curing the nails or massaging, cleaning , treating or beautifying
the hands of any human , for which a license is required by this
Act.
(8) "Board " means the Kentucky Board of Barbering or
the Kentucky Board of Hairdressers and Cosmetologists , which-
ever meaning is indicated or required by the context.
(9) "Barber school " or "school of barbering " means an
operation , place or establishment of whatsoever kind or form
in or through which persons are trained or taught the practice
of barbering .
(10) " Cosmetology school " or "school of cosmetology "
means an operation , place or establishment of whatsoever kind
or form in or through which persons are trained or taught the
practice of cosmetology .
( 11 ) “ Barber board " means the Kentucky Board of Bar-
bering .
(12) " Cosmetologist board " means the Kentucky Board of
Hairdressers and Cosmetologists .
Section 2. ( 1 ) No person shall engage in the practice of
"barbering " or " cosmetology " for other than cosmetic purposes

1036 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
nor shall any person engage in barbering or the practice of
cosmetology for the treatment of physical or mental ailments ,
except that the provisions of this Act shall not apply to :
( a ) persons authorized by the law of this state to practice
medicine , chiropody , optometry , dentistry , chiropractic , nursing
or embalming when incidental practices of barbering or cos-
metology are performed by them in the normal course of the
practice of their profession ;
(b ) commissioned medical or surgical personnel of the
United States Army, Navy, Air Force or Marine Hospital Service
performing incidental practices of barbering or cosmetology in
the course of their duties .
(2) Except as provided in subsection ( 1 ) of this section
no person shall engage in the practice of barbering or cos-
metology for the public generally or for consideration without
the appropriate license required by this Act .
(3 ) No person unless duly and properly licensed pursuant
to this Act shall :
( a ) teach barbering or cosmetology ,
(b ) operate a barber shop or beauty shop ,
(c) engage in a barber apprenticeship or cosmetology
apprenticeship ,
(d) act as a manicurist ,
(e) conduct or operate a school for barbers or school for
cosmetologists.
(4) No person shall aid or abet any person in violating the
provisions of this section, nor shall any person engage or employ
for consideration any person for the performance of any practice
licensed by this Act unless the person to perform such practice
holds and displays the appropriate license therefor .
( 5 ) Whenever a person engages in different practices
separately licensed by the provision of this Act, such person shall
procure a separate license for each of the practices so licensed .
Section 3. ( 1 ) There is hereby created an independent
agency of the State government to be known as the Kentucky
Board of Barbering , which shall have complete supervision over
the administration of the provisions of this Act relating to barbers ,
barbering , barbershops , barber schools , the teaching of barbering
and barber apprenticeship .

Section 6. ( 1 ) The barber board shall hold hearings upon
the request of any person directly affected by the board's decision
to refuse to renew a license or to deny or revoke a license and no
license renewal shall be denied nor shall a license be revoked
unless the board in advance shall have given the licensee twenty
days , written notice of the revocation or refusal to renew stating
the grounds therefor .
(2 ) Hearings conducted by the board shall afford reason-
able opportunity for the complainant to be present and repre

CHAPTER 233 1041
sented by counsel , to examine and to cross -examine any witness
and produce any competent and relevant proof though the board
need not follow strict rules of evidence in the conduct of such
hearings .
(3 ) Rulings of the barber board as a result of any hearing
may be appealed to the Franklin Circuit Court by filing with said
court within thirty days from date of entry of the order a certi-
fied copy of the ruling or order of the board . The court shall not
set aside any ruling or order of the barber board if there was any
competent evidence before the board to justify the ruling or
order.
(4) The board is authorized to issue subpoenas for the
attendance of witnesses and the production of such records and
documents as may be reasonably necessary in the conduct of any
hearing .
Section 7. ( 1 ) The barber board may employ such per-
sonnel as may be reasonably necessary to carry out the provisions
of this Act , whose compensation shall be established by the De-
partment of Personnel . The board shall by appropriate order em-
ploy an administrator who shall be charged with responsibility
of administering the provisions of this Act, and the policies of
the board relating to barbering . The administrator may receive
a salary of $7,500 per annum , or such compensation as may be
established by classification of the position by the Department
of Personnel .
(2) The barber board shall prescribe the duties of such
personnel employed by it.

Section 11. (1) To protect the health and safety of the
public or to protect the public against misrepresentation , deceit
or fraud in the practice or teaching of beauty culture , the cos-
metologist board shall prescribe reasonable rules and regula-
tions :
( a) governing the location and housing of beauty shops or
cosmetology schools ;
(b ) governing the quantity and quality of equipment , sup-
plies , materials , records and furnishings required in beauty shops
or cosmetology schools ;
( c ) governing the training and supervision of cosmetology
apprentices ;
(d ) governing the qualifications of teachers of cosmetology ;
( e ) governing the hours and courses of instruction at cos-
metology schools ;
( f ) governing the examinations of applicants for licenses .
( 2 ) Regulations pertaining to health and sanitation shall
be approved by the Kentucky State Board of Health before be-
coming effective .
Section 12. ( 1 ) All fees and charges collected by the
cosmetologist board and the barber board shall be paid into the
General Fund in the State Treasury and credited to a separate
revolving or trust and agency fund account established for
the purpose of administering the provisions of this Act . The cost
and expenses of administering the provisions of this Act including
compensation to members of the boards and
itsofficers and em-

bepaid out
ofthe General Fund

(2 ) All fees and charges collected by the boards shall be
available for the administration of the provisions of this Act , and
for no other purpose .
Section 13. No license shall be renewed or issued by
the barber board to any barber school , nor shall any license be
issued by the cosmetologist board to any cosmetology school
unless such school provides :
(a) as a prerequisite of graduation a prescribed course of
instruction of not less than fifteen hundred hours to be given
within an uninterrupted period with not more than eight hours
nor less than four hours of instruction a day , exclusive of Sundays ;
(b) courses of instruction in histology of the hair, skin ,
nails , muscles and nerves of the face and neck ; elementary chem-
istry with emphasis on sterilization and antiseptics ; disease of
the skin , hair and glands ; massaging and manipulating of the
muscles of the upper body ; cutting , shaving , arranging , dress-
ing, coloring , bleaching and tinting the hair and such other
courses as may be prescribed by regulation of the appropriate
board ;
(c) such facilities , equipment , materials , and qualified
teachers as may be required by rules and regulations of the ap-
propriate board adopted pursuant to this Act , but in no event
shall any school have fewer than one licensed teacher per fifteen
students , or more than two students per chair .
Section 14. ( 1 ) The cosmetologist board may prescribe
reasonable rules and regulations pertaining to the issuance of
a license , upon payment of the prescribed license fee, to any
person holding a comparable license issued by another state
where the laws of such state , in the opinion of the cosmetologist
board , provide comparable professional qualification , health and
safety standards .
( 2 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of
this section , a license to operate or practice in this state may be
refused or denied a holder of a license issued by another state
if such state does not extend to the holder of a Kentucky license
similar or comparable recognition and opportunity .

Section 15. ( 1 ) By agreement of the barber board and
the cosmetologist board persons employed by either may be
designated as agents for both in such duties as may be assigned
such employe in carrying out the provisions of this Act .
( 2) Such equipment , supplies and records as are now held
by the Board of Barbers and Beauty Examiners , which is abol-
ished by this Act , shall be appropriately divided between the
two boards created by this Act according to the nature of such
equipment , supplies and records .
(3 ) The principal offices of both boards created by this
Act shall be located in such place as will enable the board to
have ready access to records and technical assistance of the State
Department of Health .
(4) The State Department of Health shall give coopera-
tion and technical advice and assistance to the boards created
by this Act .
( 5 ) The Governor shall appoint an executive director and
fiscal agent who shall have been a practicing barber or cos-
metogolist for at least five years who shall act as coordinator of
the administration of this Act by the barber board and the cos-
metologist board . The executive coordinator shall serve a term
of four years unless sooner removed by the Governor , and re-
ceive a compensation of $8,500 per annum. The Governor , after
consultation with the boards , shall fix the duties of the executive
coordinator .

Section 19. ( 1 ) Licenses shall not be issued or renewed
and such licenses as are issued shall be revoked upon proper
showing of applicant's or licensee's :
( a) conviction of a felony ,
(b ) gross malpractice or incompetence , or
(c) mental or physical health that would endanger public
health or safety .
( 2) A license may be refused or revoked by the ap-
propriate board for the following reasons :
( a) failure to comply with regulations or rules of the
board,
(b ) false or deceptive advertising ,
( c ) practicing in an unlicensed shop or in a shop knowing
that such shop is not complying with this Act or regulations of
the board promulgated pursuant to this Act ,
(d) immoral or unprofessional conduct ,
(e) teaching in an unlicensed school or in a school know-
ing that such school is not complying with this Act or regulations
of the appropriate board promulgated pursuant to this Act .
Section 20. Upon the day immediately preceding the effec-
tive date of this Act , any person who lawfully held a license in
force and effect entitling such person to engage in the practices
licensed by this Act shall be entitled to the comparable license
required by this Act upon application and the payment of the
prescribed annual license fee therefor .
Section 21. This Act shall become effective June 30, 1960 .
Section 22. Sections 317.010 , 317.020 , 317.030 , 317.040 ,
317.050 , 317.060 , 317.070 , 317.080 , 317.090 , 317.100 , 317.110 ,
317.120 , 317.130 , 317.140 , 317.150 , 317.160 , 317.170 , 317.180 ,
317.190 , 317.200 , 317.210 , 317.220 , 317.230 , 317.240 , 317.250 ,
317.260 , 317.270 , 317.280 , 317.290 , 317.320 , 317.330 , 317.340 ,
317.350 , 317.360 and 317.990 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are hereby repealed ,

CHAPTER 234 1051
Section 23. ( 1 ) Any person who violates any provision
of this Act shall be fined not less than fifty dollars nor more than
five hundred dollars or imprisoned for not less than ten days nor
more than six months , or both such fine and imprisonment .
(2 ) Any person who violates any rule or regulation law-
fully adopted by a board under the authority contained in this
Act shall be fined not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than
two hundred dollars , or imprisoned for not less than ten days
nor more than three months , or both such fine and imprisonment .
Section 24. If any provision of this Act is held invalid or
unconstitutional the remaining provisions shall continue to be in
force unless the remaining parts standing alone are incomplete
and incapable of being administered without the provision de-
clared invalid .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

CHAPTER 246
(Н. В. 445)
AN ACT relating to amphetamine drugs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) "Amphetamine " means any drug consisting in whole
or in part of racemic amphetamine sulphate , dextro amphetamine
sulphate , methentermine , methamphetamine , or any other drug
designated by regulation of the State Board of Health and State
Board of Pharmacy , or any salt of any of these compounds; ex-
cept preparations for use in the nose and unfit for internal use.
( 2 ) "Practitioner " means a person licensed under the pro-
fessional licensing laws of this state to prescribe and administer
medicines and drugs .
( 3 ) "Pharmacist " means a person licensed as a pharmacist
under the pharmacy licensing law of this state .
Section 2. It shall be unlawful for any person to possess ,
manufacture , sell , dispense, prescribe or administer any
amphetamine except as authorized in this Act .
Section 3. A pharmacist may sell, dispense , and furnish
any amphetamine only upon the prescription of a practitioner
and then only if the container of such amphetamine bears a
label showing the name and address of the pharmacy or pharma-
cist ; the name of the person for whom the amphetamine was
prescribed ; the date of filling the prescription ; and if an animal
the species and owner of the same .
Section 4. A practitioner may possess amphetamines in
reasonable amounts , and deliver and dispense same to his
patients in the general course of his practice provided the con-
tainer in which such amphetamines are dispensed bears a label

1064 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
showing the name of the practitioner , the name of the person for
whom prescribed , date , and directions for use . If prescribed
for an animal , the specie and name of the owner shall be shown
on the label .
Section 5. Wholesalers and manufacturers may possess
amphetamines for manufacturing and may sell, and deliver same
at wholesale , accompanied in each case with an invoice or bill
of sale , to the following :
( 1 ) Pharmacists ;
( 2) Practitioners ;
( 3 ) For lawful research, teaching or testing , and not for
resale;
(4) Hospitals and institutions where distribution is directed
by practitioners ;
( 5) Federal , state, and local governments ;
(6 ) Manufacturers and other wholesalers .
Section 6. A person to whom or for whose use any ampheta-
mine has been prescribed , sold , or dispensed , by a practitioner or
pharmacist , and the owner of any animal for which any ampheta-
mine has been prescribed , sold , or dispensed by a practitioner or
pharmacist may lawfully possess it only in the container in which
it was delivered to him by the practitioner or pharmacist .
Section 7. This Act shall be administered jointly by the
State Department of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy ,
and their authorized agents . The State Board of Health and the
State Board of Pharmacy shall make such rules and regulations
as are deemed necessary to carry out the intent and purpose of
thisAct .
Section 8. ( 1 ) It is hereby made the duty of the State
Department of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy , their
officers , agents , inspectors , and representatives , and of all peace
officers within the state, and of all city, county , and Common-
wealth attorneys , and the Attorney General , within their re-
spective jurisdictions , to enforce
allprovisions
ofthis Act and
to
cooperate with
allagencies charged with the enforcement
of
the laws
ofthe United States
,
ofthis state
, and
ofall other
states relating
toamphetamines
,

(1) The term "board " means the State Board of Health .
(2) The term "department " means the State Department
of Health;
(3 ) The term "person" includes individual , partnership ,
corporation , and association .
(4) The term "food " means :
(a) articles used for food or drink for man or other animals ;
(b) chewing gum ; and
(c) articles used for components of any such article .
( 5) The term " drug ” means :
(a) articles recognized in the official United States Phar-
macopoeia , official Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United
States , or official National Formulary , or any supplement to
any of them; and
(b) articles intended for use in the diagnosis , cure , mit-
igation , treatment or prevention of disease in man or other
animals ; and
(c) articles (other than food ) intended to affect the structure
or any function of the body of man or other animals ; and
(d) articles intended for use as a component of any arti-
cle specified in this subsection but does not include devices or
their components , parts , or accessories .
(6) The term "device " (except when used in subsection
(12) of this section and in subsection (10) of Section 18 , sub-
section (6) of Section 4, subsection (3) of Section 7, and sub-
section ( 3 ) of Section 10 of this Act ) means instruments , appa-
ratus and contrivances , including their components , parts and
accessories , intended :
(a) for use in the diagnosis , cure , mitigation , treatment ,
or prevention of disease in man or other animals ; or
(b) to affect the structure or any function of the body
of man or other animals .
(7) The term “ cosmetic ” means :
(a) articles intended to be rubbed , poured , sprinkled , or
sprayed on, introduced into , or otherwise applied to the human
body or any part thereof for cleansing , beautifying , promoting
attractiveness , or altering the appearance ; and

( 8 ) The term "official compendium " means the official
United States Pharmacopoeia , official Homeopathic Pharma-
copoeia of the United States , official National Formulary , or any
supplement to any of them .
( 9) The term "label " means a display of written , printed
or graphic matter upon the immediate container of any article ;

tainer or wrapper , if any there be, of the retail package of such
article , or is easily legible through the outside container or
wrapper.

Section 3. A food shall be deemed to be adulterated :
(1 ) ( a ) If it bears or contains any poisonous or dele-
terious substance which may render it injurious to health ; but in
case the substance is not an added substance such food shall
not be considered adulterated under this subsection if the
quantity of such substance in such food does not ordinarily
render it injurious to health ; or
(b) If it bears or contains any added poisonous or added
deleterious substance which is unsafe within the meaning of
Section 5 of this Act ; or
(c) If it consists in whole or in part of a diseased, con-
taminated , filthy, putrid, or decomposed substance, or if it is
otherwise unfit for food ; or
(d) If it has been produced , prepared , packed , or held
under insanitary conditions whereby it may have become con-
taminated with filth , or whereby it may have been rendered
diseased, unwholesome , or injurious to health ; or
( e) If it is the product of a diseased animal or an animal
which has died otherwise than by slaughter , or that has been fed
upon the uncooked offal from a slaughter house ; or
