(d) If it is a drug and it bears or contains, for purposes of coloring only, a coal-tar color other than one from a batch certified under the authority of the Federal Act.

(2) If it purports to be or is represented as a drug the name of which is recognized in a official compendium, and its strength differs from, or its quality or purity falls below, the standard set forth in such compendium. Such determination as to strength, quality, or purity shall be made in accordance with the tests or methods of assay set forth in such compendium, or in the absence of or inadequacy of such tests or methods of assay, those prescribed under authority of the Federal Act. No drug defined in an official compendium shall be deemed to be adulterated under this subsection because it differs from the standard of strength, quality, or purity therefor set forth in such compendium, if its difference in strength, quality, or purity from such standard is plainly stated on its label. Whenever a drug is recognized in both the United States Pharmacopoeia and the Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United States it shall be subject to the requirements of the United States Pharmacopoeia unless it is labeled and offered for sale as a homeopathic drug, in which case it shall be subject to the provisions of the Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United States and not to those of the United States Pharmacopoeia.

(3) If it is not subject to the provisions of subsection (2) of this section and its strength differs from, or its purity or quality falls below, that which it purports or is represented to possess.

(4) If it is a drug and any substance has been:
    (a) mixed or packed therewith so as to reduce its quality or strength; or
    (b) substituted wholly or in part therefor.

Section 7. A drug or device shall be deemed to be misbranded:
    (1) If its labeling is false or misleading in any particular.
    (2) If in package form unless it bears a label containing:
        (a) the name and place of business of the manufacturer, packer, or distributor; and
        (b) an accurate statement of the quantity of the contents in terms of weight, measure, or numerical count; provided that

1076 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

(b) such adequate warnings against use in those pathological conditions or by children where its use may be dangerous to health, or against unsafe dosage or methods or duration of administration or application, in such manner and form, as are necessary for the protection of users; provided that where any requirement of subsection (a) of this subsection, as applied to any drug or device, is not necessary for the protection of the public health, the board shall promulgate regulations exempting such drug or device from such requirements.

(7) If it purports to be a drug the name of which is recognized in an official compendium, unless it is packaged and labeled as prescribed therein; provided that the method of packing may be modified with a consent of the department. Whenever a drug is recognized in both the United States Pharmacopoeia and the Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United States, it shall be subject to the requirements of the United States Pharmacopoeia with respect to packaging and labeling unless it is labeled and offered for sale as a homeopathic drug, in which case it shall be subject to the provisions of the Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United States, and not to those of the United States Pharmacopoeia.

(8) If it has been found by the department to be a drug liable to deterioration, unless it is packaged in such form and manner, and its label bears a statement of such precautions, as the board shall by regulations require as necessary for the protection of public health. No such regulation shall be established for any drug recognized in an official compendium until the board shall have informed the appropriate body charged with the revision of such compendium of the need for such packaging or labeling requirements and such body shall have failed within a reasonable time to prescribe such requirements.

(9) (a) If it is a drug and its container is so made, formed, or filled as to be misleading; or
(b) If it is an imitation of another drug; or
(c) If it is offered for sale under the name of another drug.

(10) If it is dangerous to health when used in the dosage, or with the frequency or duration prescribed, recommended, or suggested in the labeling thereof.

Section 11. ( 1 ) An advertisement of a food , drug, device ,
or cosmetic shall be deemed to be false if it is false or misleading
in any particular .
(2) For the purpose of this Act the advertisement of a
drug or device representing it to have any effect in albuminuria ,
appendicitis , arteriosclerosis , blood poison, bone disease , Bright's
disease, cancer , carbuncles , cholecytitis , diabetes , diphtheria ,
dropsy, erysipelas , gallstones, heart and vascular diseases , high
blood pressure , mastoiditis , measles , meningitis , mumps , neph-
ritis , otitis media , paralysis , pneumonia , poliomyelitis (infantile
paralysis ) , prostate gland disorders , pyelitis , scarlet fever , sexual
impotence , sinus infection , smallpox , tuberculosis , tumors , ty-
phoid, uremia , venereal disease , shall also be deemed to be false ,
except that no advertisement not in violation of subsection (1 )
of this section shall be deemed to be false under this subsection
if it is disseminated only to practitioners or appears only in the
scientific periodicals of these practitioners , or is disseminated
only for the purpose of public health education by persons not
commercially interested , directly or indirectly , in the sale of such
drugs or devices ; provided that whenever the board determines
that an advance in medical science has made any type of self-
medication safe as to any of the diseases named above , the board
shall by regulation authorize the advertisement of drugs having
curative or therepeutic effect for such disease , subject to such
conditions and restrictions as the board may deem necessary in
the interests of public health ; provided that this subsection shall
not be construed as indicating that self-medication for diseases
other than those named herein is safe or efficacious .
Section 12. ( 1 ) Whenever a duly authorized agent of the
department finds or has probable cause to believe , that any food ,
drug , device , or cosmetic is adulterated , or misbranded within
the meaning of this Act , he shall affix to such article a tag or
other appropriate marking , giving notice that such article is, or
is suspected of being , adulterated or misbranded and has been
detained or quarantined and warning all persons not to remove
or dispose of such article by sale or otherwise until permission
for removal or disposal is given by such agent or the court. It
shall be unlawful for any person to remove or dispose of such
detained or quarantined article by sale or otherwise without such
permission .

Section 18. The following acts and the causing thereof
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky are hereby prohibited :
( 1 ) The manufacture , sale , or delivery , holding or offering
for sale of any food , drug , device , or cosmetic that is adulterated
or misbranded .
( 2 ) The adulteration or misbranding of any food, drug ,
device , or cosmetic .
(3 ) The receipt in commerce of any food , drug, device , or
cosmetic that is adulterated or misbranded , and the delivery or
proferred delivery thereof for pay or otherwise .
(4) The sale, delivery for sale, holding for sale , or offering
for sale of any article in violation of Section 8 of this Act .
(5 ) The dissemination of any false advertisement .
( 6) The refusal to permit entry or inspection , or to permit
the taking of a sample or to permit access to records or evidence ,
as authorized by Section 16 of this Act .
(7) The giving of a guaranty or undertaking which guar-
anty or undertaking is false, except by a person who relied on a
guaranty or undertaking to the same effect signed by, and con-
taining the name and address of , the person residing in the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky from whom he received in good faith the
food , drug , device , or cosmetic.
(8 ) The removal or disposal of a detained or quarantined
article in violation of Section 12 of this Act .
( 9) The alteration , mutilation , destruction , obliteration , or
removal of the whole or any part of the labeling of, or the doing
of any other act with respect to, a food , drug , device , or cosmetic ,
if such act is done while such article is held for sale and results
in such article being adulterated or misbranded .
( 10) Forging, counterfeiting , simulating , or falsely repre-
senting , or without proper authority using any mark , stamp ,
tag, label , or other identification device authorized or required by
regulations promulgated under the provisions of this Act .
( 11) The using , on the labeling of any drug or in any ad-
vertisement relating to such drug , of any representation or sug

CHAPTER 247 1085
gestion that any application with respect to such drug is effective
under Section 8 of this Act , or that such drug complies with the
provisions of such section .
( 12 ) The manufacture , sale or exchange of any filled milk.
( 13) The manufacture , mixing , compounding , selling , or
offering for sale of any flour unless the same is enriched ; provided
that this prohibition shall not apply to flour sold to bakeries or
other commercial secondary processors , which flour is used only
in the production of enriched flour or enriched bread or in the
manufacture of products not required to be enriched .
(14) The manufacture , baking , sale , or offering for sale
of any bread except bread conforming to the definition of en-
riched bread .

Section 23. ( 1 ) Any person who violates any of the pro-
visions of Section 18 of this Act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor
and shall on conviction thereof be subject to a fine of not less
than one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars , or
by imprisonment for not more than thirty days, or both; but if
the violation is committed after a conviction of such person under
this section has become final , such person shall be subject to a
fine of not less than five hundred dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars , or by imprisonment for not more than ninety
days , or both .
(2) No person shall be subject to the penalties of sub-
section (1 ) of this section for having violated Section 18 (1 ) or
( 3 ) if he establishes a guaranty or undertaking signed by, and
containing the name and address of, the person residing in the
United States from whom he received in good faith the article , to
the effect that such article is not adulterated or misbranded
within the meaning of this Act, designating this Act .
(3) No publisher , radio broadcast licensee , or agency or
medium for the dissemination of an advertisement , except the
manufacturer , packer , distributor , or seller of the article to
which a false advertisement relates, shall be liable under this
section by reason of the dissemination by him of such false ad-
vertisement , unless he has refused , on the request of the depart-
ment, to furnish the department the name and post office address
of the manufacturer , packer , distributor , seller , or advertising
agency, residing in the United States who causes him to dissemi-
nate such advertisement .
(4) Any person who violates any provision of this Act, for
which a specific penalty is not otherwise provided , or any rule
or regulation adopted under the provisions of this Act, or who
fails to comply with an order of the department issued pursuant
thereto , shall be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than five hundred dollars . Each day of violation or non-compli-
ance shall constitute a separate offense .

CHAPTER 248
(Н. В. 450)
AN ACT providing for the creation of an area planning commission in
urbanized centers , prescribing its duties and powers , its method of
creation and continued existence as a body politic , with power to
levy a tax and to regulate and control the use of land , public im-
provements and utilities within its boundaries , providing an appeal,
and providing penalties for violation of certain of the provisions
thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. In any two or more adjacent counties , one of
which has a city having a population of more than 50,000 and
not more than 200,000 inhabitants as declared by the last Federal
Census , the various cities and the counties may consolidate their
planning operations by establishing an Area Planning Commission
which may be created under the provisions of this Act , when in
the judgment of the legislative bodies of said cities and counties
a consolidation provides for a more efficient planning operation .
Section 2. If the fiscal courts of any two or more adjacent
counties elect to be consolidated as provided in Section 1 hereof ,
and the respective legislative bodies of cities representing more
than two -thirds of the population of the residents living within
the boundaries of corporate territories of each county , by ordi-
nance or resolution , elect to consolidate as provided in Section 1
hereof , then the cities and counties so affected shall authorize
the execution of a contract between themselves agreeing to par-
ticipate in the creation of an Area Planning Commission and
agreeing to be governed by the provisions of this Act . When a
sufficient number of municipalities and counties have executed
said agreement , copies shall be filed in the office of the clerk of
each of the counties affected . Thereupon an Area Planning Com-
mission is established .

1088 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 3. ( 1 ) The Area Planning Commission as created
under the provisions of this Act shall be composed of not more
than nine members who shall be selected from governmental
units participating in the existence of the Area Planning Commis-
sion by the affirmative action of the Area Council hereinafter
provided for .
(2 ) Of the initial membership five members shall be
elected for a term of two years , and four members for a term of
one year each , and upon the expiration of their respective terms
the successors of each shall be elected for a term of two years .

Section 2. Section 382.340 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Mortgages executed by corporations , organized under
the laws of Kentucky , authorized to operate railroads , motor
transportation lines, electric light , heat and power plants , water
plants , telegraph and telephone lines, which cover right of ways ,
franchises , pipe lines , pole lines , easements , railroad tracks ,
engines, cars , motor equipment or any tangible personal property
shall be duly acknowledged as required by law and recorded as
mortgages on real estate are required to be recorded in the county

( 2 ) If the mortgage covers other real property in addition
to right of ways , franchises, pipe lines, pole lines , easements , rail-
road tracks , engines , cars , motor equipment or tangible personal

in which such other real property used as right of ways other

provided , it shall be effective against purchasers for a valuable
consideration without notice and against creditors as defined in
KRS 382.270 .
Section 3. Section 382.635 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
If any tobacco crop subject to the lien of a security interest
is sold at public auction through a duly licensed tobacco ware-
house in the ordinary course of business, a bona fide purchaser
for value of such crop shall take title thereto free and clear of
any such lien , and the warehouseman selling such tobacco crop
shall not be liable to the holder of such lien , unless written notice
of such lien and the amount thereof , the name and address of the
debtor and proper identification of the property subject to lien ,
are given to the tobacco warehouseman prior to the payment of
the proceeds of sale to the owner or producer of such tobacco
crop.
Section 4. Section 7-204 of the Uniform Commercial Code
is amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 251 1095
( 1 ) A warehouseman is liable for damages for loss
of or injury to the goods caused by his failure to exercise such
care in regard to them as a reasonably careful man would exer-
cise under like circumstances but unless otherwise agreed he is
not liable for damages which could not have been avoided by the
exercise of such care .
(2) Damages may be limited by a term in the warehouse
receipt or storage agreement limiting the amount of liability
in case of loss or damage , and setting forth a specific liability
per article or item, or value per unit of weight , beyond which the
warehouseman shall not be liable ; provided , however , that such
liability may on written request of the bailor at the time of sign-
ing such storage agreement or within a reasonable time after re-
ceipt of the warehouse receipt be increased on part or all of the
goods thereunder , in which event increased rates may be charged
based on such increased valuation , but that no such increase
shall be permitted contrary to a lawful limitation of liability con-
tained in the warehouseman's tariff, if any. No such limitation
is effective with respect to the warehouseman's liability for con-
version to his own use .
( 3 ) Reasonable provisions as to the time and manner of
presenting claims and instituting actions based on the bailment
may be included in the warehouse receipt or tariff.
Section 5. Sections 382.390 , 382.400 , 382.410 , 382.420 ,
382.600 , 382.610 , 382.620 , 382.630 , 382.640 , 382.650 , 382.660 and
382.730 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed.
Section 6. This Act shall take effect on July 1 , 1960 .

of Kentucky :

to read as follows :

1096 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

CHAPTER 252
(Н. В. 481)
AN ACT relating to public improvements in cities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of Chapter 93 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
A city of the first class may provide for the making and
financing of any public improvement as provided in KRS 94.292
to 94.325 .
Section 2. Section 94.291 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
KRS 94.291 to 94.325 provide for the making and financing
of public improvements in cities of all classes . However , these
sections are not intended to affect the provisions of KRS 94.160
and 94.170 , providing an alternate plan for the acquisition and
operation of sewerage systems , nor are they intended to affect
the provisions of KRS 94.500 to 94.540 , relating to the oiling of
public ways , or the provisions of KRS 177.041 to 177.047 .

CHAPTER 253
(Н. В. 485)
AN ACT relating to the Executive Mansion and the Old Governor's
Mansion .
WHEREAS , both the Senate and the House of Representatives have
expressed concern over the condition and furnishings of the Execu-
tive Mansion and appointed committees to inspect the mansion and

CHAPTER 254 1097
recognizing the need for providing continuous attention to the ap-
pearance and utility of the official residence of the chief executive
of the Commonwealth ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. That the Chief Justice of the Court of Appeals ,
with the concurrence of the President Pro Tem of the Senate and
the Speaker of the House of Representatives , appoint a committee
of five prominent citizens to provide continuing attention to the
maintenance , furnishings and repairs of the Executive Mansion .
Section 2. That the committee so appointed include the
first vice president of the Kentucky Historical Society and the
remaining four members be residents of Franklin county .
Section 3. That the committee herein established examine
the Executive Mansion at least once each year and that the com-
mittee supervise the maintenance of a current inventory of all
furnishings in the Executive Mansion , such inventory to be main-
tained by the Department of Finance .
Section 4. That the committee recommend , from time to
time, on the need for furnishings , maintenance or repair of the
Executive Mansion and that the Department of Finance shall ,
from funds available , take such action as is recommended .
Section 5. That the committee provide coordination and
make arrangements for an orderly transition between out -going
and in-coming chief executives .
Section 6. That the committee perform the same functions
required in relation to the Executive Mansion for the Old Gov-
ernor's Mansion insofar as they are applicable .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960

1104 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 2. A new section is created in Chapter 76 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes to follow KRS 76.265 to read as
follows :
(1) At such time as bids have been received for the work
to be performed on such improvement , the secretary -treasurer
of the district shall notify each property owner by mail as to the
expected cost of the improvement , based upon the lowest bid
received from a qualified bidder , and upon the other known costs
included under KRS 76.250 , and as to the total acreage in each
class .
( 2) Any person or persons whose property is subject to the
assessment shall have fifteen days from the mailing of such
notices within which to file in the county court of the county in
which the metropolitan sewer district is located , an objection or
objections to the cost of the project .
( 3 ) The court shall hear in a summary way any objections
to the cost of the improvement and to the assessment of the land
for the work to be done under said contract and enter an order
confirming the assessment roll or directing the board of the
metropolitan sewer district to change it in accordance with the
findings of the court . No question or objection that could have
been raised with reference to the establishment of the drainage
district or the classification of land can be raised by exceptions
to the assessment roll or statement of costs . The order of the
court shall be final and may be appealed from in the same man-
ner as from orders approving the organization of the district or
the classification of land .
Section 3. Section 76.270 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Upon completion of construction , the cost of any
drainage improvement shall be apportioned to the land in the
designated area upon the basis of the benefits for real estate in
each of said classes on an area basis in each classification . Areas
shall be determined to the nearest quarter acre , with the mini-
mum assessment for a quarter acre . The apportionment for each
• property owner shall be computed by the metropolitan sewer
district.

CHAPTER 255 1105
( 2 ) The secretary-treasurer of a metropolitan sewer dis-
trict shall notify each property owner by mail as to the amount
of the assessment against him for any improvement constructed
or on which a bid or bids for construction have been let under
the provisions of KRS 76.240 to 76.280. Any property owner
may pay to the metropolitan sewer district his assessment in
full at any time within thirty days from the date of mailing
of notice of the assessment . Every person who fails to pay the
full amount of his assessment to such metropolitan sewer dis-
trict within thirty days shall be deemed to consent to the issuance
of apportionment warrants against him and his property , unless
the total assessment against such property is less than $50.00 .
Each property owner must pay , within thirty days from date
of mailing of notice , the full amount of his assessment if it does
not exceed $50.00 , and at least $50.00 on any assessment that
exceeds that sum. Each property owner who fails to pay the
full amount of the assessment within thirty days shall be deemed
to consent to the issuance of apportionment warrants against
his property and him , for the amount due , after deducting the
$50.00 due then and any additional sum paid within the thirty
days .
If an assessment of $50.00 or less , or the first $50.00 of any
larger assessment , against any property within the designated
area , remains unpaid at the end of thirty days from the date of
the notice , it shall be certified on a statement prepared by the
secretary -treasurer of the metropolitan sewer district making the
assessment to the sheriff of the county to be collected by him
with the annual bill for county taxes , together with interest at
sixper cent per annum from the thirty

Any particular tract or tracts of land may be reclassified

Not more than one assessment shall be made in any one fiscal

1108 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 6. A new section is created in Chapter 76 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes to read as follows :
After a drainage improvement has been authorized for a
designated area and an assessment made , further proceedings
may be taken for additional improvements within any section
of the designated area through the same procedure as specified
in KRS 76.240 to 76.280 . In order to institute such additional
improvement within the designated area it must appear that the
original improvement project did not relieve sufficiently the
drainage conditions within such section. The additional benefit
accruing by reason of such improvement in a section of the
previous project shall not be a ground for reclassification of
benefits for maintenance under the original project .
Became law without Governor's signature , March 31 , 1960
CHAPTER 256
(Н. В. 511)
AN ACT relating to commissioners and receivers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 64.270 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1 ) In counties containing a city of the first class, com-
missioners and receivers appointed by the circuit court shall
receive an annual salary of $7,200 , payable out of the fees of the
office . In counties containing a population of 75,000 or over , but
not containing a city of the first class, commissioners and re-
ceivers appointed by the circuit court shall receive an annual
salary of $ 7,200 payable out of the fees of the office .
( 2) The amount , if any , allowed for necessary office ex-
penses of said commissioners and receivers shall be fixed and
regulated by an order entered upon the order book of the circuit
court . A certified copy of the order and any subsequent changes
made therein shall , as soon as entered, be forwarded to the De-
partment of Finance .
(3 ) Each of said commissioners and receivers shall enter
and keep in books in his office an account of the amounts earned

by him or due him for official services rendered , when and to
whom the service was rendered , what part has been paid, and

, and

CHAPTER 300
By H. B. 8917

An act to fix the salaries and receipts.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:

Section 1. 270 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows:

(1) In counties containing a city of the first class, commissioners and receivers appointed by the circuit court shall receive an annual salary of $7,200, payable out of the fees of the office. In counties containing a population of 75,000 or over, but not containing a city of the first class, commissioners and receivers appointed by the circuit court shall receive an annual salary of $7,200 payable out of the fees of the office.

(2) The amount, if any, allowed for necessary office expenses of said commissioners and receivers shall be fixed and regulated by an order entered upon the order book of the circuit court. A certified copy of the order and any subsequent changes made therein shall, as soon as entered, be forwarded to the Department of Finance.

(3) Each of said commissioners and receivers shall cause to keep in books in his office an account of the amounts received.
